vocabWords.definition_7_10: When something improves from how it was before, becoming more pleasant, comfortable, or helpful than it used to be.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: I felt better after taking medicine for my headache.

pages.label: Page 8
pages.text: Bear poured two cups of cocoa. The cocoa was warm and sweet.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Bear pouring steaming cocoa from a pot into two colorful mugs on a wooden table. Steam curls up from both cups, and marshmallows float on top.

vocabWords.definition_2_4: Made liquid flow from one place to another.
vocabWords.definition_4_7: When you tip a container to make liquid flow out.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: When you tilt a container so that liquid inside flows out in a steady stream into another container or space.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: Mom poured milk into my cereal bowl at breakfast.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Small containers used for drinking liquids like water or juice.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Small round containers with handles that are designed for holding and drinking hot or cold beverages like coffee or tea.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: We put our cups in the dishwasher after dinner.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: A taste that is like sugar, honey, or candy.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: A pleasant taste that reminds you of sugar, fruit, or desserts and makes your mouth feel happy and satisfied.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The ripe strawberries tasted very sweet and juicy.

pages.label: Page 9
pages.text: Fox and Bear sat by the fire. They drank their cocoa together.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Fox and Bear sitting side by side in comfortable chairs by the fireplace, each holding a steaming mug of cocoa. Both look happy and cozy.

vocabWords.definition_2_4: Hot flames that give warmth and light.
vocabWords.definition_4_7: Bright hot flames that burn wood and make heat.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: A burning flame that gives off heat and light, often made by burning wood, gas, or other materials safely.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: We roasted marshmallows over the campfire under the stars.

vocabWords.definition_2_4: Put liquid in your mouth and swallowed it.
vocabWords.definition_4_7: When you swallow liquid like water, juice, or milk.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: The past action of taking liquid into your mouth and swallowing it to quench thirst or enjoy the taste.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: After playing soccer, the children drank water to cool down.

vocabWords.definition_2_4: At the same time, with someone else.
vocabWords.definition_4_7: When people do something at the same time with each other.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: When two or more people share an activity, place, or moment at the same time, creating companionship and connection.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The family played board games together on Sunday afternoon.

pages.label: Page 10
pages.text: "Thank you, Bear," said Fox. "You are a very kind friend."
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Fox and Bear smiling at each other, both holding their cocoa mugs. Fox looks warm and happy now, with dry fluffy fur. The cabin glows with warm light around them.

vocabWords.definition_2_4: A nice word when someone helps you.
vocabWords.definition_4_7: What you say when someone does something nice for you.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: A polite expression of gratitude you give to someone who has helped you, given you something, or been kind to you.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: I always thank the bus driver when I get off the school bus.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Someone who is gentle, helpful, and cares about others.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: A person who shows gentleness, generosity, and consideration for others' feelings and needs without expecting anything back.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The kind neighbor brought soup when our family was sick.

vocabWords.definition_2_4: Someone you like and who likes you.
vocabWords.definition_4_7: A person you enjoy spending time with and who cares about you.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Someone who enjoys your company, supports you through good and bad times, and chooses to share experiences and trust with you.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: My best friend and I love to ride bikes around the neighborhood.

fr2.title: The Sharing Tree
fr2.obstacle: a magical tree that only gives apples when you share them

pages.label: Page 1
pages.text: Maya discovered a wonderful tree in her grandmother's garden. Its branches stretched wide like welcoming arms, covered in the most beautiful red apples she had ever seen. The sweet smell of ripe fruit filled the warm afternoon air.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: A young girl with curly hair standing in a sunny garden, looking up at a magnificent apple tree with bright red apples and lush green leaves

vocabWords.definition_4_7: To find something new or special for the first time.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: To find or learn about something wonderful that you didn't know about before.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: Emma discovered a beautiful seashell buried in the sand at the beach.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: The long wooden arms that grow out from a tree trunk.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: The woody parts of a tree that spread out like arms to hold leaves and fruit.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The bird built its nest on the sturdy branches of the oak tree.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: To reach out as far as you can go.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: To extend your body or arms as far as possible to reach something.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The cat stretched its legs after waking up from a long nap.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Being friendly and making someone feel happy to be there.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Making someone feel comfortable and wanted, like they belong in that special place.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The teacher had a welcoming smile when the new student entered the classroom.

pages.label: Page 2
pages.text: Maya reached up on her tiptoes, stretching as high as she could. But no matter how hard she tried, the apples stayed firmly on their branches. "That's strange," she whispered, scratching her head in confusion.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Maya stretching up toward the apple tree with her arms raised, looking puzzled as the apples remain out of reach despite being low enough to touch

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Standing on the very tips of your feet to be taller.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Balancing on the front part of your feet to reach up higher than usual.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: Sarah stood on her tiptoes to see over the tall fence.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Staying in place very strongly and not moving at all.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Staying attached or in position with great strength, refusing to budge or fall.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: Dad held the ladder firmly so it wouldn't slip while Mom climbed up.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: The feeling when you don't understand what's happening around you.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: The puzzled feeling you get when something doesn't make sense or work as expected.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: There was confusion in the classroom when the fire drill started unexpectedly.

pages.label: Page 3
pages.text: Just then, her grandmother appeared with a knowing smile. "This is the Sharing Tree, dear Maya," she explained gently. "It only gives its apples to those who promise to share them with others."
pages.scene: illustration prompt: A kind elderly woman with silver hair and a warm smile standing next to Maya in the garden, gesturing toward the apple tree

vocabWords.definition_4_7: To show up or come into sight when you weren't there before.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: To suddenly become visible or arrive somewhere, often when you weren't expected.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: A rainbow appeared in the sky right after the rainstorm ended.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: To tell someone about something so they can understand it better.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: To share information in a clear way that helps someone understand how something works.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The coach explained the rules of the game to all the new players.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: When you say you will definitely do something important.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: A special agreement you make when you give your word to do something meaningful.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: Jake made a promise to walk his neighbor's dog every morning.

pages.label: Page 4
pages.text: Maya thought about this carefully. She was very hungry and the apples looked deliciously juicy. But she remembered her friends at school who sometimes forgot their snacks. A warm feeling grew in her heart.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Close-up of Maya with a thoughtful expression, her hand on her chin, with thought bubbles showing her school friends

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Thinking about something slowly and with lots of attention.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Taking time to think deeply about something important before making a decision.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: Mom carefully planned the surprise party so everything would be perfect.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Tasting so good that it makes you very happy.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Having such a wonderful taste that it brings you pure joy and satisfaction.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The chocolate cake was deliciously sweet and made everyone smile.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: To think about something that happened before right now.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: To bring back into your mind something from the past that's important to you.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: Grandpa remembered the funny story from when he was a little boy.

pages.label: Page 5
pages.text: "I promise to share," Maya said softly to the tree. Suddenly, the branches swayed gently, and three perfect apples dropped right into her waiting hands. They felt smooth and warm from the sunshine.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Maya with her hands cupped as three bright red apples fall gently from the tree branches into her palms, with golden sunlight filtering through the leaves

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Speaking in a very quiet and gentle voice.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Using a tender, quiet voice that sounds kind and peaceful to others.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The mother sang softly to help her baby fall asleep.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: To move gently back and forth like dancing.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: To rock or move smoothly from side to side in a graceful way.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The tall grass swayed in the gentle breeze near the pond.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: The bright warm light that comes from the sun.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: The golden rays of light that make everything warm and bright during the day.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The flowers opened up when the morning sunshine touched their petals.

pages.label: Page 6
pages.text: The next day at school, Maya packed the apples in her lunch box. During snack time, she noticed her friend Ben looking sad. "I forgot my snack again," he sighed, watching everyone else eat.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: A school cafeteria scene with Maya sitting at a table with her lunchbox open, looking at a sad boy named Ben who has no snack

vocabWords.definition_4_7: To put things carefully into a bag or box.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: To organize and place items neatly inside something to carry them somewhere else.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: We packed our suitcases with clothes for the family vacation.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: To see something and pay attention to what's happening.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: To become aware of something by looking carefully and thinking about what you see.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: I noticed that my dog was limping after our long walk.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: To breathe out slowly when you feel sad or tired.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: To let out a long, deep breath that shows you're feeling disappointed or weary.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: Tommy sighed when he saw all the homework he had to finish.

pages.label: Page 7
pages.text: Maya smiled and offered Ben one of her special apples. His face lit up with joy as he bit into the crispy, sweet fruit. "This is the best apple I've ever tasted!" he exclaimed happily.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Maya handing a red apple to Ben, whose face is bright with happiness and surprise as he takes the apple

vocabWords.definition_4_7: To give something nice to someone because you want to help.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: To kindly give or share something with another person, especially when they need it.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The librarian offered to help me find books about dinosaurs.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: To say something in a loud and excited voice.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: To speak with great enthusiasm and energy, showing strong feelings about something wonderful.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: Anna exclaimed with joy when she saw her best friend at the party.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Hard and crunchy when you bite into it.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Having a satisfying crunch that makes a pleasant sound when you take a bite.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The fresh crackers were crispy and made a loud crunch.

pages.label: Page 8
pages.text: At recess, Maya saw Emma sitting alone on a bench. Emma had scraped her knee and looked ready to cry. Maya gently offered her second apple, and soon Emma was smiling through her tears.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Maya sitting on a playground bench next to Emma, who has a small bandage on her knee, offering her an apple with a caring expression

vocabWords.definition_4_7: The fun time at school when kids play outside.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: A special break during the school day when children go outside to play and have fun.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: During recess, we played tag on the playground with our friends.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: To hurt your skin by rubbing it against something rough.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: To injure the surface of your skin by sliding or falling against something hard or bumpy.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: I scraped my elbow when I fell off my bicycle yesterday.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Doing something in a soft and careful way.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Acting with tenderness and care, being very careful not to cause any harm.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: She gently petted the scared kitten until it felt safe.

pages.label: Page 9
pages.text: That afternoon, Maya returned to the Sharing Tree with her last apple. She sat beneath its shady branches and took a bite. It was the sweetest, most wonderful apple she had ever tasted in her whole life.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Maya sitting contentedly under the apple tree's shade, taking a bite of her apple with a peaceful, happy expression

vocabWords.definition_4_7: In the space under something, like sitting under a tree.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: In the protected area below something, often feeling safe and covered from above.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The rabbit hid beneath the big rock during the thunderstorm.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: A cool place where the sun doesn't shine directly.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: A comfortable spot that's protected from bright sunlight by trees or other things above.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: We had our picnic in the shady area under the big oak tree.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Something so good that it makes you feel amazing inside.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Something absolutely beautiful and special that fills your heart with happiness and joy.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The circus show was wonderful and made all the children laugh with delight.

pages.label: Page 10
pages.text: From that day on, Maya visited the Sharing Tree whenever someone needed kindness. The tree always provided enough apples to share, and Maya's heart grew as full and warm as a summer day.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Maya standing under the flourishing apple tree surrounded by several happy friends, all sharing apples together in the sunny garden

vocabWords.definition_4_7: To go see someone or something in a special place.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: To spend time with someone or at a place that's important and meaningful to you.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: We visited our cousins at their farm and saw all the animals.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Every time that something happens or is needed.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: At any moment when a particular thing occurs or when someone needs help.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: Mom always gives me a hug whenever I feel scared or worried.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Being nice and caring about other people's feelings.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: The warm and loving way you treat others when you want to help make them happy.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The teacher's kindness made the new student feel welcome and loved.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: To give someone exactly what they need when they need it.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: To supply or offer something helpful at just the right time when it's needed most.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The school provided free lunch for all students during the special event.

comprehensionQuestions.question: What did Maya have to promise before the tree would give her apples?

options.text: To share the apples with others
To water the tree every day
To climb the tree carefully

comprehensionQuestions.question: Why do you think the tree was called the Sharing Tree?

options.text: It only gave apples to people who promised to share
It had the biggest apples in the garden
It grew next to other fruit trees

comprehensionQuestions.question: How do you feel when you share something special with a friend?

options.text: Happy and warm inside
Excited to help someone
Good about being kind

predictionPause.question: Maya promised to share and got three apples from the magical tree. What do you think she'll do with them?

options.text: Share them with friends who need snacks
Save them for a special family dinner
Plant the seeds to grow more sharing trees

fr3.title: The Quiet Friend
fr3.obstacle: nobody at school talks to him because he's too quiet

pages.label: Page 1
pages.text: Eli sat at the back corner of his classroom, watching raindrops race down the tall windows. His desk was perfectly organized with sharp pencils lined up like tiny soldiers and his notebook opened to a fresh, clean page. While other children chattered excitedly about their weekend adventures, Eli quietly traced patterns in the condensation on the glass. He had wonderful stories in his head about brave knights and friendly dragons, but the words always seemed to get stuck in his throat when he tried to share them.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: A quiet boy with kind eyes sitting alone at a desk by rain-streaked classroom windows, watching raindrops while other children talk in the background

vocabWords.definition_4_7: When things are put in neat, tidy places.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Having everything arranged in a careful, orderly way where each item has its proper place.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: Sarah organized her art supplies in labeled boxes on the shelf.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Talked quickly and excitedly about many different things.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Spoke rapidly and enthusiastically, like birds chirping together in animated conversation.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The children chattered about their favorite movies during the bus ride.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Water drops that form on cold glass or windows.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Tiny water droplets that appear on cool surfaces when warm, moist air touches them.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: Morning condensation covered the car windshield with little water beads.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Exciting trips or experiences that are fun and different.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Thrilling journeys or experiences filled with discovery, excitement, and unexpected moments.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The family went on camping adventures in the mountains every summer.

pages.label: Page 2
pages.text: At lunch, Eli unwrapped his peanut butter and strawberry jam sandwich and found his usual spot under the big oak tree. The rough bark felt comforting against his back as he watched groups of classmates playing tag and sharing jokes. Sometimes a soccer ball would roll near his feet, and he would gently kick it back with a small smile. But no one ever asked him to join their games, and he wasn't sure how to ask if he could play too.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Eli sitting alone under a large oak tree eating lunch, watching other children play in the sunny schoolyard in the distance

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Took the paper or covering off of something.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Carefully removed the wrapping or outer covering to reveal what was hidden inside.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: Mom unwrapped the birthday present to show us the surprise inside.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Making someone feel safe, calm, and better.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Providing a warm, soothing feeling that makes worries fade away and brings peace.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The soft blanket felt comforting when Emma was feeling sad.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Other kids who are in the same class at school.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Fellow students who share the same classroom and learning experiences throughout the school year.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: Jake invited his classmates to his birthday party at the park.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Doing something in a soft, careful, kind way.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Moving or acting with tender care and delicate touch, like handling something precious.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The veterinarian gently examined the injured kitten's paw.

pages.label: Page 3
pages.text: One Tuesday morning, Eli noticed a new girl standing nervously by the classroom door. Her dark curly hair was tied with a bright yellow ribbon, and she clutched a worn teddy bear close to her chest. Mrs. Peterson introduced her as Maya, and Eli could see her hands trembling slightly as she looked around the room full of unfamiliar faces. When Maya was assigned the empty desk next to his, Eli felt his heart beat a little faster with both excitement and worry.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: A nervous girl with curly hair and yellow ribbon holding a teddy bear, standing by the classroom door while other children stare curiously

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Feeling worried or scared about what might happen.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Acting with anxious uncertainty, like butterflies are fluttering around inside your stomach.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: Tom nervously waited for the dentist to call his name.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Held something very tightly in your hands.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Gripped firmly with fingers wrapped around something, refusing to let it slip away.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The little boy clutched his mother's hand during the thunderstorm.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Shaking a little bit because you feel scared or nervous.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Quivering slightly with small, uncontrollable movements caused by strong emotions or excitement.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The puppy was trembling with excitement when it saw the treat.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Something you don't know or haven't seen before.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Strange and unknown, like entering a place where everything feels new and mysterious.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The unfamiliar neighborhood made Lisa feel lost and confused.

pages.label: Page 4
pages.text: Maya sat down quietly and unpacked her supplies with careful movements. Eli noticed she had the same set of colored pencils he did, and her eraser was shaped like a tiny elephant. When she dropped her pencil case and crayons scattered across the floor like colorful confetti, some of the other children giggled. Eli quickly bent down to help gather the wayward crayons, handing them back to Maya one by one without saying a word, but offering her the warmest smile he could manage.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Eli kneeling on the floor helping Maya collect scattered colorful crayons while she looks grateful, with other children watching from their desks

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Took things out of a bag or box.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Carefully removed items from their container and arranged them in their new place.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: After the move, we unpacked our clothes and put them in the closet.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Spread out in different directions all over the place.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Spread randomly across a wide area, like seeds blown by the wind in all directions.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: Autumn leaves scattered across the yard after the windy storm.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Tiny pieces of colored paper thrown at celebrations.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Small colorful paper bits that flutter through the air during parties and special occasions.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: Bright confetti fell from the ceiling when the team won the championship.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Going in the wrong direction or getting lost.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Wandering away from where they should be, like adventurous explorers going their own way.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The wayward puppy kept running toward the busy street instead of the park.

pages.label: Page 5
pages.text: During art time, Eli noticed Maya struggling with her watercolor painting of a rainbow. The colors kept bleeding into each other, creating muddy brown patches where bright stripes should be. Her shoulders sagged with disappointment, and Eli could see tears threatening to spill from her eyes. Without overthinking it, he gently slid his own painting over to show her how he waited for each color to dry before adding the next. Maya looked up at him with grateful eyes and whispered a soft 'thank you.'
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Eli showing Maya his rainbow watercolor painting technique at an art table covered with paints, brushes, and colorful artwork

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Having a hard time doing something difficult.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Working very hard to accomplish something challenging, like climbing a steep mountain.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: Maria was struggling to solve the tricky math problem on her homework.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: A type of paint that mixes with water.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Special paints that blend smoothly with water to create soft, flowing artistic effects.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The artist used watercolor paints to create a beautiful sunset picture.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: When colors run into each other and mix together.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Colors flowing beyond their boundaries and mixing together, creating unintended blended effects.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The red marker was bleeding through the paper onto the desk.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Feeling sad when something doesn't work out right.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: The heavy feeling in your heart when something you hoped for doesn't happen as expected.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: There was disappointment on his face when the game was cancelled.

pages.label: Page 6
pages.text: At recess, Maya sat alone on the wooden bench near the playground, watching the other children on the swings and monkey bars. Eli had been planning to read his library book under his favorite oak tree, but something made him walk over to the bench instead. He sat down beside her, leaving a comfortable space between them, and opened his book about a magical forest. The colorful illustrations seemed to catch Maya's attention, and she leaned slightly closer to see the pictures of talking animals and enchanted mushrooms.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Eli and Maya sitting together on a wooden bench, looking at a picture book with magical forest illustrations while playground equipment is visible behind them

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Made from wood that came from trees.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Crafted from natural tree material, with the warm texture and grain of living wood.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The wooden rocking chair creaked gently as grandma sat down.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: A place at school where kids go to play outside.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: An outdoor area filled with swings, slides, and equipment designed for children's active fun.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The new playground had a tall slide and colorful climbing bars.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Pictures drawn in books to help tell the story.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Carefully crafted drawings or artwork that bring words to life and help readers visualize stories.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The fairy tale book had beautiful illustrations of castles and dragons.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Magical and special, like something from a fairy tale.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Filled with magical wonder and mysterious charm, as if touched by fairy tale spells.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The enchanted garden had flowers that glowed in the moonlight.

pages.label: Page 7
pages.text: The next day, Maya brought her own book to share – a collection of stories about brave princesses from around the world. She pointed to the beautiful illustrations and told Eli about her favorite princess who could speak to dolphins and lived in an underwater castle. For the first time in months, Eli felt brave enough to share one of his own stories, quietly describing the dragon in his imagination who collected shiny pebbles instead of treasure. Maya listened with wide, interested eyes and said it sounded like the best dragon ever.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Maya and Eli sitting close together, sharing books and talking animatedly, with Maya's princess book and Eli's fantasy book open between them

vocabWords.definition_4_7: A group of similar things kept together.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: A special gathering of related items that someone has carefully chosen and kept together.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: His rock collection filled three shelves with colorful stones from different places.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Girls who are part of royal families in stories.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Female members of royal families, often brave and kind heroines in fairy tales and legends.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The movie featured princesses from many different countries and time periods.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Smart ocean animals that are friendly to people.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Intelligent marine mammals known for their playful nature and ability to communicate with humans.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The dolphins jumped high out of the water during the boat trip.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Below the surface of water, like at the bottom.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Beneath the water's surface in the mysterious depths where fish and sea creatures live.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The underwater cave was home to colorful fish and coral reefs.

pages.label: Page 8
pages.text: When Maya felt sick one Thursday and had to go home early, Eli carefully copied the math homework assignment in his neatest handwriting. He drew a small smiling sun in the corner of the paper and asked Mrs. Peterson if he could deliver it to Maya's house after school. Walking up to Maya's front door felt scary, but when Maya answered with a surprised smile and invited him in for hot chocolate with marshmallows, Eli knew he had made the right choice.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Eli at Maya's front door holding a paper with neat writing and a small sun drawing, while Maya opens the door with a happy, surprised expression

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Homework or work that a teacher gives students to do.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: A specific task or project that teachers give students to complete and learn from.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The science assignment asked us to grow plants and measure their height.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: The most careful and tidy way of writing or doing something.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Done with the greatest care and precision, creating the most orderly and clean result possible.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: She wrote her name in her neatest handwriting on the birthday card.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: To bring something to someone at their house or place.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: To personally carry and give something to someone at their specific location or address.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The mail truck will deliver packages to our house this afternoon.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Soft, white, squishy candy that's sweet and fluffy.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Pillowy soft white confections that melt on your tongue and float perfectly in hot cocoa.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: We roasted marshmallows over the campfire until they turned golden brown.

pages.label: Page 9
pages.text: Maya's living room smelled like cinnamon and vanilla, and family photos covered the mantle above a cozy fireplace. As they sipped their warm hot chocolate, Maya confessed that she had been worried about making friends at her new school. Eli nodded and admitted that he often felt too shy to talk to other kids, even though he really wanted to. They discovered they both loved drawing, reading adventure stories, and building elaborate castles out of blocks. 'Maybe we don't need to be loud to be good friends,' Maya said thoughtfully.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Eli and Maya sitting cross-legged on a soft rug in a cozy living room, sharing hot chocolate with marshmallows, with family photos and a warm fireplace in the background

vocabWords.definition_4_7: A sweet spice that smells really good and tastes warm.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: A fragrant brown spice that adds warmth and sweetness, making everything smell like home.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: Mom sprinkled cinnamon on the apple pie before baking it.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: A sweet flavor that smells nice and tastes creamy.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: A smooth, sweet scent and flavor that brings comfort like a warm hug in a bottle.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The vanilla candle made the whole room smell like a bakery.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: The shelf above a fireplace where people put decorations.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: The decorative ledge above a fireplace where families display treasured photos and special items.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: Christmas stockings hung from the mantle above the crackling fire.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Very detailed and fancy with lots of special parts.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Incredibly detailed and complex, with many intricate parts carefully planned and beautifully crafted.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The birthday cake had elaborate decorations with flowers and ribbons.

pages.label: Page 10
pages.text: From that day forward, Eli and Maya became inseparable friends who understood each other perfectly. They created a special spot under the oak tree where they would read stories, share drawings, and plan imaginary adventures. Other classmates began to notice their quiet friendship and sometimes joined them for peaceful activities like drawing or looking at interesting books. Eli learned that being a good friend wasn't about talking loudly or being the center of attention – it was about listening carefully, sharing kindly, and always being there when someone needed you.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Eli and Maya under their oak tree surrounded by books and drawings, with a few other children sitting nearby, all engaged in quiet, peaceful activities together

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Always together and never wanting to be apart.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: So closely connected that they cannot be divided, like two pieces of a perfect puzzle.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The twin sisters were inseparable and did everything together every day.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Something you think up in your mind that isn't real.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Created in your imagination and existing only in your mind, like dreams that feel completely real.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The little boy had an imaginary friend who went on adventures with him.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Quiet, calm, and relaxing without any fighting or noise.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Filled with gentle tranquility and harmony, like a still lake reflecting the morning sky.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The peaceful garden was the perfect place to read and think quietly.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: When everyone looks at you and thinks about what you're doing.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: The focused interest and awareness that others give to someone who stands out from the crowd.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The magician captured everyone's attention with his amazing disappearing trick.

comprehensionQuestions.question: What did Eli like to do during lunch time?

options.text: Sit under the big oak tree and eat his sandwich
Play soccer with his classmates on the field
Go to the library to read books quietly

comprehensionQuestions.question: Why do you think Maya felt nervous on her first day?

options.text: She was starting at a new school and didn't know anyone
She forgot to bring her lunch from home
She was worried about a big test that day

comprehensionQuestions.question: How do you feel when you meet someone new who might become a friend?

options.text: Excited but also a little nervous about what to say
Curious about what they like to do for fun
Happy to have someone new to talk with

predictionPause.question: What do you think will happen next between Eli and Maya?

options.text: They will start talking and become friends
Eli will help Maya with more school activities
Maya will invite Eli to play or share something

fr4.title: Puddle Pals
fr4.obstacle: Duck and Pig both want the same puddle to splash in

pages.label: Page 1
pages.text: Duck loved to splash! After the rain, she waddled to find the perfect puddle. Her yellow feet went splish-splash on the wet grass.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: A happy yellow duck with orange feet walking across green grass dotted with small puddles, with gray rain clouds clearing in the background

vocabWords.definition_2_4: When water makes noise and flies around.
vocabWords.definition_4_7: Water that jumps and makes noise when you hit it.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Water that flies through the air and makes sounds when something moves through it quickly.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The boy made a big splash when he jumped in the pool.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Moving side to side while walking like ducks do.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Walking with a swaying motion from side to side, the way ducks and penguins move.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The penguin waddled across the ice to find fish.

vocabWords.definition_2_4: A little pool of water on the ground.
vocabWords.definition_4_7: Water that sits in one spot after rain.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: A small pool of water that forms on the ground after rain or spills.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: I stepped in a puddle and got my shoes wet.

pages.label: Page 2
pages.text: There it was! The biggest, most wonderful puddle ever. It sparkled in the sunshine like a tiny lake. Duck's eyes grew wide with joy.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: A large, shimmering puddle reflecting sunlight, with Duck standing at the edge looking excited and amazed

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Made lots of little shiny lights like stars.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Reflected light in many tiny bright points that seemed to dance and twinkle.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The snow sparkled in the morning sun.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: The bright warm light that comes from the sun.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: The warm, bright light that shines down from the sun on a clear day.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: We played outside in the warm sunshine.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Something that makes you feel very happy and excited.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Something so good and amazing that it fills you with joy and delight.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: We had a wonderful time at the birthday party.

pages.label: Page 3
pages.text: But wait! Pig was running toward the same puddle. His pink snout wiggled with excitement. 'My puddle!' he squealed happily.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: A round pink pig with a curly tail running toward the puddle from the opposite side, with Duck already standing there

vocabWords.definition_4_7: The long nose part of a pig's face.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: The protruding nose and mouth area of animals like pigs, dogs, and elephants.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The dog's snout was wet and cold.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Moved from side to side in a wobbly way.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Made quick, small movements back and forth or from side to side repeatedly.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The worm wiggled through the dirt.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: The feeling when something makes you very happy.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: A strong feeling of joy and energy that makes you want to move and smile.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The children jumped with excitement when they saw the circus.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Made a high squeaky noise when very excited.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Made a sharp, high-pitched sound that shows strong emotion like excitement or surprise.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The girl squealed when she saw the surprise present.

pages.label: Page 4
pages.text: 'I saw it first!' said Duck, ruffling her soft feathers. 'No, I did!' snorted Pig, stamping his little hooves. The puddle waited quietly between them.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Duck and Pig facing each other on opposite sides of the puddle, both looking determined but not angry, with ruffled feathers and stamping feet

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Moving feathers to make them stick up and fluffy.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Disturbing feathers or fur so they become uneven, messy, or stand up from the body.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The wind was ruffling the bird's feathers.

vocabWords.definition_2_4: The soft things that cover birds.
vocabWords.definition_4_7: The light fluffy things that keep birds warm.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: The soft, light coverings that grow on birds' bodies to help them fly and stay warm.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The peacock showed off its colorful feathers.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Pushed air out of the nose with a loud sound.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Made a sharp, loud sound by forcing air through the nose, often when annoyed or excited.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The horse snorted and shook its head.

vocabWords.definition_2_4: Hard feet on animals like pigs.
vocabWords.definition_4_7: The hard bottom parts of some animals' feet.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: The hard, horn-like coverings on the feet of animals like horses, pigs, and cows.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The horse's hooves made clicking sounds on the road.

pages.label: Page 5
pages.text: Duck stepped into the puddle. Pig stepped in too. The water felt cool and perfect on their feet. But there was no room to splash!
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Duck and Pig both standing awkwardly in the same puddle, looking cramped and uncomfortable, with water barely covering their feet

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Moved by putting one foot in a place.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Placed a foot down in a particular spot while walking or moving carefully.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: She stepped on a rock while crossing the stream.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: A nice temperature that feels good and not warm.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: A pleasant temperature that feels refreshing and comfortable, not warm or hot.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The cool breeze felt good on the hot day.

vocabWords.definition_2_4: Just right, the best it can be.
vocabWords.definition_4_7: Exactly how you want something to be.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Something that is exactly right and couldn't be any better in any way.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The weather was perfect for our picnic.

pages.label: Page 6
pages.text: Duck looked at Pig's sad face. Pig looked at Duck's droopy feathers. The puddle was big enough for both of them. Why were they being silly?
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Close-up of Duck and Pig looking at each other with sad, thoughtful expressions, realizing they could both fit in the large puddle

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Hanging down low like when you feel sad.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Hanging down limply or sagging, often showing that someone feels sad or tired.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The flowers looked droopy because they needed water.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Without making any noise or very little noise.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: In a way that makes very little or no sound, peacefully and without disturbance.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The cat quietly walked across the room.

vocabWords.definition_2_4: In the middle of two things.
vocabWords.definition_4_7: In the space that separates two things or people.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: In the area or space that lies in the middle, separating two or more things.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The ball rolled between the two trees.

pages.label: Page 7
pages.text: 'Want to splash together?' asked Duck sweetly. Pig's curly tail wiggled with joy. 'Yes! Let's make big splashes!' he oinked happily.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Duck extending a wing toward Pig in friendship, while Pig's tail curls with excitement, both smiling in the sparkling puddle

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Speaking in a gentle and kind way.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: In a gentle, kind, and pleasant way that makes others feel good and cared for.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: She sweetly asked if she could help with the dishes.

vocabWords.definition_2_4: Twisted in circles like a spring.
vocabWords.definition_4_7: Twisted around in circles or loops like a spiral.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Formed into curves, loops, or spirals that twist and turn in a rounded pattern.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The girl had beautiful curly hair.

vocabWords.definition_2_4: Made the sound a pig makes.
vocabWords.definition_4_7: Made the noise that pigs make when they talk.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Made the characteristic grunting sound that pigs produce when they communicate or express emotion.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The little pig oinked loudly when it saw the food.

pages.label: Page 8
pages.text: SPLASH! They jumped together. Water flew everywhere, making rainbow drops in the sunshine. They giggled and splashed some more.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Duck and Pig mid-jump in the puddle with water droplets flying all around them, creating small rainbows in the sunlight, both laughing joyfully

vocabWords.definition_4_7: In every place you can see or think of.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: In all places and directions around a particular area, covering a wide space completely.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: There were toys everywhere in the playroom.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: A curved line of many colors in the sky.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: A curved band of colors that appears in the sky when sunlight shines through water drops.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: We saw a beautiful rainbow after the storm ended.

vocabWords.definition_2_4: Laughed in a happy, quiet way.
vocabWords.definition_4_7: Laughed with little happy sounds that bubble up.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Laughed in a light, bubbly way with soft, happy sounds that show joy and amusement.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The baby giggled when daddy made funny faces.

pages.label: Page 9
pages.text: Duck spun in circles. Pig did belly flops. Together they made the most wonderful splashes. The puddle was even more fun with a friend!
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Duck spinning gracefully while Pig does a playful belly flop, both creating different splash patterns in the puddle, looking absolutely delighted

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Round shapes that go around and around continuously.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Perfectly round shapes where every point is the same distance from the center, like rings or wheels.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The children sat in a circle to play the game.

vocabWords.definition_2_4: The round part in front of your body.
vocabWords.definition_4_7: The soft round part of your body where food goes.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: The front part of the body between the chest and legs where the stomach is located.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The dog rolled over to show his belly.

vocabWords.definition_2_4: Falls down in a loose, floppy way.
vocabWords.definition_4_7: Falls or drops down in a soft bouncy way.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Falls or drops down heavily in a loose, uncontrolled way that often makes a soft thud.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The tired dog flops down on the soft couch.

pages.label: Page 10
pages.text: When the puddle got small, they found another one. Duck and Pig became the very best puddle pals. Sharing made everything better!
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Duck and Pig walking together toward a new puddle in the distance, their reflections showing in the water, with big smiles and friendship radiating between them

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Letting others use or have some of your things.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Giving part of what you have to others so everyone can enjoy it together.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The boy was sharing his cookies with his friends.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Changed from one thing into something else over time.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Gradually changed or developed from one state or condition into something different or new.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The caterpillar became a beautiful butterfly.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Friends who like to spend time together and play.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Close friends who enjoy each other's company and like to do fun activities together.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The two boys were best pals who did everything together.

fr5.title: The New Kid
fr5.obstacle: being new at school where everyone already has friends

pages.label: Page 1
pages.text: Amara pressed her nose against the cool car window and stared at Sunshine Elementary School. The red brick building looked enormous, and children were laughing and running everywhere. Her tummy felt like butterflies were doing somersaults inside it.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: A young girl with curly dark hair looking nervously out a car window at a cheerful brick school building with colorful playground equipment and children playing

vocabWords.definition_4_7: To push something firmly against another thing.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: To push or squeeze something firmly against another surface with steady pressure.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: She pressed the doorbell and waited for someone to answer.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Something that is really, really big.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Something so large that it seems almost too big to believe or imagine.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The enormous elephant was the biggest animal at the zoo.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Rolling forward or backward in a complete circle.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: A gymnastic move where you roll your body in a complete circle, head over heels.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The gymnast did three somersaults across the mat.

pages.label: Page 2
pages.text: "Remember, sweetheart," Mom said gently, squeezing Amara's hand. "Being brave doesn't mean you're not scared. It means you try new things even when you feel nervous." Amara nodded and took a deep breath that smelled like her mom's vanilla perfume.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: A caring mother with warm eyes holding hands with her daughter in the front seat of a car, both looking supportive and gentle

vocabWords.definition_4_7: A loving name parents call their children.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: A special, tender name used to show love and affection for someone dear.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: Grandma always calls me sweetheart when she hugs me.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Feeling worried or scared about something that might happen.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Feeling anxious and unsure, like butterflies are fluttering around inside your stomach.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: I felt nervous before my first piano recital.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: A sweet smell that comes from a special plant.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: A warm, sweet fragrance that comes from vanilla beans and is used in perfumes.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The bakery smelled like vanilla and fresh cookies.

pages.label: Page 3
pages.text: The hallway buzzed with excitement as children hugged their friends and shared stories about their weekend. Amara's new sneakers squeaked softly on the shiny floor as she followed Mrs. Chen to her classroom. Everyone seemed to know exactly where they belonged.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: A bright school hallway with colorful bulletin boards, children reuniting with friends, and a nervous girl with a backpack following a kind teacher

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Made a busy, humming sound like lots of bees.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Filled with busy, excited activity that creates a humming sound of voices and movement.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The marketplace buzzed with people buying and selling things.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: A happy, bouncy feeling when something fun is happening.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: A thrilling feeling of joy and energy that makes you want to jump and celebrate.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The children could barely contain their excitement about the birthday party.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Made a high, little sound like a mouse.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Made a short, high-pitched sound like a door that needs oil on its hinges.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The old rocking chair squeaked every time Grandpa sat in it.

pages.label: Page 4
pages.text: "Class, I'd like you to meet Amara," Mrs. Chen announced with a warm smile. Twenty pairs of curious eyes turned toward her, and Amara felt her cheeks grow hot. She managed a small wave and whispered, "Hi," wishing she could disappear behind her purple backpack.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: A classroom full of diverse children at their desks looking curiously at a shy new girl standing at the front next to a friendly teacher with a warm smile

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Said something important in a loud, clear voice.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Spoke in a clear, official way to make sure everyone could hear important news.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The principal announced that school would end early because of snow.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Wanting to know more about something interesting.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Eager to learn and discover new things, like a detective looking for clues.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The curious kitten poked its head into every box it found.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Was able to do something even though it was hard.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Successfully did something difficult by trying hard and not giving up easily.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: Even with a broken arm, she managed to write her name.

pages.label: Page 5
pages.text: During art time, Amara sat alone at the craft table, carefully coloring a picture of a rainbow. She listened to the other children giggling as they shared crayons and compared their drawings. The yellow crayon felt smooth in her fingers, but her heart felt a little lonely.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: A girl sitting alone at a craft table coloring a rainbow picture while other children work together in small groups, sharing art supplies and laughing

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Making things with your hands using paper and glue.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Creating art projects and handmade items using materials like paper, glue, and scissors.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: We made a craft project using pinecones and colorful ribbons.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Making light, happy laughing sounds that bounce up and down.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Laughing in a light, bubbly way that sounds like tiny bells of joy ringing together.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The baby kept giggling every time the dog licked her face.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Looked at two things to see how they're the same or different.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Examined different things side by side to notice what makes them similar or unique.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: We compared our heights by standing back to back against the wall.

pages.label: Page 6
pages.text: At recess, Amara watched from the green wooden bench as groups of friends played tag and jumped rope. The spring air smelled like fresh grass and blooming flowers. She remembered her mom's words about being brave and slowly stood up, her heart beating like a drum.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: A girl sitting alone on a green bench watching other children play tag and jump rope on a sunny playground with blooming trees and fresh spring grass

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Time at school when kids go outside to play.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: A special break time during the school day when children can play and have fun outdoors.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: During recess, we played soccer on the grassy field behind our house.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: When flowers are opening up and showing their pretty colors.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: The magical time when flower buds open to reveal their beautiful petals and sweet scents.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The cherry trees were blooming with pink flowers all along the street.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Making the thump-thump sound that hearts make inside your chest.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Pounding rhythmically like a drum, the way your heart sounds when you're excited or scared.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: After running up the stairs, my heart was beating very fast.

pages.label: Page 7
pages.text: Near the monkey bars, Amara noticed a girl with braided hair sitting by herself, looking sad. The girl was holding a book with a sparkly cover. Taking a deep breath for courage, Amara walked over, her legs feeling wobbly like jelly.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: A girl with braided hair sitting alone under playground equipment holding a sparkly book, while Amara approaches nervously but with determination

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Hair that's been twisted together in a special pattern.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Hair that's been woven together in an over-and-under pattern to create a rope-like style.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: My sister braided colorful ribbons into her long, dark hair.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Shiny and glittery like tiny stars twinkling.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Covered with small, shimmering pieces that catch the light and twinkle like little diamonds.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: Her sparkly dress caught the light every time she danced.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Being brave enough to do something that scares you.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: The inner strength that helps you face your fears and do the right thing.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: It took courage for the little boy to pet the big, friendly dog.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Shaky and unsteady, like something might fall over.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Moving in an unsteady way, like a tower of blocks that might tumble at any moment.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The wobbly table made it hard to write without the paper shaking.

pages.label: Page 8
pages.text: "Hi, I'm Amara," she said softly. "I like your book. Is it about magic?" The girl looked up with surprised brown eyes and smiled. "I'm Sofia! Yes, it's about a wizard cat. I love stories about magic and adventure. Do you want to sit with me?"
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Two girls meeting for the first time - one holding a sparkly book about magic, both smiling as they begin to connect, sitting on playground mulch

vocabWords.definition_4_7: A magical person who can do spells and tricks.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: A person with magical powers who can cast spells and make impossible things happen.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The wizard waved his magic wand and turned the pumpkin into a carriage.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: An exciting trip or experience with surprises.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: An exciting journey or experience filled with unknown challenges and wonderful discoveries.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: Our camping trip turned into a great adventure when we discovered the hidden waterfall.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Feeling shocked when something unexpected happens.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Feeling amazed and caught off guard when something happens that you didn't expect at all.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: I was surprised to find a birthday present hidden under my bed.

pages.label: Page 9
pages.text: Sofia patted the soft ground beside her, and Amara sat down gratefully. They discovered they both loved purple, had pet goldfish, and enjoyed drawing pictures of castles. Sofia's laugh sounded like tiny silver bells ringing in the sunshine.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Two girls sitting together on the ground, animatedly talking and laughing, with the sparkly book between them and spring flowers blooming nearby

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Touched something gently with your hand several times.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Gave gentle, repeated touches with your hand to show kindness or comfort.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: She patted the sad puppy's head to make it feel better.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Feeling very thankful and happy that someone was nice to you.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: With a heart full of appreciation and thankfulness for someone's kindness or help.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: I gratefully accepted the warm coat when I was shivering in the cold.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Found out something new that you didn't know before.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Learned or found something exciting and new, like an explorer finding hidden treasure.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: We discovered a family of rabbits living under our back porch.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Big, strong buildings where kings and queens used to live.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Large, fortress-like buildings with tall towers where royal families lived long ago in fairy tales.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The princess lived in one of the most beautiful castles in the kingdom.

pages.label: Page 10
pages.text: When the bell rang, Sofia grabbed Amara's hand. "Want to be my reading partner?" she asked with sparkling eyes. Walking back to class together, Amara realized her butterfly feelings had transformed into warm, happy bubbles. Being brave had led her to something wonderful - a new friend.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Two girls holding hands and walking happily toward the school building together, both smiling brightly with the sunny playground behind them

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Quickly took hold of something with your hand.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Reached out quickly and took firm hold of something with sudden, eager movement.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: She grabbed her umbrella when she saw the dark rain clouds.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Shining bright like little stars twinkling in the light.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Glittering and shimmering with tiny points of light that dance and twinkle beautifully.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The sparkling snow looked like diamonds covering the mountain.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Changed from one thing into something completely different.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Changed completely from one form into another, like a caterpillar becoming a beautiful butterfly.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The old, empty lot was transformed into a beautiful community garden.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Round, see-through balls filled with air that float.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Light, airy spheres that float gently through the air and pop with the tiniest touch.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The children chased the soap bubbles floating across the backyard.

comprehensionQuestions.question: How did Amara feel when she first saw the school?

options.text: Like butterflies were doing somersaults in her tummy
Excited to play with all the children
Angry that she had to go to a new school

comprehensionQuestions.question: Why do you think Amara walked over to Sofia?

options.text: Sofia looked sad and alone, just like Amara felt
Sofia had the prettiest book cover
The teacher told her to go talk to Sofia

comprehensionQuestions.question: Have you ever felt nervous about trying something new?

options.text: Yes, starting at a new school or meeting new friends
Yes, trying a new sport or activity for the first time
Yes, speaking up in front of other people

predictionPause.question: What do you think Amara will do when she reaches Sofia?

options.text: She'll say hello and ask about the sparkly book
She'll get too scared and walk away
She'll sit down quietly without saying anything

fr6.title: The Blanket Fort
fr6.obstacle: his sister wants to come into his blanket fort but he wants it all to himself

pages.label: Page 1
pages.text: Milo found the softest blue blanket. He pulled it over two chairs. "My fort!" he said happily.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: A young boy with curly hair dragging a fluffy blue blanket over two wooden chairs in a sunny living room, smiling with excitement

vocabWords.definition_4_7: The most gentle and smooth thing you can feel.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Something that feels incredibly gentle and comfortable when you touch it, like a cloud.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The kitten's fur was the softest thing in the whole house.

vocabWords.definition_2_4: Something soft to keep you warm.
vocabWords.definition_4_7: A soft cover that keeps you cozy and warm.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: A large, soft piece of fabric used to cover yourself for warmth and comfort.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: Dad tucked the blanket around me at bedtime.

vocabWords.definition_2_4: A special hiding place to play.
vocabWords.definition_4_7: A protected place built for playing and hiding inside.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: A shelter or protected space that someone builds as a safe place to play.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The children built a fort out of cardboard boxes in the backyard.

pages.label: Page 2
pages.text: Inside his fort, everything was cozy and blue. Milo brought his teddy bear and favorite book.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Inside view of the blanket fort with soft blue light filtering through, Milo sitting cross-legged with a brown teddy bear and colorful picture book

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Feeling warm, comfortable and safe in a small space.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: A comfortable, warm feeling when you're in a snug, pleasant place that feels safe.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The little cabin felt cozy with the fireplace crackling.

vocabWords.definition_2_4: A soft toy bear to hug.
vocabWords.definition_4_7: A stuffed bear toy that's soft and cuddly.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: A soft, stuffed toy bear that children love to hold and cuddle with.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: Sarah carries her teddy bear everywhere she goes.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Something you like more than anything else like it.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: The thing you prefer above all others because it makes you especially happy or comfortable.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: Chocolate is my favorite flavor of ice cream.

pages.label: Page 3
pages.text: "Can I come in?" asked his little sister Emma. She peeked under the blanket with big, hopeful eyes.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: A small girl with pigtails lifting the edge of the blue blanket, looking hopefully inside where Milo sits with his arms protectively around his teddy bear

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Looked at something secretly or carefully without being seen.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Took a quick, careful look at something while trying not to be noticed.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The mouse peeked out of its hole to check for cats.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Feeling like something nice might happen soon.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Having a positive feeling that something you want will probably happen or work out well.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The children felt hopeful that it would stop raining so they could play outside.

pages.label: Page 4
pages.text: "No!" said Milo. "This is MY fort. Go away!" Emma's face looked sad and disappointed.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Milo frowning and pointing away while Emma's face shows disappointment, her shoulders drooping as she lets the blanket edge fall

vocabWords.definition_2_4: Feeling sad when something doesn't happen.
vocabWords.definition_4_7: Sad because something you wanted didn't happen the way you hoped.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: The unhappy feeling you get when something you were looking forward to doesn't work out.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: Jack was disappointed when the playground was closed for repairs.

pages.label: Page 5
pages.text: Milo read his book alone. But the fort felt too quiet. Too big for just him and teddy.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Milo sitting alone in the large blanket fort looking small and lonely, his teddy bear beside him, the book open but unread on his lap

vocabWords.definition_2_4: By yourself with no one else.
vocabWords.definition_4_7: Being somewhere by yourself without other people around.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: In a place or situation where you are the only person there, without company.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The old tree stood alone in the middle of the empty field.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: A place or time when there's very little sound.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Having little or no noise, creating a peaceful but sometimes lonely feeling around you.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The library was so quiet you could hear a pin drop.

pages.label: Page 6
pages.text: He heard Emma playing with blocks nearby. She was building something and humming a little song.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: View from inside the fort showing Emma in the background building colorful blocks into a tower, humming happily to herself

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Something that is not far away from your location.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: At a short distance from where you are, close enough to easily see or hear.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: We found a nearby park where we could have our picnic.

vocabWords.definition_2_4: Square toys you can stack up.
vocabWords.definition_4_7: Square or rectangle toys used for building and stacking.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Small, solid pieces usually made of wood or plastic that children use to build structures.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The baby knocked down the tower of blocks with one swipe.

vocabWords.definition_2_4: Making music sounds with your mouth closed.
vocabWords.definition_4_7: Singing a tune quietly with your lips closed together.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Making musical sounds by vibrating your voice while keeping your mouth gently closed, creating a soft melody.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: Mom was humming her favorite song while she cooked dinner.

pages.label: Page 7
pages.text: "Emma?" called Milo softly. "Do you want to come in my fort? We can read together."
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Milo peeking his head out from the blanket fort with a gentle smile, reaching his hand toward Emma who looks up from her blocks with surprise and joy

vocabWords.definition_2_4: Speaking in a very quiet voice.
vocabWords.definition_4_7: Using a gentle, quiet voice when you talk to someone.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Speaking in a tender, low voice that shows care and doesn't disturb the peaceful mood.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The teacher spoke softly to calm the crying child.

vocabWords.definition_2_4: Being with someone else at the same time.
vocabWords.definition_4_7: Two or more people doing something at the same time.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: People being in the same place or doing the same activity at the same time, sharing an experience.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The family worked together to plant flowers in their garden.

pages.label: Page 8
pages.text: Emma crawled in with her stuffed bunny. "It's so cozy!" she whispered. They snuggled close together.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Both children sitting happily inside the blue-tinted fort, Emma holding a white stuffed bunny, both children smiling as they sit shoulder to shoulder

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Moving forward by using your hands and knees to go somewhere.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Moving along the ground by supporting your body with your hands and knees instead of walking.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The baby crawled across the carpet to reach her favorite toy.

vocabWords.definition_2_4: A toy filled with soft things inside.
vocabWords.definition_4_7: A toy animal filled with soft material to make it cuddly.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: A toy made of fabric and filled with soft materials like cotton to create a huggable companion.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The stuffed elephant was so big it barely fit on the bed.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Speaking so quietly that only people very close can hear you.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Speaking in an extremely soft voice, usually to keep something secret or avoid disturbing others nearby.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: She whispered the secret into her friend's ear so no one else could hear.

vocabWords.definition_2_4: Getting close and cozy with someone.
vocabWords.definition_4_7: Moving close to someone to feel warm and comfortable together.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Cuddling up close to someone in a comfortable way that makes you both feel warm and safe.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The puppies snuggled together in their cozy basket to stay warm.

pages.label: Page 9
pages.text: Milo read the story out loud. Emma pointed at the pictures. Their toys listened too.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Milo holding the open picture book while Emma points excitedly at colorful illustrations, their teddy bear and bunny arranged as an audience

vocabWords.definition_2_4: Using your finger to show something.
vocabWords.definition_4_7: Using your finger to show someone where to look at something.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Extending your finger toward something to direct someone's attention to a specific thing or place.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The teacher pointed at the map to show where we live.

vocabWords.definition_2_4: Drawings or photos that show things.
vocabWords.definition_4_7: Images that show people, animals, or things in books or on walls.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Visual images like drawings, paintings, or photographs that represent people, places, or things we can see.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The museum had pictures of dinosaurs from millions of years ago.

vocabWords.definition_2_4: Using your ears to hear sounds.
vocabWords.definition_4_7: Paying attention to sounds and words with your ears.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Focusing your attention on sounds, voices, or music to understand or enjoy what you're hearing.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The children listened carefully to the thunder rumbling in the distance.

pages.label: Page 10
pages.text: The blanket fort was much more fun with Emma. Sharing made everything better and more magical.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Wide view of the glowing blue blanket fort with both children's silhouettes visible inside, laughing and playing together, toys scattered around them in the warm light

vocabWords.definition_2_4: Letting others use your things too.
vocabWords.definition_4_7: Giving part of what you have to others so everyone can enjoy it.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: The act of dividing or giving portions of something you have to others so everyone can participate or benefit.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: Sharing the cookies made everyone at the party feel happy and included.

vocabWords.definition_2_4: Something that feels like magic and wonder.
vocabWords.definition_4_7: Something so special and wonderful it feels like it has magic powers.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Having a special, enchanting quality that fills you with wonder and makes ordinary things feel extraordinary and amazing.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The first snowfall of winter created a magical scene in the forest.

fr7.title: The Bridge Builders
fr7.obstacle: two groups of kids on opposite sides of a creek who won't cooperate

pages.label: Page 1
pages.text: Ravi loved playing by Willow Creek, where the water gurgled over smooth stones and dragonflies danced in the afternoon sun. But there was one big problem that made his heart heavy. The kids from Maple Street played on one side of the creek, while the kids from Oak Avenue stayed on the other side. Nobody ever crossed over to play together, and Ravi wondered why two groups of children who could have so much fun were keeping themselves apart.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Ravi sitting alone by a babbling creek with willow trees drooping over the water, looking thoughtfully across to where two separate groups of children play on opposite banks

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Made a bubbling sound like water flowing over rocks.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Made a cheerful bubbling sound, like water laughing as it flows over stones in a creek.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The baby gurgled happily when her mom tickled her belly.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Colorful flying insects with big wings and long bodies.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Beautiful winged insects that hover and dart over water, catching smaller bugs with amazing speed.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The dragonflies in our garden help keep mosquitoes away.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Thought deeply about something and wanted to understand it.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Felt curious and thoughtful about something, really wanting to figure out the answer or reason.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: She wondered why her cat always hid under the bed during thunderstorms.

pages.label: Page 2
pages.text: One sunny morning, Ravi watched as the Maple Street kids built an amazing fort out of fallen branches and colorful leaves. Across the creek, the Oak Avenue children were creating something equally wonderful - a series of smooth stone towers that sparkled in the sunlight. Both groups kept glancing at each other's projects with curious eyes, but nobody made a move to get closer. Ravi could practically feel the invisible wall between them.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Two groups of children on opposite sides of a creek - one group building a leafy fort, the other stacking stone towers, with both groups stealing glances at each other

vocabWords.definition_4_7: So good or impressive that it surprises you.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: So wonderful and impressive that it fills you with wonder and makes you want to look closer.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The magician did an amazing trick that made a rabbit appear from thin air.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Shined with tiny bright flashes of light.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Glittered and gleamed with small points of light, like tiny stars dancing on a surface.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The snow sparkled like diamonds in the morning sunlight.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Something that cannot be seen with your eyes.
