vocabWords.definition_7_10: Something that exists but cannot be seen, like a barrier you can feel but not touch.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The invisible fence kept the dog in the yard using special signals.

pages.label: Page 3
pages.text: "Why don't you all play together?" Ravi asked Maya from Maple Street. She shrugged and pointed across the bubbling water. "They think they're better at building things than us," she said. When Ravi crossed the creek using the old fallen log and asked Sam from Oak Avenue the same question, Sam crossed his arms. "Those Maple Street kids always hog the best games," he replied. Ravi realized both groups had the wrong idea about each other.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Ravi talking to Maya near the leafy fort, then crossing a fallen log over the creek to speak with Sam near the stone towers

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Lifted your shoulders up and down to show uncertainty.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Made a gesture by raising and lowering your shoulders to show you don't know or care.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: When asked about the missing cookies, Tommy just shrugged and walked away.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Making lots of small round air pockets in water.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Creating foam and little air bubbles while moving, like a pot of boiling water or flowing stream.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The bubbling soda made her nose tickle when she took a sip.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Suddenly understood something that you didn't know before.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Had a moment when the truth became clear, like a light bulb turning on in your mind.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: She realized she had been wearing her shirt inside out all day.

pages.label: Page 4
pages.text: That evening, storm clouds gathered like gray cotton balls in the sky, and rain began to patter on the creek. By morning, Willow Creek had turned into a rushing river, and the old log bridge had been swept away by the strong current. Now the water was too deep and fast for anyone to cross safely. Ravi stared at the swirling brown water and felt worried - the two groups were more separated than ever before.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: A rain-swollen creek rushing rapidly between the banks, with the old fallen log nowhere to be seen, dark clouds overhead and puddles reflecting the gray sky

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Came together in one place from different areas.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Collected or assembled in the same spot, like when things come together to form a group.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The students gathered around the teacher to hear the exciting announcement.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Made quick, light tapping sounds over and over.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Created a gentle rhythm of soft tapping sounds, like tiny feet dancing on a roof.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: We could hear the patter of squirrel feet running across the attic floor.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: The flow of water moving in one direction.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: The powerful force of water flowing downstream, like an invisible river within a river pushing everything along.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The strong ocean current carried the beach ball far from shore.

pages.label: Page 5
pages.text: The next day brought sunshine, but the creek was still too dangerous to cross. Ravi noticed something interesting - Maya and her friends kept looking longingly at the Oak Avenue side, while Sam and his group seemed just as curious about what was happening on Maple Street. "We need those smooth stones for our fort," Maya sighed. Across the water, Sam was saying, "Those colorful leaves would make our towers even more beautiful." Ravi's eyes lit up with an idea.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Ravi standing between the two groups on either side of the swollen creek, with Maya pointing toward the stones and Sam gesturing toward the leafy fort

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Looking at something you really want but can't have.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Gazing with deep desire and sadness at something you wish you could reach or possess.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The puppy stared longingly at the treat on the high shelf.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Very interested in learning about or seeing something.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Filled with wonder and eagerness to discover, explore, or understand something better.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The curious kitten poked its nose into every box and bag.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Breathed out slowly and deeply when feeling sad or tired.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Let out a long, soft breath that showed feelings of disappointment, tiredness, or longing.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: Mom sighed when she saw the messy kitchen after our baking adventure.

pages.label: Page 6
pages.text: "What if we built a bridge together?" Ravi called out, his voice carrying across the water. Both groups turned to look at him with surprise. "The Maple Street kids have strong branches and rope," he continued, "and the Oak Avenue kids have those perfect flat stones for the foundation." For a moment, everyone was quiet except for the sound of water flowing. Then Maya and Sam looked at each other across the creek and slowly began to nod.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Ravi standing at the water's edge with his arms spread wide, addressing both groups, with children on both sides listening intently and Maya and Sam making eye contact across the water

vocabWords.definition_4_7: A sudden feeling when something unexpected happens.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: The startled feeling you get when something happens that you didn't see coming or expect.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: Her eyes widened in surprise when she opened the birthday present.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: The strong bottom part that holds up a building.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: The solid base layer that supports everything above it, like the roots of a strong structure.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The workers poured concrete to make a foundation for the new house.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Placing things carefully in the best spots for success.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Positioning something thoughtfully and cleverly to achieve the best possible result or advantage.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: She strategically placed her chess pieces to protect her king.

pages.label: Page 7
pages.text: The bridge-building began with careful planning and lots of shouting back and forth across the creek. The Oak Avenue kids selected the biggest, flattest stones and placed them strategically in the shallow parts near each bank. Meanwhile, the Maple Street children gathered the longest, sturdiest branches they could find. Working from opposite sides, both groups had to communicate constantly, calling out measurements and suggestions. Even though they couldn't cross yet, they were finally working as one team.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Children working energetically on both sides of the creek, some carrying large stones, others dragging branches, with kids calling instructions across the water

vocabWords.definition_4_7: The strongest and most solid things you can find.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: The most robust and reliable items that won't break or bend easily under pressure.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: Dad chose the sturdiest ladder to reach the roof safely.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Share ideas and information by talking or using signals.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Exchange thoughts, feelings, or information with others through words, gestures, or other methods.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The dolphins communicate with each other using special clicking sounds.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Numbers that tell you how big, long, or heavy something is.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Precise information about the size, length, weight, or amount of something using numbers and units.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The carpenter took careful measurements before cutting the wood for the bookshelf.

pages.label: Page 8
pages.text: As the bridge began to take shape, something wonderful started happening. Sam called out, "Maya, that knot you're tying looks really strong! Can you show me how?" Maya beamed and demonstrated slowly so he could see from across the creek. Meanwhile, one of the Oak Avenue kids shouted, "We should angle this stone the way you built your fort - it'll be much sturdier!" Ravi watched with growing excitement as compliments and helpful ideas flowed back and forth like the water beneath them.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Maya demonstrating rope-tying techniques while Sam watches from across the creek, with other children pointing and sharing ideas, the bridge structure growing between them

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Showed someone how to do something by doing it yourself.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Taught by example, showing the steps clearly so others could learn and copy the action.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The chef demonstrated how to fold dumplings on the cooking show.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Smiled in a very happy and bright way.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Showed pure joy through a radiant smile that seemed to light up your entire face.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The proud grandmother beamed when her grandson won the spelling bee.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Nice things you say about someone to make them happy.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Kind words that praise someone's abilities, appearance, or actions to show appreciation and encouragement.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: She gave her friend compliments about her beautiful singing voice.

pages.label: Page 9
pages.text: Finally, the moment arrived to connect both sides of the bridge. Ravi stood in the middle on a large stone, helping guide the longest branch as children from both groups pushed it across the final gap. "Almost there!" he called out as the branch settled into place with a satisfying thunk. The Oak Avenue kids cheered, the Maple Street children whooped with joy, and Ravi felt his heart swell with pride. They had built something amazing together that none of them could have made alone.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Ravi balanced on a stone in the creek, guiding a large branch into place while children from both sides push and cheer, the completed bridge spanning the water

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Join two things together so they touch or link up.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Bring separate parts together to form one continuous piece, creating a bridge between different sides.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: We need to connect these puzzle pieces to complete the picture.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Giving you a good feeling when something works out right.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Creating a deep sense of pleasure and accomplishment when everything comes together perfectly as planned.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: There's something satisfying about organizing a messy room and making it clean.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Made loud, happy shouting sounds to show excitement.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Let out joyful yells and cheers that expressed pure happiness and celebration about something wonderful.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The fans whooped with delight when their team scored the winning goal.

pages.label: Page 10
pages.text: Maya was the first to test the bridge, walking carefully across the sturdy branches and smooth stones to shake hands with Sam. Soon, all the children were crossing back and forth, sharing ideas and materials for their building projects. The Maple Street fort got beautiful stone decorations, while the Oak Avenue towers were crowned with the most colorful leaves anyone had ever seen. As Ravi watched his friends working and playing together, he smiled knowing that the best bridges aren't just made of wood and stone - they're built with friendship, cooperation, and the willingness to see the good in others.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Children happily crossing the completed bridge in both directions, with the fort now decorated with sparkling stones and the towers topped with bright autumn leaves, everyone working together in harmony

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Strong and solid, not easy to break or fall down.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Built to last with strength and stability, able to support weight and resist damage reliably.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The sturdy oak tree weathered many storms without losing a single branch.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Pretty things you add to make something look more beautiful.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Ornamental objects or designs added to enhance beauty and make something more visually appealing and special.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: We hung colorful decorations around the classroom for the holiday party.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Working together with others to reach the same goal.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: The spirit of teamwork where people combine their different strengths and ideas to achieve something great together.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The successful school fundraiser was a result of cooperation between teachers, parents, and students.

comprehensionQuestions.question: What happened to the old log bridge that let people cross the creek?

options.text: It was swept away by the storm water
The kids broke it while playing on it
It rotted away because it was too old

comprehensionQuestions.question: Why did Ravi think the two groups should work together to build the bridge?

options.text: Each group had different materials the other group needed
He wanted to be the boss of both groups
Building bridges was his favorite hobby

comprehensionQuestions.question: How do you feel when you have to work with someone you don't know very well?

options.text: A little nervous but also excited to make a new friend
Worried they might not like the same things I do
Curious about what cool ideas they might have

predictionPause.question: What do you think will happen when the kids try to build the bridge together?

options.text: They'll argue about the best way to build it
They'll discover they work really well as a team
The bridge will be too hard to build and they'll give up

fr8.title: The Wrong Gift
fr8.obstacle: he made a gift for his friend but it turned out all wrong

pages.label: Page 1
pages.text: Theo loved making things with his hands. His friend Maya's birthday was coming soon, and he wanted to make her something special. He gathered colorful paper, sparkly glitter, and his favorite markers. "This will be perfect!" he said with a big smile.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Theo sitting at a sunny craft table covered with art supplies - colored paper, glitter containers, markers, scissors. He's smiling excitedly with his hands reaching for the materials.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Brought different things together in one place.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Collected and brought together various items or materials for a specific purpose or activity.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: She gathered flowers from the garden to make a bouquet.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Something that shines with lots of tiny bright lights.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Having a bright, glittering appearance that catches light and creates small flashes of brightness.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: Her sparkly dress caught everyone's attention at the dance recital.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Tiny shiny pieces that make things look fancy and bright.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Very small reflective particles used for decoration that create a shimmering, sparkling effect.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The craft store had glitter in every color imaginable.

pages.label: Page 2
pages.text: Theo remembered that Maya loved butterflies. He carefully cut out butterfly shapes from bright orange paper. The scissors made satisfying snip-snip sounds as he worked. He hummed a happy tune, imagining Maya's surprised face when she saw his gift.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Theo concentrating as he cuts orange paper with child-safe scissors. Butterfly shapes are scattered on the table around him. Sunlight streams through a window.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Pretty insects with big colorful wings that fly around flowers.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Delicate flying insects with large, often brightly colored wings that flutter gracefully from flower to flower.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: Butterflies migrated south when the weather turned cold.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Something that makes you feel happy and pleased inside.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Giving a feeling of contentment and pleasure because something has been done well or correctly.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: It was satisfying to finally solve the difficult puzzle.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Thinking about something that isn't happening right now.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Creating pictures in your mind of things that aren't real or haven't happened yet.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: He was imagining what it would be like to fly like a bird.

pages.label: Page 3
pages.text: Next came the glitter! Theo squeezed bottles of purple and silver sparkles onto the paper butterflies. But something went wrong - way too much glitter poured out! The butterflies were buried under thick, lumpy mountains of glitter that wouldn't stick properly.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Theo looking surprised as excessive glitter spills from bottles onto his butterfly cutouts. Glitter is everywhere - on the table, floor, and Theo's hands. The butterflies are barely visible under glitter piles.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Pressed something tightly to make what's inside come out.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Applied pressure to something flexible to force its contents out through an opening.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: Mom squeezed the toothpaste tube to get the last bit out.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Little shiny bits that make things look bright and pretty.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Small reflective particles that catch and reflect light to create a twinkling, glittering effect.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The snow sparkles like diamonds in the sunlight.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Covered up completely so you can't see what's underneath.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Hidden completely under a layer of something, making the original object invisible or hard to find.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The dog buried his bone in the backyard.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Very big piles that look like tall hills.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Large elevated landforms that rise high above the surrounding area, or very big piles of something.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The mountains were covered with snow all year long.

pages.label: Page 4
pages.text: "Oh no!" Theo whispered. He tried to fix the butterflies by shaking off the extra glitter, but they tore apart in his hands. The beautiful orange paper became crumpled and soggy. Glitter scattered everywhere like tiny silver raindrops.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Theo holding torn, soggy butterfly pieces with glitter falling around him like confetti. His expression shows disappointment. The craft table is messy with glitter and paper scraps.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Spoke very quietly so only people close by could hear.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Spoke in a very soft, quiet voice, usually to keep others from hearing what was said.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: She whispered the secret in her friend's ear.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Got all wrinkled up and lost its smooth shape.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Became wrinkled and folded irregularly, losing its original flat or smooth appearance completely.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The letter got crumpled in his pocket.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Wet and soft, not dry and firm anymore.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Saturated with moisture and having a heavy, soft texture that lacks firmness or crispness.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The cereal got soggy when he left it in milk too long.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Spread out in different directions all over the place.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Spread widely in various directions without any organized pattern, covering a large area randomly.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The wind scattered the autumn leaves across the yard.

pages.label: Page 5
pages.text: Theo's shoulders drooped as he looked at the ruined gift. Maya's party was tomorrow, and his special present was completely wrong. He thought about buying something from the store instead. Maybe Maya would never know about his failed craft project.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Theo sitting sadly at the messy craft table, chin in his hands, looking at the ruined butterfly pieces. The room feels quiet and disappointed. Afternoon light casts long shadows.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Hung down sadly, like when you feel disappointed.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Sagged or hung downward, often showing sadness, tiredness, or disappointment through body language.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The flower drooped because it needed water.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Broken or messed up so badly it can't be fixed.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Damaged beyond repair or usefulness, making something completely worthless or unusable for its intended purpose.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The rain ruined their picnic plans.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: All the way, with nothing left out or missing.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: In every way possible, with absolutely nothing excluded, partial, or left undone in the process.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: She completely forgot about her dentist appointment.

pages.label: Page 6
pages.text: The next morning, Theo walked slowly to Maya's birthday party. He carried a store-bought gift in a shiny bag, but his heart felt heavy. The bag crinkled with each step, reminding him of his secret. Something didn't feel right about hiding the truth.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Theo walking down a sidewalk carrying a glossy gift bag, looking uncertain. Trees and houses line the street. His posture shows hesitation and worry.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Made small wrinkly sounds when touched or moved.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Produced light crackling or rustling sounds, usually from paper or plastic being handled or compressed.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The candy wrapper crinkled as she unwrapped it.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Making someone think about something they already knew.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Causing someone to remember or think about something from the past or something they had forgotten.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The smell of cookies was reminding her of her grandmother's kitchen.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Something you don't want other people to know about.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Information that is kept hidden from others and not meant to be shared or discovered.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: They had a secret handshake that only they knew.

pages.label: Page 7
pages.text: At the party, colorful balloons danced in the breeze and children laughed joyfully. Maya opened present after present, her eyes sparkling with delight. When she reached Theo's gift, he felt butterflies in his stomach - but not the good kind.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: A cheerful backyard party scene with balloons, children sitting in a circle, and Maya opening presents. Theo sits nearby looking nervous as Maya reaches for his gift bag.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: In a way that shows you're very happy and excited.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: With great happiness and enthusiasm, showing pure delight and celebration in every action or sound.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The children joyfully ran outside when school ended.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Shining brightly with lots of little flashes of light.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Glittering brilliantly with small points of reflected light, creating a bright and magical appearance.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The sparkling water looked beautiful in the sunlight.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: A feeling of being very happy and pleased about something.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Great pleasure and joy that comes from experiencing something wonderful or receiving something special.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: Her face showed pure delight when she saw the surprise party.

pages.label: Page 8
pages.text: Maya loved the store-bought puzzle, but Theo couldn't enjoy her smile. The secret felt like a rock in his chest. Finally, he took a deep breath and said, "Maya, I need to tell you something. I tried to make you a gift, but it went all wrong."
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Theo and Maya sitting together, with Theo looking earnest and a bit worried as he starts to speak. Maya is holding the puzzle box and looking at him with kind, curious eyes.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Something you buy from a shop instead of making yourself.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Purchased from a retail store rather than being handmade or created at home by yourself.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: His mom preferred homemade cookies over store-bought ones.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: A game with pieces you put together to make a picture.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: A challenging game consisting of interlocking pieces that must be assembled to form a complete image.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The thousand-piece puzzle took her all weekend to complete.

pages.label: Page 9
pages.text: Theo told Maya about the glittery butterfly disaster. He described the lumpy glitter mountains and the torn paper pieces. To his surprise, Maya giggled and her eyes lit up. "That sounds amazing! I wish I could have seen the glitter explosion!" she said warmly.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Maya laughing with delight as Theo gestures, telling his story. Her expression is full of joy and friendship. Other party elements are softly blurred in the background.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: When something goes very wrong and makes a big mess.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: An event that goes terribly wrong, causing damage, confusion, or failure of the original plan.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The kitchen was a disaster after he tried to make pancakes alone.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Told someone about something using lots of details and words.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Explained what something was like by giving specific details and information to help others understand.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: She described the beautiful sunset she saw at the beach.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: When something bursts and spreads out everywhere very fast.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: A sudden, dramatic bursting or scattering of something in all directions with great force or energy.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The balloon made a loud pop when it had an explosion of air.

pages.label: Page 10
pages.text: "Next time, let's make crafts together!" Maya suggested, giving Theo a big hug. Theo felt the heavy feeling lift from his chest like a balloon floating away. He learned that telling the truth made their friendship even stronger, and that was the best gift of all.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Maya and Theo hugging happily, both smiling broadly. The party continues around them with warm golden light. Both children look relaxed and joyful, showing their strong friendship.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Had an idea and told someone else about it.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Offered an idea or recommendation for someone else to consider as a possible solution or action.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The teacher suggested they work together on the project.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Moving gently through the air like it has no weight.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Drifting smoothly and weightlessly through air or water without sinking or falling down.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The leaf was floating on top of the pond.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: The special feeling you have with people you really like.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: A close relationship between people who care about each other and enjoy spending time together.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: Their friendship grew stronger after they helped each other through difficult times.

comprehensionQuestions.question: What happened when Theo tried to put glitter on the butterflies?

options.text: Too much glitter poured out and made a mess
The glitter was the wrong color for the project
He ran out of glitter before finishing

comprehensionQuestions.question: Why do you think Theo felt better after telling Maya the truth?

options.text: Keeping secrets made him feel heavy and sad inside
He wanted Maya to help him make more crafts
He was worried Maya would find out anyway

comprehensionQuestions.question: How do you feel when you make a mistake on something important?

options.text: Disappointed and worried about what others will think
Frustrated but ready to try a different way
Embarrassed but glad when friends understand

predictionPause.question: What do you think Theo will do about his secret?

options.text: Tell Maya the truth about what really happened
Keep the secret and pretend everything is fine
Show Maya the broken butterfly pieces

fr9.title: Side by Side
fr9.obstacle: her best friend is moving away at the end of summer

pages.label: Page 1
pages.text: Nina and Maya had been best friends since they were tiny. Every summer morning, they met at the old oak tree between their houses. The sweet smell of honeysuckle filled the air as they planned another day of adventures together.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Two young girls meeting under a large oak tree with honeysuckle vines. Their houses are visible in the background. Morning sunlight filters through the leaves.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Exciting trips or activities that are fun to do.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Exciting journeys or experiences that bring joy and create wonderful memories to treasure.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The pirates went on adventures to find hidden treasure on distant islands.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: A sweet-smelling flower that grows on vines.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: A fragrant flowering vine that produces tiny blossoms with a wonderfully sweet scent.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The honeysuckle growing on the garden fence attracted many buzzing bees.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Thought about what to do before doing it.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Carefully thought about and decided what activities or actions to do ahead of time.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: We planned a surprise party for Mom's birthday next week.

pages.label: Page 2
pages.text: They rode their bicycles down bumpy dirt paths, wheels spinning fast. Maya's red bike and Nina's blue bike looked like colorful streaks racing through the meadow. Their laughter echoed across the fields as dandelion seeds danced in the warm breeze.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Two girls on bicycles racing through a sunny meadow filled with dandelions. Their hair and clothes are flowing in the wind, faces full of joy.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Bikes with two wheels that you pedal to move.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Two-wheeled vehicles that you ride by pedaling with your feet to make them roll forward.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The store sells many different types of bicycles for children and adults.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Fast lines of color that move quickly by.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Bright bands or lines of color that flash past when something moves very quickly.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: Lightning streaks lit up the dark sky during the thunderstorm.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: A big field of grass and wild flowers.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: An open area of land covered with grass and wildflowers where animals often graze.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: Butterflies fluttered over the colorful flowers in the meadow.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: A sound that bounces back and repeats.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: When sounds bounce off surfaces and come back to you, making them repeat loudly.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: His voice echoed in the empty gymnasium when he called out.

pages.label: Page 3
pages.text: One afternoon, Maya sat quietly on the porch swing, not her usual bouncy self. Nina noticed her friend's sad eyes and the way she twisted her fingers nervously. Something was different, and Nina's heart felt heavy with worry.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Maya sitting alone on a wooden porch swing looking sad, while Nina approaches with a concerned expression. The porch has flowering plants and looks cozy but somber.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: A covered area outside the front of a house.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: A covered platform or deck attached to the entrance of a house where people sit.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: Grandma likes to sit on her porch and watch the sunset.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Full of energy and jumping around a lot.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Having lots of energy and enthusiasm, like someone who jumps and moves around happily.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The bouncy puppy couldn't sit still and kept hopping around the yard.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Feeling worried and moving in a fidgety way.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Acting in a restless, anxious way when you feel worried or scared about something.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: She nervously tapped her fingers while waiting for the test results.

pages.label: Page 4
pages.text: "My dad got a new job," Maya whispered, her voice barely audible. "We have to move to another state at the end of summer." Tears rolled down her cheeks like raindrops on a window. Nina felt her own eyes fill with tears too.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: The two girls sitting close together on the porch swing, Maya crying while Nina listens with tears in her eyes. Their body language shows comfort and sadness.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Talked very quietly so others can barely hear.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Spoke in a very soft, quiet voice that's almost too quiet to hear clearly.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The librarian whispered to remind everyone to be quiet.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Loud enough for people to hear clearly.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Able to be heard clearly by others, not too quiet or muffled to understand.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The teacher's voice was barely audible from the back of the classroom.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Small drops of water that fall from clouds.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Tiny drops of water that fall from the sky when it rains, making everything wet.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: Raindrops splashed against the car window during the storm.

pages.label: Page 5
pages.text: For the next few days, both girls felt gloomy. They walked instead of running, spoke in quiet voices instead of giggling loudly. Even their favorite ice cream from the truck tasted less sweet than usual.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: The girls walking slowly together, shoulders slumped, sharing ice cream from an ice cream truck in the background. The colors are slightly muted to show their sad mood.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Feeling sad and not happy about things.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Feeling sad, dark, and unhappy, like when storm clouds cover the bright sun.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The rainy weather made everyone feel gloomy and want to stay inside.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Laughing in a light, happy way that sounds musical.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Making soft, happy laughing sounds that show you think something is funny or delightful.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The children were giggling at the silly jokes in their picture book.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: The thing you like best out of all choices.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Something you enjoy more than anything else and always choose when you have options.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: Chocolate chip is my favorite type of cookie to eat after dinner.

pages.label: Page 6
pages.text: Then Nina had a wonderful idea that made her heart flutter with excitement. "Let's make this the best summer ever!" she declared. "We'll create so many happy memories that distance won't matter." Maya's face brightened like sunshine breaking through clouds.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Nina with an excited, determined expression gesturing enthusiastically while Maya's sad face begins to transform into a hopeful smile. They're standing in a sunny garden.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Something that makes you feel really happy and excited.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Something amazing and delightful that fills your heart with joy and excitement.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The circus performance was wonderful with dancing horses and funny clowns.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: To move quickly back and forth like butterfly wings.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: To move lightly and quickly with small, fast movements like a bird's wings beating.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The flag began to flutter in the strong wind outside the school.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Said something in a strong, clear voice.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Announced or stated something firmly and confidently so everyone could hear and understand.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The mayor declared that the new playground would open next month.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: How far apart two places or people are.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: The amount of space between two places, people, or things when they're far apart.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The distance between our house and the beach is about ten miles.

pages.label: Page 7
pages.text: They built the most magnificent blanket fort, using every pillow and sheet they could find. Inside their cozy hideaway, they told stories by flashlight and shared secrets that only best friends know. The fort smelled like lavender fabric softener and adventure.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: A large, elaborate blanket fort made from colorful sheets and pillows. The girls are inside with a flashlight, surrounded by snacks and books, looking happy and cozy.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Really amazing and beautiful to look at.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Extremely impressive and wonderful, something so beautiful it takes your breath away.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The magnificent castle had tall towers and sparkled in the morning sunlight.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: A soft cover used to keep warm in bed.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: A large piece of soft fabric used to cover yourself and stay cozy and warm.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: She wrapped herself in a fuzzy blanket while reading by the fireplace.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: A secret place where you can go to be alone.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: A special private place where you can hide, relax, and feel safe from the world.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The treehouse was their perfect hideaway for reading comic books together.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: A purple flower that smells really nice.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: A fragrant purple flower known for its sweet, calming scent that helps people relax.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: Mom put lavender soap in the bathroom because it smells so peaceful.

pages.label: Page 8
pages.text: They planted sunflower seeds in matching pots, promising to send photos as the flowers grew tall. Each seed was planted with hope and watered with friendship. "These will remind us we're still connected," Nina said, patting the rich, dark soil gently.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: The girls kneeling beside two matching flower pots, planting sunflower seeds. Their hands are dirty from the soil, and there's a watering can nearby. Both look hopeful and focused.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: A big yellow flower that follows the sun.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: A tall plant with large, bright yellow petals that turns to face the sun throughout the day.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The sunflower in our garden grew taller than my big brother this year.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Two things that look the same or go together.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Items that are identical or very similar, designed to look good together as a pair.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: She wore matching socks with the same polka dot pattern on both feet.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Joined together or linked in some way.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Joined or linked together, having a special bond that keeps things or people close.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The two islands are connected by a long bridge over the water.

pages.label: Page 9
pages.text: On Maya's last day, Nina gave her a friendship bracelet she had woven from bright purple and yellow threads. Maya hugged it close to her heart, then hugged Nina even closer. "Best friends forever, no matter how far apart," they promised each other.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: The girls sharing a tight hug in front of a moving truck. Nina is giving Maya a colorful braided bracelet. Their faces show both sadness and love. Moving boxes are visible nearby.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: A special close feeling between people who care about each other.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: A loving relationship between people who enjoy spending time together and support each other.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: Their friendship grew stronger after they helped each other through difficult times.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Jewelry that goes around your wrist to look pretty.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: A decorative band or chain worn around the wrist as jewelry or for special meaning.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The silver bracelet with tiny charms jingled softly on her wrist.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Made by crossing threads or strips over and under each other.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Created by carefully interlacing threads, strips, or strands together to form a pattern or fabric.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The basket was woven from long strips of colorful reed and grass.

pages.label: Page 10
pages.text: Now Nina tends two sunflower gardens and sends photos of both to Maya. They video call every week, sharing new adventures and old memories. True friendship grows stronger with love and care, just like sunflowers reaching toward the sun.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Nina in her garden taking a photo of tall, bright sunflowers with her phone. In the corner, there's a small image showing Maya on a video call, also surrounded by sunflowers. Both girls are smiling.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Takes care of something by giving it what it needs.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Carefully looks after and cares for something by providing attention, water, or other needs.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The farmer tends his vegetable garden by watering it every morning.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Places where people grow flowers, vegetables, or plants.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Special areas of land where people plant and care for flowers, vegetables, or other plants.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The community gardens provide fresh vegetables for families in the neighborhood.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Stretching out to try to touch or get something.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Extending or stretching toward something you want to touch, grab, or move closer to.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The cat was reaching its paw toward the toy mouse on the shelf.

comprehensionQuestions.question: What did Nina and Maya plant together before Maya moved away?

options.text: Sunflower seeds
Tomato plants
Rose bushes

comprehensionQuestions.question: Why do you think the girls decided to make it the best summer ever?

options.text: To create happy memories before being apart
To win a contest for best friends
To impress their parents

comprehensionQuestions.question: How do you stay close to friends when they live far away?

options.text: Video calls and messages
Letters and pictures
Sharing special memories

predictionPause.question: What do you think Nina and Maya will do to make their last summer together special?

options.text: Build something fun together
Go on exciting adventures
Make something to remember each other

fr10.title: The Argument
fr10.obstacle: he and his best friend had a big fight and aren't speaking

pages.label: Page 1
pages.text: Zack dragged his feet through the colorful autumn leaves as he walked to school. The crisp morning air should have made him feel excited, but instead his stomach felt heavy like a gray rain cloud. Yesterday, he and his best friend Marcus had their biggest fight ever. Now they weren't speaking to each other, and Zack didn't know how to fix it.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: A sad boy with dark hair walking alone on a leaf-covered sidewalk toward a school building, autumn trees with red and orange leaves overhead, morning sunlight filtering through branches

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Pulled something along the ground without lifting it up.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: To move slowly and heavily, as if pulling a heavy weight behind you.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: She dragged the heavy suitcase up the stairs.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Fresh, cold, and clean feeling in the air.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Sharp and refreshing, like the cool bite of fresh morning air.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The crisp apple made a loud crunch when I bit it.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Feeling very happy and looking forward to something.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Full of energy and joy about something that's happening or about to happen.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The children were excited about going to the zoo.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: The part inside your body that holds food.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: The organ in your belly that digests food, but also where you feel emotions.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: My stomach growled because I was hungry for lunch.

pages.label: Page 2
pages.text: In the classroom, Zack stole glances at Marcus sitting three desks away. Marcus was doodling rocket ships in his notebook, something he always did when he was upset. They had been best friends since kindergarten, sharing everything from lunch snacks to secret hideouts. But now an invisible wall seemed to stand between them, and it was all because of what happened at recess yesterday.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: A classroom scene showing Zack looking sadly across the room at Marcus, who is hunched over his desk drawing rockets, other students working at their desks, colorful educational posters on the walls

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Quick looks at something without staring too long.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Brief, sneaky peeks when you don't want someone to notice you're looking.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: She stole glances at the clock during the boring meeting.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Drawing little pictures without really thinking about it.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Making casual drawings or scribbles while your mind is thinking about something else.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: He was doodling flowers on his homework paper.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Something you cannot see with your eyes.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Cannot be seen, like air or wind, but you might still feel its effects.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The invisible wind rustled the leaves in the trees.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Secret places where you can go to hide.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Special hidden spots where friends gather privately, away from everyone else.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The kids built a hideout in the woods behind their house.

pages.label: Page 3
pages.text: The fight had started over something so silly - a new trading card that Jake from fourth grade wanted to trade. Both Zack and Marcus wanted the shiny dragon card, and they had argued about who saw it first. But the real problem wasn't the card at all. Zack had said something mean about Marcus always getting the best stuff because his parents had more money. The words had tumbled out before he could stop them.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Flashback scene showing two boys arguing over a glittering trading card near playground swings, with an older boy holding the card between them, both boys looking upset and angry

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Giving something to get something else in return.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Swapping one thing for another because both people want what the other has.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The kids were trading baseball cards during lunch.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Something that reflects light and looks bright and glossy.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Gleaming and smooth, catching the light like a mirror or polished metal.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The shiny new penny sparkled in the sunlight.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Had a fight with words about something you disagree on.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Disagreed strongly and spoke angrily about who was right or wrong about something.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The siblings argued about whose turn it was to wash dishes.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Fell or rolled down in a messy, uncontrolled way.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Spilled out quickly and carelessly, like rocks rolling down a steep hill.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The books tumbled off the shelf when I bumped into it.

pages.label: Page 4
pages.text: Marcus's face had turned bright red, and his eyes had filled with hurt tears. 'That's not fair, Zack! You know that's not true!' he had shouted before running away. Zack had gotten the dragon card, but it felt worthless in his hands. He knew he had been jealous and mean, and seeing his best friend's crushed expression made his chest ache with regret.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Close-up of Marcus with tears in his eyes and a hurt expression, running away from Zack who stands holding a trading card with a regretful look on his face, playground equipment in the background

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Very strong and intense in color or light.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Vivid and glowing, standing out boldly like a flame or neon sign.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The bright yellow school bus could be seen from far away.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Completely broken or very badly hurt inside your feelings.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Emotionally devastated, like your heart has been squeezed until it's flattened and broken.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: She felt crushed when her best friend moved to another city.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: The look on someone's face that shows their feelings.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: The way your face appears when emotions show through your eyes, mouth, and features.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: His worried expression told me something was wrong.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Feeling sorry and sad about something you did wrong.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: The heavy, uncomfortable feeling when you wish you could take back something you said or did.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: She felt regret after yelling at her little brother.

pages.label: Page 5
pages.text: At lunch, Zack sat alone at their usual table, picking at his peanut butter sandwich. The cafeteria buzzed with laughter and conversation, but everything sounded muffled and far away. He watched Marcus sitting with Tyler and Emma, but even surrounded by friends, Marcus looked as miserable as Zack felt. The dragon trading card sat forgotten in Zack's backpack, and he wished he had never seen it.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: School cafeteria with Zack sitting alone at a lunch table looking sad, while Marcus sits at another table with other children but also appears unhappy, lunch trays and colorful lunch boxes on tables

vocabWords.definition_4_7: A big room where lots of people eat meals together.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: A large dining hall where students or workers gather to eat their lunch or dinner.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The hospital cafeteria serves food to doctors and patients.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Made a low humming sound like lots of bees.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Filled with a constant, busy sound like many conversations happening at once.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The busy market buzzed with vendors calling out their prices.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Sounds that are quiet and hard to hear clearly.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Dulled or dampened sounds, as if heard through a thick blanket or wall.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: His voice sounded muffled when he spoke through the pillow.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Feeling very sad and unhappy about everything.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: So deeply unhappy that nothing seems fun or good, like a dark cloud over your heart.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The sick puppy looked miserable lying in its bed.

pages.label: Page 6
pages.text: That afternoon during art class, Mrs. Peterson asked everyone to paint something that made them happy. Zack stared at his blank paper for a long time, his paintbrush dripping blue paint onto the newspaper-covered desk. All he could think about were the fun times with Marcus - building snow forts, catching fireflies, and laughing until their stomachs hurt. Without thinking, he began painting two stick figures under a bright yellow sun.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Art classroom with Zack sitting at a desk covered in newspaper, holding a paintbrush and looking thoughtfully at a paper with two simple painted figures, art supplies and colorful paintings around the room

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Looked at something for a long time without looking away.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Gazed intensely and continuously, focusing your eyes on one thing without blinking much.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The cat stared at the bird sitting on the windowsill.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: A tool with soft hairs used for putting paint on paper.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: An artist's tool with bristles attached to a handle for applying paint to canvas or paper.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: She dipped her paintbrush in red paint to color the roses.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Liquid falling down drop by drop from something.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Slowly falling in small drops, one after another, like water from a leaky faucet.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: Water was dripping from the broken pipe onto the floor.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Small bugs that light up and glow in the dark.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Magical-looking insects that flash like tiny lanterns on summer evenings in gardens and fields.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: We caught fireflies in jars during our camping trip.

pages.label: Page 7
pages.text: After school, Zack walked slowly toward home, taking the long way past the park where he and Marcus used to play. The swings creaked gently in the breeze, and Zack remembered how they used to see who could swing the highest. His mom always said that being honest, even when it's hard, was the bravest thing you could do. Maybe it was time to be brave and tell Marcus the truth about why he had been so mean.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Empty playground with swings moving gently in the breeze, Zack standing at the edge of the playground looking thoughtful, afternoon sunlight casting long shadows, trees and houses in the background

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Made a squeaky, groaning sound like old wood bending.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Produced a slow, complaining sound that old things make when they move or bend.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The old wooden stairs creaked under my feet as I climbed up.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: In a soft, careful way that doesn't hurt anything.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: With tenderness and lightness, like a butterfly landing on a flower petal.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: She gently petted the nervous kitten until it calmed down.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: A light, soft wind that feels nice on your skin.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: A gentle current of air that whispers through trees and cools you on warm days.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The ocean breeze made the hot day feel much more comfortable.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Always telling the truth and never lying about things.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Being truthful and genuine, even when it's difficult or might get you in trouble.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The honest student admitted she forgot to do her homework.

pages.label: Page 8
pages.text: The next morning, Zack's hands felt sweaty as he approached Marcus at his locker. His heart hammered like a woodpecker against his ribs, but he took a deep breath and stepped forward. 'Marcus,' he said quietly, 'I need to tell you something.' Marcus turned around, his expression guarded but curious. The hallway seemed extra loud with slamming lockers and chattering students, but Zack focused on his friend's familiar brown eyes.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: School hallway with colorful lockers, Zack approaching Marcus who is at his open locker, other students visible in the background, both boys looking nervous but hopeful

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Wet with little drops of water from your body.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Moist with perspiration, usually from being nervous, hot, or working hard at something.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: After running the race, his sweaty shirt stuck to his back.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Walked closer and closer to someone or something.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Moved gradually nearer to a person or place, step by step, getting ready to interact.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The dog approached the new visitor cautiously, sniffing the air.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Hit something over and over with loud, hard sounds.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Pounded repeatedly and forcefully, like a tool striking nails into wood with steady beats.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: Rain hammered against the window during the thunderstorm.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: A bird that uses its beak to make holes in trees.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: A forest bird famous for its rapid, rhythmic pecking sounds as it drills into tree bark.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The red woodpecker made a loud tapping noise in the old oak tree.

pages.label: Page 9
pages.text: 'I was wrong yesterday,' Zack said, his voice shaky but determined. 'I wasn't really mad about the trading card. I was jealous, and that made me say something really mean and untrue. You're the best friend anyone could have, and I'm sorry I hurt your feelings.' He pulled the dragon card from his pocket and held it out. 'I don't even want this anymore. I just want my best friend back.'
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Close-up of the two boys facing each other in the hallway, Zack extending the shiny trading card toward Marcus with an apologetic expression, Marcus looking surprised and touched, warm lighting suggesting hope

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Trembling and unsteady, like when you're nervous or scared.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Quivering with emotion or uncertainty, like leaves trembling in a strong wind.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: Her shaky hands could barely hold the heavy box.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Deciding to do something and not giving up no matter what.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Having strong willpower and refusing to quit, even when something is difficult or scary.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The determined climber reached the top of the mountain despite the storm.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Feeling mad because someone else has something you want.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Envious and resentful when others have things, experiences, or advantages that you wish were yours.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: He felt jealous when his sister got a bigger piece of cake.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Not real or correct; the opposite of true facts.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: False and inaccurate, not matching what really happened or what is actually real.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The rumor about school being canceled tomorrow was completely untrue.

pages.label: Page 10
pages.text: Marcus's face slowly broke into a smile, like sunshine peeking through storm clouds. 'I missed you too, Zack,' he said, pushing the card gently back toward his friend. 'Keep it. But maybe next time we can share it?' They both laughed, and Zack felt the heavy feeling in his chest float away like a balloon. As they walked to class together, sharing jokes and planning their weekend adventure, Zack realized that being honest had been scary, but it was also the key to getting his best friend back.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: The two boys walking down the school hallway together, both smiling and laughing, Marcus's arm around Zack's shoulder in a friendly gesture, bright classroom doorways and happy students visible, conveying friendship and reconciliation

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Bright, warm light that comes from the sun in the sky.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Golden rays of light from the sun that bring warmth, brightness, and happiness to the world.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The flowers turned toward the sunshine streaming through the window.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Looking at something quickly by sticking your head out a little.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Taking a brief, cautious look from a hidden position, like playing peek-a-boo with the world.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The mouse was peeking around the corner to see if the cat was there.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: An exciting trip or activity that might have surprises.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: A thrilling journey or experience filled with discovery, fun, and maybe a little bit of risk.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The camping adventure included hiking, fishing, and sleeping under the stars.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Suddenly understood something important that you didn't know before.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Had a moment of clear understanding when the truth or answer became obvious and bright in your mind.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: She realized she had been wearing her shirt inside-out all day.

comprehensionQuestions.question: What did Zack and Marcus fight about at recess?

options.text: A shiny dragon trading card
Who could swing the highest
Building a snow fort together

comprehensionQuestions.question: Why do you think Marcus was doodling rocket ships in his notebook?

options.text: He was bored in class
He always drew rockets when he was upset
He wanted to be an astronaut

comprehensionQuestions.question: How do you handle it when you've hurt a friend's feelings?

options.text: Apologize and tell them the truth
Give them time and then talk about it
Do something nice to show you care

predictionPause.question: What do you think Zack will say to Marcus at the lockers?

options.text: He'll apologize and admit he was jealous
He'll give Marcus the dragon card
He'll ask if they can be friends again

s1.title: The Backwards Day
s1.obstacle: everything is happening backwards and nobody knows why

pages.label: Page 1
pages.text: Jax woke up to the strangest morning ever. His alarm clock was ticking backwards, and the sun seemed to be setting instead of rising! Even his orange juice tasted like it was flowing upward in his mouth.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Jax sitting up in bed looking puzzled, with a backwards-ticking alarm clock on his nightstand and an orange glow from the window that looks like sunset

vocabWords.definition_4_7: The most weird or unusual thing you've ever seen.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Something so unusual and odd that it's completely different from anything you've experienced before.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The strangest animal at the zoo was a zebra with purple stripes.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Making small clicking sounds like a clock does.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Making steady, rhythmic clicking sounds that mark the passage of time on a timepiece.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The ticking of the grandfather clock helped me fall asleep.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Going in the opposite direction from the normal way.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Moving or happening in the reverse direction from what is usual or expected.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: She walked backwards to surprise her little brother.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Moving up toward the sky or ceiling above you.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Moving in a direction that goes up toward a higher place or position.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The balloon drifted upward until it disappeared in the clouds.

pages.label: Page 2
pages.text: Downstairs, Mom was walking backwards to the kitchen while stirring pancake batter in reverse. The fluffy pancakes were shrinking back into batter, and the sweet smell of syrup was somehow floating downward instead of up.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Mom walking backwards in the kitchen, holding a mixing bowl with pancake batter swirling in reverse, with golden pancakes on the griddle appearing to melt back into batter

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Moving a spoon around in a bowl to mix things.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Using a utensil to mix ingredients together by moving it in circular motions.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: Mom was stirring the soup to make sure it cooked evenly.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: The wet mixture you use to make pancakes or cakes.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: A smooth, liquid mixture of ingredients like flour and milk used for cooking pancakes or baking.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The cake batter looked smooth and creamy in the bowl.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Light and soft like a cloud or cotton ball.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Having a light, airy texture that feels soft and bouncy when you touch it.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The fluffy kitten felt like a soft pillow when I petted it.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Getting smaller and smaller until it almost disappears.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Becoming progressively smaller in size, often until it's much tinier than it was before.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The ice cube was shrinking as it melted in the warm sun.

pages.label: Page 3
pages.text: Outside, the mailman was walking backwards from house to house, taking mail out of mailboxes instead of putting it in. Even the cheerful yellow flowers in Mom's garden seemed to be closing up and shrinking back into the soil!
pages.scene: illustration prompt: View through a window showing the mailman walking backwards while taking letters from a mailbox, with yellow flowers in the garden appearing to sink into the ground

vocabWords.definition_4_7: The person who brings letters and packages to your house.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: A postal worker whose job is to deliver mail and packages to homes and businesses.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The mailman always waves at me when he delivers our letters.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: The special boxes where people put mail and letters.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Container boxes placed near homes or on streets where mail is delivered and collected.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: All the mailboxes on our street are painted bright blue.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Happy and bright, making you feel good when you see it.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Full of joy and brightness that makes others feel happy and uplifted too.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The cheerful music made everyone want to dance and smile.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: When flower petals fold up and shut like a door.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: The action of petals folding inward or shutting, opposite of blooming and opening up.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The morning glories were closing as the sun began to set.

pages.label: Page 4
pages.text: At school, Jax's teacher Ms. Rivera was erasing words onto the whiteboard instead of writing them. The colorful chalk marks appeared like magic, while her eraser left behind bright letters and numbers that glowed in rainbow colors.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Ms. Rivera at a whiteboard holding an eraser that's creating colorful letters instead of erasing them, with Jax and other children watching in amazement from their desks

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Rubbing something out so the marks go away completely.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Using an eraser or similar tool to remove written or drawn marks from a surface.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: I was erasing my mistake so I could write the correct answer.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: A white board that teachers write on with special markers.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: A smooth white surface used in classrooms for writing and drawing with dry-erase markers.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The teacher drew a map on the whiteboard for our geography lesson.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Having lots of bright, pretty colors like a rainbow.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Filled with many vibrant hues and shades that create a bright, eye-catching appearance.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The colorful butterfly had wings with orange, blue, and yellow spots.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Gave off a soft, bright light like a firefly.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Emitted a warm, steady light that seemed to shine from within without being harsh.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The campfire glowed orange and red in the dark night.

pages.label: Page 5
pages.text: During recess, the playground was completely topsy-turvy. Children were sliding up the slide instead of down, and the swings were pushing kids instead of being pushed. The rubber mulch felt bouncy under Jax's feet as he watched in wonder.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: A playground scene with children sliding upward on slides and swings moving by themselves, with Jax standing on bouncy mulch watching everything with wide eyes and a curious smile

vocabWords.definition_4_7: The fun time at school when kids play outside.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: A break period during the school day when children go outside to play and exercise.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: During recess, we played soccer on the grass field.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Everything mixed up and upside down in a crazy way.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Completely disorganized and chaotic, with everything turned around or reversed from normal.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: My room was topsy-turvy after I looked for my missing sock.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Using your hands or body to move something away from you.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Applying force with your hands or body to move an object in a particular direction.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: Dad was pushing the heavy box across the room to the closet.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Springy and soft, like you could jump on it.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Having an elastic quality that springs back when pressed, creating a jumping sensation.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The bouncy castle made all the kids giggle as they jumped around.

pages.label: Page 6
pages.text: "This backwards day is strange," Jax thought, "but maybe I should try some backwards things too!" He decided to walk backwards to the monkey bars, even though it felt wobbly and made his sneakers squeak on the playground surface.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Jax carefully walking backwards toward the monkey bars with his arms out for balance, looking determined and curious, with other playground equipment visible in the background

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Made up your mind about what you want to do.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Thought carefully about choices and then picked what seemed like the best option.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: Sarah decided to wear her red dress to the birthday party.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Shaky and unsteady, like you might fall over.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Unsteady and unstable, moving back and forth without staying in one position.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The wobbly table kept moving when I tried to write on it.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Soft shoes with rubber bottoms for playing and running.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Comfortable athletic shoes with rubber soles designed for sports and casual activities.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: I tied my sneakers tight before running around the track.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Make a high, sharp sound like a mouse does.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Produce a short, high-pitched sound that often happens when things rub together.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The old door would always squeak when we opened it slowly.

pages.label: Page 7
pages.text: Jax tried swinging across the monkey bars backwards, gripping the cool metal bars with both hands. It felt scary at first, but then something magical happened - he discovered he could see the whole playground from a completely new angle!
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Jax hanging from monkey bars facing backwards, with a unique upside-down view of the colorful playground spread out below him, his face showing surprise and delight

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Moving from one bar to another using your hands.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Moving through the air by gripping bars or ropes and letting your body hang and swing.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The monkey was swinging from branch to branch in the tall tree.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Holding onto something very tight with your hands.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Grasping something firmly and securely with your fingers and hands to maintain control.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: I was gripping the bike handlebars as I rode down the steep hill.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Special and wonderful, like something from a fairy tale.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Extraordinary and enchanting in a way that seems to possess special powers or wonder.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The magical sunset painted the sky in pink and gold colors.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Found out something new that you didn't know before.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Learned or found something for the first time through exploration or investigation.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: We discovered a secret path through the woods behind our house.

pages.label: Page 8
pages.text: Next, Jax tried eating his peanut butter sandwich from the last bite to the first. The nutty, sweet flavors seemed to dance on his tongue in a completely new way. "This is actually fun!" he laughed, crumbs falling upward like confetti.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Jax sitting at a lunch table holding a partially eaten sandwich, laughing as crumbs float upward around him like golden confetti, with sunlight streaming through nearby windows

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Tasting like peanuts or other nuts you can eat.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Having the rich, earthy flavor that comes from peanuts, almonds, or similar edible nuts.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The nutty granola bar was my favorite snack for hiking trips.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: The different tastes you notice when you eat food.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: The distinct tastes that your tongue and mouth detect when eating or drinking something.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The ice cream shop had thirty different flavors to choose from.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Tiny pieces of food that fall off when you eat.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Small fragments or bits of food that break off from bread, crackers, or other baked goods.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The birds quickly ate up all the bread crumbs I scattered on the ground.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Tiny colorful pieces of paper thrown at parties.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Small bits of colored paper or material scattered in the air during celebrations and festivities.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: Golden confetti fell from the sky when our team won the championship.

pages.label: Page 9
pages.text: As Jax tried more backwards activities, other children began joining him. Soon the whole playground was filled with kids walking backwards, talking backwards, and playing backwards games. The air buzzed with giggles and excitement as everyone discovered new ways to have fun.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: The entire playground filled with children doing various backwards activities - walking backwards, playing backwards games, all laughing and having fun together with Jax in the center smiling proudly

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Different things you can do for fun or learning.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Various actions, games, or tasks that people do for enjoyment, exercise, or education.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: Summer camp had many fun activities like swimming, hiking, and crafts.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Coming together to be part of a group or activity.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Becoming part of something by participating with others who are already involved.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: My sister is joining the school choir because she loves to sing.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Made a humming sound like bees flying around flowers.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Filled with a continuous humming or vibrating sound that creates an atmosphere of activity.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The busy marketplace buzzed with the voices of people shopping and talking.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: The happy, jumpy feeling you get when something great happens.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: A feeling of eager enthusiasm and joy that makes your heart beat faster with anticipation.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The excitement in the theater grew as the curtain slowly began to rise.

pages.label: Page 10
pages.text: When the backwards day finally ended and everything returned to normal, Jax felt proud and happy. He had learned that trying new things - even backwards things - could lead to wonderful discoveries and lots of fun with friends.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Jax walking normally toward home as the sun rises properly in the background, with a big smile on his face and other children waving goodbye, the playground returning to normal behind them

vocabWords.definition_4_7: The usual way things happen every day.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: The regular, expected way that things typically occur without any unusual changes.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: After the storm passed, the normal sunny weather returned to our town.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Feeling really good about something you did well.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Having a sense of satisfaction and accomplishment from achieving something meaningful or worthwhile.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: Mom felt proud when I learned to tie my shoes all by myself.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: New and interesting things you find out for the first time.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Exciting findings or realizations that come from exploring and trying new experiences or ideas.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The scientist made important discoveries about how butterflies find their way home.

comprehensionQuestions.question: What was happening to Jax's alarm clock on this strange morning?

options.text: It was ticking backwards
It was ringing too loudly
It stopped working completely

comprehensionQuestions.question: Why do you think Jax decided to try backwards things too?

options.text: He wanted to fit in with the backwards day
He was confused and didn't know what to do
His teacher told him he had to

comprehensionQuestions.question: How would you feel if you woke up to a backwards day like Jax did?

options.text: Curious and excited to explore
A little scared but also amazed
Happy to try something new

predictionPause.question: What do you think will happen when Jax tries doing backwards things?

options.text: He'll discover something fun and new
Other kids will want to join him
He'll have trouble but keep trying

s2.title: The Hiccup Cure
s2.obstacle: Bunny has hiccups that won't stop and each hiccup does something funny

pages.label: Page 1
pages.text: Bunny was eating juicy orange carrots. *HIC!* Oh no! Bunny had the hiccups.
