pages.scene: illustration prompt: A fluffy white bunny sitting in a sunny garden with bright orange carrots scattered around, mouth open in surprise with one paw on chest

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Full of sweet liquid that tastes really good.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Having lots of flavorful liquid inside that makes something taste extra delicious and refreshing.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The juicy watermelon dripped down my chin.

vocabWords.definition_2_4: A bright color like a pumpkin.
vocabWords.definition_4_7: A color that is red and yellow mixed together.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: A warm, bright color that looks like autumn leaves or a beautiful sunset.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: My orange crayon is perfect for drawing pumpkins.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Long orange vegetables that rabbits love to eat.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Crunchy orange root vegetables that grow underground and are good for your eyes.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: We planted carrots in our garden this spring.

vocabWords.definition_2_4: Little jumps your body makes with funny sounds.
vocabWords.definition_4_7: Quick sounds your body makes that you can't control.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Sudden little spasms in your chest that make a sharp sound you can't stop.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: I got hiccups after drinking my soda too fast.

pages.label: Page 2
pages.text: *HIC!* Bunny's ears turned bright purple! *HIC!* Now they were green! The hiccups made funny magic.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Bunny with one purple ear and one green ear, looking confused and amazed, surrounded by sparkly magical dots in the air

vocabWords.definition_2_4: Parts of your head that hear sounds.
vocabWords.definition_4_7: The parts on your head that let you hear things.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: The organs on either side of your head that detect sounds and help you balance.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: I cover my ears when the music is too loud.

vocabWords.definition_2_4: Very colorful and easy to see.
vocabWords.definition_4_7: Having lots of light or very strong colors.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Shining with intense light or having colors that are vivid and eye-catching.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The bright sun made me squint my eyes.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: A color made by mixing red and blue together.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: A rich color that combines red and blue, like violets or royal robes.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: My favorite purple dress has sparkles on it.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Amazing things that happen in a mysterious way.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Mysterious powers that can make impossible or wonderful things happen like in fairy tales.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The magician used magic to make the rabbit disappear.

pages.label: Page 3
pages.text: *HIC!* Bunny bounced up high like a balloon! *HIC!* Down came Bunny with a soft thump.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Bunny floating in mid-air above the ground with surprised eyes, fluffy tail spread out, with motion lines showing bouncing movement

vocabWords.definition_2_4: Jumped up and down like a ball.
vocabWords.definition_4_7: Jumped up high and came back down again.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Sprang up energetically and then came back down, like a rubber ball hitting the ground.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The basketball bounced three times before I caught it.

vocabWords.definition_2_4: A colorful thing that floats in the air.
vocabWords.definition_4_7: A round rubber thing filled with air that floats.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: A flexible bag filled with air or gas that can float up in the sky.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: My red balloon floated away at the carnival.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: A dull sound when something hits the ground softly.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: A muffled sound made when something heavy but soft hits a surface gently.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: I heard a thump when my teddy bear fell off the bed.

pages.label: Page 4
pages.text: Mouse heard the funny sounds. She ran over fast. "Bunny, are you okay?" asked Mouse.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: A small gray mouse with kind eyes running toward Bunny, who is sitting on the grass looking worried and holding their tummy

vocabWords.definition_2_4: Used your ears to know about sounds.
vocabWords.definition_4_7: Used your ears to listen to sounds that happened.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Detected sounds through your ears and understood what was making those noises.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: I heard my mom calling my name from downstairs.

vocabWords.definition_2_4: Noises that you hear with your ears.
vocabWords.definition_4_7: Different noises that your ears can pick up.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Vibrations in the air that your ears detect and your brain interprets as noise.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The sounds of birds chirping woke me up this morning.

vocabWords.definition_2_4: A small furry animal with a long tail.
vocabWords.definition_4_7: A tiny animal that has whiskers and likes cheese.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: A small rodent with a pointed nose, long tail, and quick movements that scurries around.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The little mouse ran across the kitchen floor.

pages.label: Page 5
pages.text: *HIC!* Bunny's nose turned into a trumpet! It played loud music. Mouse giggled at the silly sound.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Bunny with a golden trumpet for a nose, musical notes floating in colorful bubbles around them, while Mouse covers her mouth giggling

vocabWords.definition_2_4: The part of your face that smells things.
vocabWords.definition_4_7: The part on your face that helps you breathe and smell.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: The organ in the center of your face used for breathing and detecting different scents.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: My nose tickles when I smell flowers.

vocabWords.definition_2_4: A shiny horn that makes music sounds.
vocabWords.definition_4_7: A musical instrument you blow into to make loud sounds.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: A brass wind instrument with valves that musicians blow into to create musical notes.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The trumpet player wore a fancy uniform in the marching band.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Laughed in a light, happy way that sounds musical.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Produced soft, bubbling laughter that shows you think something is amusing or delightful.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The baby giggled when I made funny faces.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Acting funny or goofy to make people laugh.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Behaving in a playful, foolish way that makes others smile or feel happy.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: My dad does silly dances that make me laugh.

pages.label: Page 6
pages.text: "I know how to help!" said Mouse. She brought Bunny a cup of cool water.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Kind Mouse holding a small blue cup of water with both paws, offering it to Bunny whose trumpet-nose is shrinking back to normal

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Took something from one place and gave it to someone.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Transported an object from one location to another person who needed or wanted it.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: My teacher brought cookies to share with the class.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: A small container that holds drinks for you to sip.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: A small drinking vessel with a handle that holds beverages like water or juice.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: I use my special cup for drinking hot chocolate.

vocabWords.definition_2_4: Not hot, feels nice and cold.
vocabWords.definition_4_7: A temperature that feels cold and refreshing to touch.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Having a lower temperature that feels pleasant and refreshing, especially when you're warm.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The cool breeze felt wonderful on the hot summer day.

pages.label: Page 7
pages.text: Bunny sipped the water slowly. *HIC!* One more little hiccup made a tiny rainbow appear.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Bunny drinking from the blue cup while a small, delicate rainbow arcs over both friends in soft, pastel colors

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Drank something slowly by taking very small amounts.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Consumed a liquid gradually by taking small, careful mouthfuls rather than big gulps.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: I sipped my hot soup so I wouldn't burn my tongue.

vocabWords.definition_2_4: Taking a long time, not fast.
vocabWords.definition_4_7: Moving or doing something at a speed that isn't quick.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Performing an action at a gradual pace without rushing, like a turtle walking.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The old dog walked slowly across the yard.

vocabWords.definition_2_4: Pretty colors in the sky like a bridge.
vocabWords.definition_4_7: Curved bands of different colors that appear in the sky.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: A colorful arc of light that appears when sunlight shines through water droplets in the air.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: We saw a beautiful rainbow after the rainstorm ended.

pages.label: Page 8
pages.text: The hiccups were gone! Bunny's ears were white again. "Thank you, Mouse!" said Bunny with a big hug.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Bunny and Mouse embracing in a warm hug, both smiling happily, with normal white bunny ears and the little rainbow fading sweetly in the background

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Something that was there before but isn't there now.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: No longer present in a place where it used to be, having disappeared or departed.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The cookies are all gone because we ate them.

vocabWords.definition_2_4: The color of snow and clouds.
vocabWords.definition_4_7: A bright color like fresh snow or fluffy clouds.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: The lightest color that reflects all light, like clean paper or winter snow.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: My white shoes got muddy when I walked through the puddle.

vocabWords.definition_2_4: Putting your arms around someone with love.
vocabWords.definition_4_7: Wrapping your arms around someone to show you care.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Embracing someone by putting your arms around them to express affection and warmth.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: Grandma always gives the best warm hugs when I visit.

pages.label: Page 9
pages.text: "Friends always help friends," said Mouse. They shared the rest of the orange carrots together.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Bunny and Mouse sitting close together on soft green grass, happily munching orange carrots, with content smiles and crumbs around them

vocabWords.definition_2_4: People who like you and play with you.
vocabWords.definition_4_7: Special people who care about you and like spending time together.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: People who enjoy each other's company, support one another, and share happy times together.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: My best friends and I love to build sandcastles at the beach.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Something that happens every single time without fail.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Continuously and forever, happening every time without any exceptions or breaks.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: Mom always reads me a bedtime story before I sleep.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Let other people have some of what you have.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Divided something you own with others so everyone can enjoy it together fairly.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: I shared my crayons with my little sister during art time.

s3.title: Spaghetti Hair
s3.obstacle: she wished her hair was more interesting and now it's made of spaghetti

pages.label: Page 1
pages.text: Olive stared at her reflection in the bathroom mirror and sighed deeply. Her straight brown hair hung like plain curtains around her face. "I wish my hair was more interesting," she whispered to her sleepy cat, Meatball, who was curled up on the fuzzy bath mat.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: A young girl with plain brown hair looking disappointed in a cozy bathroom mirror, with an orange tabby cat sleeping on a colorful bath mat nearby

vocabWords.definition_4_7: What you see when you look in a mirror.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: The image of yourself that appears when you look into a mirror or shiny surface.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: She saw her reflection in the calm water of the pond.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Made a long, sad breath sound when feeling disappointed.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Released a long, deep breath that shows you're feeling sad, tired, or frustrated about something.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: Dad sighed when he saw the messy playroom.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Pieces of cloth that hang to cover windows.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Long pieces of fabric that hang down to cover windows or divide spaces in rooms.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: Mom opened the curtains to let sunshine into the living room.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Talked very quietly so others couldn't hear well.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Spoke in a very soft, quiet voice so that only someone close by could hear you.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: She whispered the secret in her sister's ear.

pages.label: Page 2
pages.text: At school, Olive watched her friends with envy. Maya's curly red hair bounced like springs when she laughed. Sophie's blonde braids swished like golden ropes during jump rope. Even Tommy's spiky hair looked exciting with its purple streaks that caught the sunlight.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: A diverse group of children playing on a sunny playground, showing various interesting hairstyles - curly red hair, blonde braids, spiky purple-streaked hair - while Olive watches from the side

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Feeling jealous because someone else has something you want.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: The unhappy feeling you get when you wish you had something that belongs to someone else.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: Tim felt envy when his friend got a new bike.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Moved up and down like a ball hitting the ground.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Moved with a lively jumping motion, going up and down repeatedly like a rubber ball.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The basketball bounced three times before rolling away.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Moved back and forth making a soft whooshing sound.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Moved smoothly through the air with a gentle sweeping sound, like fabric flowing in the wind.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The horse's tail swished to chase away the flies.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Long thin lines of color or light.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Narrow bands or strips of color that run through something, creating lines or patterns.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: Lightning streaks lit up the dark storm clouds.

pages.label: Page 3
pages.text: That evening, Olive made a wish upon the first twinkling star she spotted outside her bedroom window. "Please make my hair more interesting," she whispered into the cool night air. A shooting star zipped across the sky, leaving a glittery trail behind it.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Olive leaning out her bedroom window at dusk, pointing at a bright star in a purple twilight sky with a shooting star streaking across, her room glowing warmly behind her

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Shining with little flashes of light that go on and off.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Sparkling with tiny points of light that seem to flicker and dance, like stars do in the night sky.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The Christmas tree lights were twinkling in different colors.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Saw something after looking carefully or by chance.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Noticed or caught sight of something, often after searching or when you weren't expecting to see it.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: We spotted a deer drinking from the stream.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Covered with tiny pieces that shine and sparkle brightly.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Decorated with small shiny particles that catch the light and create a sparkling, magical appearance.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: Her glittery dress caught everyone's attention at the party.

pages.label: Page 4
pages.text: The next morning, Olive woke up and felt something strange tickling her shoulders. She reached up to touch her hair and gasped! Instead of her usual brown strands, long pieces of golden spaghetti noodles hung from her head, smelling faintly of butter and herbs.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Olive sitting up in bed with wide surprised eyes, touching long golden spaghetti noodles that have replaced her hair, morning sunlight streaming through her window

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Lightly touching something in a way that makes you giggle.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Gently brushing against your skin in a way that creates a funny, tingly feeling that makes you want to laugh.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The feather was tickling my nose and made me sneeze.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Made a sudden, surprised sound by breathing in quickly.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Drew in a sharp, quick breath because you were shocked, surprised, or excited about something unexpected.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: Everyone gasped when the magician made the rabbit disappear.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Thin pieces of hair, string, or thread.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Individual thin pieces or threads that make up something larger, like single hairs or pieces of yarn.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: She found strands of yarn tangled in the cat's paws.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Plants that people use to make food taste better.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Special plants with strong smells and flavors that cooks add to food to make it more delicious.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: Mom grows fresh herbs like basil in her garden.

pages.label: Page 5
pages.text: "Mom!" Olive called, rushing downstairs with her spaghetti hair bouncing and swaying behind her. Her mother nearly dropped her coffee mug when she saw Olive's noodle hair glistening in the kitchen light. "Well," Mom said slowly, "that's certainly... interesting!"
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Olive running into a bright kitchen with spaghetti hair flowing behind her, while her surprised mother holds a coffee mug with wide eyes and a gentle smile

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Moving very fast because you're excited or in a hurry.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Moving quickly with energy and urgency, usually because something important or exciting is happening.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The kids came rushing inside when it started to rain.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Moving slowly back and forth like grass in the wind.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Rocking or moving gently from side to side in a smooth, rhythmic motion like a tree in the breeze.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The flowers were swaying in the gentle summer breeze.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Shining wetly like something covered with water drops.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Sparkling with moisture or oil, reflecting light in a way that looks shiny and slightly wet.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The morning dew was glistening on the spider's web.

pages.label: Page 6
pages.text: At school, everyone stared and giggled when Olive walked into the classroom. The spaghetti strands kept getting tangled in her backpack straps and drooped into her art supplies. During lunch, three hungry birds tried to nibble on her hair through the window!
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Olive in a colorful classroom looking embarrassed while classmates point and giggle, with spaghetti hair tangled in her backpack, and small birds pecking at the window behind her

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Laughed in a quiet, silly way that sounds light and happy.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Made soft, quiet laughing sounds that show you think something is funny or amusing in a gentle way.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The baby giggled when her mom made funny faces.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Twisted together in a messy way that's hard to undo.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Twisted and knotted together so badly that it's difficult to separate or straighten out the pieces.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: My fishing line got tangled in the tree branches.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Hung down sadly like a wilted flower.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Sagged or bent downward because of weight, tiredness, or sadness, like a plant without water.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The dog's ears drooped when it got in trouble.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Take tiny bites of food with small teeth or beak.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Eat something by taking very small, gentle bites, the way mice or birds eat their food.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The rabbit likes to nibble on fresh carrots.

pages.label: Page 7
pages.text: By afternoon, Olive felt terrible. Her spaghetti hair was sticky and heavy, and she couldn't concentrate on her math problems. "I should have been happy with my regular hair," she thought sadly, trying to untangle a particularly stubborn noodle from her pencil.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Olive sitting at her school desk looking sad and frustrated, with messy spaghetti hair tangled around a pencil, math worksheet in front of her, afternoon sunlight casting long shadows

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Something that sticks to your fingers when you touch it.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Having a surface that feels gooey and clings to things when you touch it, like honey or glue.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: My hands were sticky after eating the candy apple.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Think very hard about one thing without getting distracted.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Focus all your attention and mental energy on one task without letting other things interrupt your thoughts.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: It's hard to concentrate on homework when the TV is loud.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Something that won't move or change even when you try.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Refusing to budge, change, or cooperate no matter how much effort you put into making it happen.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The stubborn jar lid wouldn't open no matter how hard we tried.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Carefully separate things that are twisted together into knots.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Work patiently to undo knots and twisted parts so that something becomes straight and organized again.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: Dad helped me untangle my kite string from the fence.

pages.label: Page 8
pages.text: During recess, kind Maya approached Olive with a warm smile. "Your hair is definitely unique," Maya said gently, "but you seem really unhappy. What made you special was never just your hair, you know. You're creative, funny, and the best friend anyone could ask for."
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Maya with her curly red hair putting a comforting hand on Olive's shoulder on the playground, both girls sitting on a colorful bench with other children playing in the background

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Time at school when kids go outside to play.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: A break during the school day when children go outside or to a special area to play and have fun.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: We played tag during recess on the playground.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Came closer to someone in a friendly way.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Walked toward someone or moved nearer to them, usually to talk or offer help in a gentle way.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The friendly dog approached us wagging its tail.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Special and different from everything else in the world.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: One of a kind and unlike anything else, making it special because there's nothing exactly like it anywhere.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: Every snowflake has a unique pattern that's different from all others.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Good at thinking of new ideas and making interesting things.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Having the ability to imagine new things and create original art, stories, or solutions that others haven't thought of.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: She's very creative and makes beautiful sculptures from clay.

pages.label: Page 9
pages.text: Olive looked up at Maya with tears sparkling in her eyes. "You really think so?" she asked hopefully. Maya nodded enthusiastically. "Absolutely! Remember when you helped me with my science project? And you always make the funniest jokes during lunch. That's what makes you special!"
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Close-up of Olive and Maya sitting together, Olive with tears of hope in her eyes and spaghetti hair, Maya smiling encouragingly, warm golden afternoon light surrounding them

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Shining brightly with tiny flashes of light like diamonds.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Glittering with many small points of light that twinkle and dance, creating a magical, bright appearance.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The snow was sparkling in the bright sunlight.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: In a way that shows you really want something good to happen.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: With a feeling of optimism and expectation that something positive will occur, showing you believe in good outcomes.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: She looked hopefully at the cloudy sky, wishing for sunshine.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: With lots of excitement and energy about something you really like.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: In a way that shows great excitement, energy, and positive feelings about something you care about deeply.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The crowd cheered enthusiastically when their team scored.

pages.label: Page 10
pages.text: That night, Olive looked at the same twinkling star and made a new wish. "I wish to have my regular hair back," she said confidently. "I'm already interesting just the way I am!" She smiled at her reflection, spaghetti hair and all, feeling proud of who she was inside.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Olive at her bedroom window again, but this time standing tall and confident with a genuine smile, looking at the same bright star, her spaghetti hair catching the moonlight

vocabWords.definition_4_7: In a way that shows you believe in yourself and feel sure.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: With strong belief in yourself and your abilities, showing that you feel certain and self-assured about what you're doing.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: She walked confidently onto the stage to give her speech.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: What you see when you look in a mirror.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: The image of yourself that appears when you look into a mirror or shiny surface like water or glass.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The lake was so still it showed a perfect reflection of the mountains.

pages.label: Page 11
pages.text: The next morning, Olive woke up and felt her familiar brown hair tickling her shoulders. She ran to the mirror and grinned at her reflection. Her plain hair had never looked more beautiful! Meatball purred approvingly from his sunny spot on the windowsill.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Olive in her bathroom mirror again, but this time smiling brightly at her reflection with her regular brown hair, looking confident and happy, with Meatball the cat stretching in a sunny window

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Something you know well because you've seen it many times before.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Easy to recognize because you've experienced it often, making it feel comfortable and known to you.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The familiar smell of cookies made him think of grandma's house.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Smiled in a big, happy way that shows your teeth.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Made a wide, joyful smile that spreads across your whole face, showing you feel really pleased and happy.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: He grinned when he saw his birthday present.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: In a way that shows you think something is good and right.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: With satisfaction and agreement, showing that you like what you see and think it's the right thing to do.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: Mom nodded approvingly when she saw my clean room.

comprehensionQuestions.question: What happened to Olive's hair after she made her first wish?

options.text: It turned into spaghetti noodles
It became curly and red like Maya's
It grew purple streaks like Tommy's

comprehensionQuestions.question: Why do you think Olive wanted her regular hair back?

options.text: The spaghetti hair was messy and caused problems
She wanted to try a different magical hair style
Her mom told her to change it back

comprehensionQuestions.question: How do you feel special just the way you are?

options.text: I'm good at helping my friends
I have my own unique talents and interests
I make people laugh with my silly jokes

predictionPause.question: Olive is feeling really unhappy with her spaghetti hair. What do you think she'll do next?

options.text: Ask her friend Maya for advice
Make another wish to change her hair again
Try to wash the spaghetti out with shampoo

s4.title: The Talking Shoe
s4.obstacle: his left shoe starts talking and won't stop giving bad advice

pages.label: Page 1
pages.text: Max loved his bright red sneakers with their squeaky white laces. Every morning, he would carefully tie them tight and race outside to play. The shoes made the most wonderful slapping sounds on the sidewalk as he ran.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: A cheerful boy with curly brown hair sitting on his bed, tying bright red sneakers with white laces. Morning sunlight streams through the window, and toys are scattered around his colorful bedroom.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Special shoes you wear to run and play outside.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Athletic shoes made for running and playing that often have rubber soles and laces.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: Sarah put on her sneakers before going to the playground.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Making a high, sharp sound when you touch or move it.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Creating a high-pitched noise that happens when something rubs or moves against another surface.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The squeaky door needed oil to stop making noise.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Making a loud smacking sound when hitting against something flat.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Creating a sharp hitting sound when one surface strikes against another with force.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The waves were slapping against the side of the boat.

pages.label: Page 2
pages.text: One Tuesday morning, as Max finished his breakfast of crunchy cereal, something very strange happened. "Psst! Hey Max!" came a tiny, squeaky voice. Max looked around the kitchen, but only saw Mom washing dishes.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Max sitting at a sunny kitchen table with a bowl of colorful cereal, looking puzzled. His mom stands at the sink with soapy bubbles floating around. Max's red shoes are visible under the table.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Making a crispy sound when you bite or chew it.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Having a texture that creates a crisp, crackling sound when eaten or stepped on.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The crunchy leaves made noise under my feet in autumn.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Something that seems weird or different from what you expect.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Unusual or odd in a way that makes you curious or confused about what's happening.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The strange sound in the attic made everyone wonder what was up there.

pages.label: Page 3
pages.text: "Down here, silly!" squeaked the voice again. Max nearly choked on his orange juice when he looked down. His left shoe was wiggling its tongue at him! "Surprise! I'm your talking shoe, and do I have some great ideas for you!"
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Max looking down in amazement at his left red sneaker, which has a mischievous expression with its tongue (shoe tongue) wiggling. Orange juice spills slightly from his glass, and his eyes are wide with surprise.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: When food or drink goes down the wrong way and makes you cough.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Having trouble breathing or swallowing because something blocks your throat, making you gasp or cough.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: He choked on his water when he heard the funny joke.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Moving back and forth in small, quick movements.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Making small, continuous movements from side to side or up and down in a playful way.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The puppy was wiggling its tail because it was so excited.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: The part inside your mouth that helps you taste and talk.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: The muscular organ in your mouth used for tasting food, speaking, and swallowing.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: She stuck out her tongue to catch the falling snowflake.

pages.label: Page 4
pages.text: At school, the talking shoe whispered constantly. "Tell Mrs. Garcia you forgot your homework because aliens stole it!" it suggested during reading time. The shoe's voice tickled Max's foot as it spoke, making him squirm in his chair.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Max sitting at a school desk looking uncomfortable, with other children reading books around him. His left shoe glows slightly with a sneaky expression, while Mrs. Garcia, a kind teacher, stands at the front of the classroom.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Talking very quietly so only certain people can hear you.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Speaking in a very soft, quiet voice so that others nearby cannot easily hear what you're saying.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: She whispered the secret so her little brother wouldn't hear.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Happening all the time without stopping or taking a break.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Continuing to happen over and over again without pause, like something that never ends.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The baby was constantly crying because she was hungry.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Moving around in your seat because you feel uncomfortable.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Twisting and moving restlessly because you feel awkward, uncomfortable, or can't sit still.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The child began to squirm during the long movie.

pages.label: Page 5
pages.text: Max knew that wasn't true, but the shoe kept talking. "Come on, it'll be funny! Or say a giant hamster ate it!" Max felt his cheeks turn as red as his sneakers. When Mrs. Garcia asked about his homework, he told the truth instead.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Max with flushed red cheeks standing beside Mrs. Garcia's desk, while his classmates watch. His left shoe looks disappointed, and Mrs. Garcia smiles kindly as Max speaks honestly.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: The soft parts of your face on both sides of your nose.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: The rounded areas of your face below your eyes that can turn red when you're embarrassed.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: Grandma always pinches my cheeks when she visits.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: A small, furry animal that people keep as a pet.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: A tiny rodent with soft fur and cheek pouches that many families keep as a household pet.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The hamster ran on its wheel all night long.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Doing one thing when you could have done something else.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Choosing to do something different from what was first planned or suggested by someone else.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: Instead of watching TV, we decided to go outside and play.

pages.label: Page 6
pages.text: At lunch, the shoe had more terrible ideas. "Drop your sandwich and blame Tommy!" it squeaked. Max's peanut butter sandwich smelled delicious, and Tommy was being perfectly nice. Max ignored his shoe and ate his lunch peacefully.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Max sitting in a bright cafeteria with Tommy and other friends, holding a peanut butter sandwich. His left shoe has an annoyed expression while the children chat happily around a lunch table.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Very bad ideas that would cause problems or hurt feelings.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Extremely poor or harmful suggestions that could lead to trouble or make others feel upset.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: It was a terrible idea to leave the gate open during the storm.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Food that tastes really, really good when you eat it.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Having a wonderful flavor that makes you want to eat more because it tastes so good.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The delicious cookies made the whole kitchen smell amazing.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Doing something in a calm, quiet way without any fighting.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Acting in a gentle, calm manner without causing trouble or getting into arguments with others.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The cats slept peacefully in the sunny window.

pages.label: Page 7
pages.text: During recess, the shoe became even sillier. "Tell everyone you can fly! Jump off the monkey bars!" Max loved the warm sunshine and didn't want to ruin playtime with fibs. His friends were having too much fun building a fort in the sandbox.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Max standing near playground monkey bars with the golden sun shining overhead. His friends are building a sand fort nearby, and his left shoe looks frustrated while making grand gestures.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: The fun time at school when you go outside to play.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: A break during the school day when children go outside or to the gym to play and relax.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: During recess, the children played tag on the playground.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: The bright, warm light that comes from the sun.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: The golden light and heat that the sun sends down to earth on clear, bright days.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The sunshine made the flowers in the garden grow tall and colorful.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Small lies that aren't true but aren't meant to be mean.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Minor untruths or made-up stories that people sometimes tell, usually not to hurt others badly.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: Mom knew he was telling fibs about brushing his teeth.

pages.label: Page 8
pages.text: By afternoon, Max felt tired from all the shoe's chatter. His foot was sore from the constant wiggling, and the silly suggestions were giving him a headache. "Please stop," Max whispered. "Your ideas aren't helping me at all!"
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Max looking weary, rubbing his temples with both hands. His left shoe appears deflated and slightly sad. The classroom behind him shows afternoon light streaming through windows.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Talking quickly and a lot about many different things.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Continuous, rapid talking that goes on and on, often about unimportant or random topics.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The birds' chatter woke me up early in the morning.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Something that keeps happening and never stops or takes a break.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Continuing without pause or interruption, happening over and over again in the same way.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The constant rain made it impossible to have a picnic outside.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: When your head hurts and feels sore or painful.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: A painful feeling in your head that can happen when you're tired, stressed, or sick.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: Reading in dim light gave her a terrible headache.

pages.label: Page 9
pages.text: The shoe's voice grew quieter. "But... but I thought I was being helpful! Don't you want to have adventures and surprises?" Max knelt down and gently patted his shoe. "Real adventures come from being honest and kind," he said softly.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Max kneeling down and gently patting his left shoe, which now looks sad but thoughtful. The setting is peaceful, with soft afternoon light, and Max's expression is patient and kind.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Exciting trips or experiences where new and fun things happen.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Thrilling experiences or journeys that are full of interesting discoveries and unexpected events.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The family went on adventures to explore caves and mountains.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Things that happen when you don't expect them to happen.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Unexpected events or gifts that catch you off guard and can make you feel excited or amazed.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: Birthday parties are full of surprises like games and presents.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Doing something in a soft, careful way that doesn't hurt.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Acting with kindness and care, using a light touch that shows you don't want to cause harm.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: She gently petted the kitten so it wouldn't get scared.

pages.label: Page 10
pages.text: From that day on, the shoe still talked, but now it whispered good ideas. "Help carry those books!" or "Share your crayons!" Max discovered that telling the truth and being helpful led to the very best adventures of all. His left shoe learned to be his very best friend.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Max happily helping classmates carry books and sharing colorful crayons. His left shoe has a warm, friendly smile, and children around him are smiling too. The scene is bright and joyful with rainbow-colored crayons scattered on the table.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Found out something new that you didn't know before.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Learned or found something important through experience that you hadn't understood or noticed earlier.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The scientist discovered a new type of butterfly in the rainforest.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Colorful sticks made of wax that you use for drawing pictures.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Waxy coloring tools that come in many bright colors and are used for drawing and coloring pictures.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The art teacher gave each student a box of crayons for the project.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Being kind by doing things that make life easier for others.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Providing assistance or support to others in a way that makes their tasks easier or their day better.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The helpful librarian showed us where to find books about dinosaurs.

comprehensionQuestions.question: What did Max's talking shoe want him to tell Mrs. Garcia about his homework?

options.text: That aliens stole it
That he finished it early
That he left it at home

comprehensionQuestions.question: Why do you think Max's cheeks turned red when the shoe gave him bad ideas?

options.text: He was embarrassed by the silly suggestions
He was angry at his teacher
He was excited about the ideas

comprehensionQuestions.question: How do you feel when someone gives you advice that might get you in trouble?

options.text: Worried about making the right choice
Confused about what to do
Proud that I can think for myself

predictionPause.question: Max is tired of his shoe's bad ideas and asks it to stop. What do you think the shoe will do next?

options.text: The shoe will get quiet and stop talking
The shoe will start giving good advice instead
The shoe will get even sillier and louder

s5.title: Super Slow Sloth
s5.obstacle: he's trying to win a race but he's incredibly slow

pages.label: Page 1
pages.text: Stanley is a sloth. He hangs upside down in his green tree. Stanley moves very, very slowly.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: A cute sloth with big sleepy eyes hanging upside down from a leafy green branch, surrounded by tropical leaves and colorful flowers

vocabWords.definition_2_4: A slow animal that lives in trees.
vocabWords.definition_4_7: A furry animal that moves very slowly and hangs from tree branches.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: A tropical mammal known for moving extremely slowly and spending most of its life hanging upside down in trees.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The sloth at the zoo barely moved during our visit.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: To hold onto something from up high without falling down.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: To suspend oneself or something else from a higher point using grip or attachment.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The coat hangs on the hook by the door.

vocabWords.definition_2_4: The top part is down low.
vocabWords.definition_4_7: When the top of something is at the bottom instead.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: In a position where what is normally at the top is positioned at the bottom.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: She turned the picture upside down to confuse her brother.

pages.label: Page 2
pages.text: Today is the big jungle race! All the animals line up. Rabbit runs fast. Monkey swings fast. Stanley moves slow.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Various jungle animals lined up at a colorful starting line with a banner, including a speedy rabbit, energetic monkey, and Stanley the sloth moving very slowly to join them

vocabWords.definition_2_4: A place with lots of big trees and animals.
vocabWords.definition_4_7: A thick forest where many wild animals live together.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: A dense tropical forest filled with tall trees, vines, and many different kinds of wild animals.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: We heard monkey sounds coming from the deep jungle.

vocabWords.definition_2_4: Running to see who is fastest.
vocabWords.definition_4_7: A contest to see who can get somewhere first.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: A competition where participants try to reach a destination faster than their opponents.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The children had a race to the playground.

vocabWords.definition_2_4: Moves back and forth through the air.
vocabWords.definition_4_7: To move through the air by holding onto something.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: To move in a smooth arc through space while gripping or hanging from a support.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The girl swings on the rope over the creek.

pages.label: Page 3
pages.text: BEEP! The race starts! Rabbit hops away quickly. Monkey swings through trees. Stanley takes one tiny step forward.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Dynamic scene showing rabbit bounding away in a cloud of dust, monkey swinging on vines, while Stanley has barely moved from the starting line

vocabWords.definition_2_4: A loud sound that tells you something.
vocabWords.definition_4_7: A sharp noise that signals when something should start.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: A brief electronic sound used as a signal or alert to indicate an event.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The microwave makes a beep when the food is ready.

vocabWords.definition_2_4: Jumps up and down to move.
vocabWords.definition_4_7: To move forward by jumping with both feet together.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: To propel oneself forward using short bouncing jumps, typically on both feet simultaneously.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The bunny hops across the garden to find carrots.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Moving or doing something in a very fast way.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: At a rapid pace or speed, completing an action in a short amount of time.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: She quickly ran inside when it started raining.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Moving in the direction you are facing or going.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: In the direction that is ahead of you or toward your intended destination.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The soldier marched forward toward the castle.

pages.label: Page 4
pages.text: Stanley keeps going. Step... step... step. The other animals are far away now. But Stanley does not stop.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Stanley taking slow, determined steps along a winding jungle path, with tiny clouds of dust around his feet, other animals visible as dots in the distance

vocabWords.definition_4_7: To continue doing the same thing without stopping.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: To maintain or continue an action or state without interruption or change.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: She keeps practicing piano every day after school.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Moving one foot forward to walk somewhere.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: A single movement of lifting one foot and placing it down in a different location.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: Take one step up onto the curb.

pages.label: Page 5
pages.text: The sun gets hot. Stanley feels tired. His furry body is warm. But he remembers: Never give up!
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Stanley wiping sweat from his fuzzy forehead under a bright yellow sun, looking determined with little beads of sweat on his gray fur

vocabWords.definition_2_4: When your body wants to rest.
vocabWords.definition_4_7: Feeling like you need to sleep or take a break.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Experiencing physical or mental fatigue that makes you want to rest or sleep.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: After playing all day, the puppy felt very tired.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Having lots of soft hair or fur covering the body.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Covered with a thick layer of soft hair or fur that provides warmth and protection.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The furry kitten loves to be petted.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: To think about something that happened or you learned before.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: To bring back into your mind something from the past that you experienced or learned.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: She always remembers to brush her teeth before bed.

pages.label: Page 6
pages.text: Step... step... step. Stanley sees Rabbit sleeping under a shady tree. Stanley walks right past him. Step... step... step.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Rabbit fast asleep on his back under a tree with 'Z's floating above him, while Stanley slowly walks by in the background

vocabWords.definition_4_7: When someone is resting quietly with their eyes closed.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: The natural state of rest where the body and mind are inactive and unconscious.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The baby is sleeping peacefully in the crib.

vocabWords.definition_2_4: A place where the sun can't reach.
vocabWords.definition_4_7: A cool place that is protected from bright sunlight.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: An area that is sheltered from direct sunlight, typically by trees or other objects.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: We sat in the shady spot under the umbrella.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Moving by something or someone and continuing on.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Moving beyond a particular point, person, or object while continuing in the same direction.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The bus drove past our house this morning.

pages.label: Page 7
pages.text: Stanley keeps walking. Now he sees Monkey! Monkey stopped to eat yummy bananas. Stanley walks right past Monkey too.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Monkey sitting surrounded by banana peels, stuffing his face with bananas, while Stanley continues his steady pace in the background

vocabWords.definition_2_4: Moving by putting one foot in front of the other.
vocabWords.definition_4_7: Moving forward by taking steps with your feet.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: The action of moving at a regular pace by lifting and setting down each foot in turn.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: We enjoy walking through the park on sunny days.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: When someone or something was moving but then quit moving.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Ceased movement or activity, coming to a halt from a previous state of motion.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The car stopped at the red traffic light.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Something that tastes so good you want more of it.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Having a delicious taste that is very pleasant and appealing to eat.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The yummy cookies smelled amazing coming out of the oven.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Long yellow fruits that are sweet and soft inside.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Curved tropical fruits with yellow peels and sweet, creamy flesh that many animals enjoy eating.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: Mom packed bananas in my lunch box for a healthy snack.

pages.label: Page 8
pages.text: The finish line is close! Stanley can see the bright red ribbon. His friends cheer: 'Go Stanley! Go!'
pages.scene: illustration prompt: A colorful finish line with red ribbon ahead, jungle animals cheering and waving from the sidelines with paws and wings raised

vocabWords.definition_4_7: The place or time when something comes to an end.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: The final point or conclusion of an activity, race, or task that marks its completion.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: We celebrated when she reached the finish of her puzzle.

vocabWords.definition_2_4: Very easy to see because of lots of light.
vocabWords.definition_4_7: Something that shines with a lot of light or color.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Emitting or reflecting a strong amount of light, making colors appear vivid and clearly visible.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The bright stars twinkled in the dark night sky.

vocabWords.definition_2_4: A long piece of pretty cloth.
vocabWords.definition_4_7: A long narrow piece of fabric used for decoration.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: A strip of fine material used for tying, decorating, or marking special occasions and achievements.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: She tied a pink ribbon around the gift box.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: To shout encouragement or praise for someone you support.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: To express enthusiastic approval and support through loud vocal encouragement and celebration.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The fans cheer loudly when their team scores a goal.

pages.label: Page 9
pages.text: Stanley crosses the finish line first! Confetti falls like colorful rain. Everyone claps and cheers for Super Slow Sloth!
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Stanley breaking through the finish line ribbon with colorful confetti falling all around him, wearing a big smile as jungle animals celebrate

vocabWords.definition_2_4: Goes from one side to the other side.
vocabWords.definition_4_7: To move from one side of something to the other side.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: To pass over or through from one side of a space, line, or boundary to the opposite side.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: She crosses the street carefully at the crosswalk.

vocabWords.definition_2_4: Tiny pieces of paper that fall for parties.
vocabWords.definition_4_7: Small colorful pieces of paper thrown during celebrations.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Tiny bits of colored paper or material scattered in the air during festive occasions and celebrations.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: Golden confetti filled the air at the New Year's party.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Something that has many bright and pretty colors on it.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Displaying a variety of vivid hues and shades that create a bright and attractive appearance.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The colorful butterfly landed gently on the flower.

vocabWords.definition_2_4: Happy shouts from lots of people.
vocabWords.definition_4_7: Loud happy sounds people make when they are excited.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Enthusiastic vocal expressions of approval, encouragement, or celebration from a group of people.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The crowd's cheers echoed through the stadium.

pages.label: Page 10
pages.text: Stanley gets a shiny gold medal! He learned something special: Going slow is okay. Never giving up is what matters!
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Stanley proudly wearing a large gold medal around his neck, surrounded by all his jungle friends who are smiling and celebrating with him

vocabWords.definition_2_4: Bright and sparkly like it glows.
vocabWords.definition_4_7: Something that reflects light and looks bright and glossy.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Having a smooth, polished surface that reflects light and appears bright and lustrous.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The shiny coin gleamed in the sunlight on the sidewalk.

vocabWords.definition_2_4: A special prize you wear for doing great.
vocabWords.definition_4_7: A round metal prize given to someone who wins something.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: A circular piece of metal awarded as a prize to recognize achievement, victory, or honor.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The brave firefighter received a medal for helping save lives.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Discovered or understood something you didn't know before.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Gained knowledge or understanding through experience, study, or being taught something new.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: She learned how to ride a bike during summer vacation.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Something that is important and makes a big difference.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Has significance or importance, making a meaningful difference in a situation or outcome.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: Being kind to others really matters in making friends.

s6.title: The Upside Down House
s6.obstacle: her whole house flipped upside down overnight

pages.label: Page 1
pages.text: Kira loved her perfectly organized bedroom with its neat rows of books and tidy toy boxes. Every morning, she followed the same routine: brush teeth, comb hair, eat breakfast at the kitchen table. She liked knowing exactly where everything belonged and exactly what came next. Change made her feel wobbly inside, like standing on a rocking boat.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: A neat, organized bedroom with a young girl with dark curly hair looking content, surrounded by perfectly arranged books, toys in labeled boxes, and everything in its proper place

vocabWords.definition_4_7: When things are put in their right places and arranged neatly.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Having everything carefully arranged in proper order, like books lined up by height or toys sorted by color.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The teacher kept her classroom organized with labeled bins for supplies.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: The same things you do in the same order every day.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: A regular pattern of activities that you follow daily, like a schedule that becomes automatic and comfortable.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: My bedtime routine includes taking a bath, reading a story, and brushing my teeth.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Feeling shaky and unsteady, like you might fall over.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Unstable and unbalanced, moving back and forth in an uncertain way that makes you feel dizzy or nervous.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The baby took wobbly steps when learning to walk.

pages.label: Page 2
pages.text: But when Kira woke up Tuesday morning, something was terribly wrong. Her pillow was on the floor, her blanket was floating above her, and her stuffed elephant Peanuts was stuck to the ceiling! She blinked hard three times, but everything was still topsy-turvy. The whole house had somehow flipped completely upside down overnight.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Kira in pajamas looking shocked and confused, standing on what used to be the ceiling, with furniture, books, and toys scattered around in impossible positions, her stuffed elephant on what's now the floor above her

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Moving in the air without touching the ground or floor.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Staying up in the air without any support, drifting gently like a balloon or a feather on the wind.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The soap bubbles were floating through the garden on the breeze.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Everything mixed up and upside down in a confusing way.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Completely disordered and chaotic, with everything turned around backward or upside down from how it should be.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: After the tornado, the playground was topsy-turvy with swings tangled and slides flipped over.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Turned completely over to the opposite side or direction.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Rotated or turned around completely, like doing a somersault or turning a pancake over in a pan.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The gymnast flipped through the air and landed perfectly on her feet.

pages.label: Page 3
pages.text: "Mom! Dad!" Kira called, carefully stepping around her lamp that was now growing down from above like a strange metal tree. Her parents appeared in the doorway, walking on the ceiling as if it were perfectly normal. "Good morning, sweetie!" Mom said cheerfully. "Ready for breakfast?" Kira's stomach did a little flip-flop of worry.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Kira's parents walking casually upside down in the doorway, smiling normally while everything around them is flipped, with Kira looking uncertain and worried

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Doing something slowly and safely to avoid making mistakes or getting hurt.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Moving or acting with great attention and caution, taking time to think about each step to prevent accidents or problems.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: She carefully carried the birthday cake so it wouldn't fall.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Showed up or came into sight where you could see them.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Became visible suddenly or gradually, like when someone steps out from behind a curtain or emerges from around a corner.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: A rainbow appeared in the sky after the storm passed.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Speaking or acting in a happy, positive way with a smile.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Doing something with joy and enthusiasm, showing happiness through your voice, face, and actions that makes others feel good too.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The mail carrier cheerfully waved to everyone on his route.

pages.label: Page 4
pages.text: In the upside-down kitchen, cereal boxes hung from the floor like colorful stalactites, and milk poured upward in graceful white ribbons. Dad was somehow cooking pancakes on a stove that defied all logic. "I don't know how to eat breakfast on the ceiling," Kira said, her voice small and uncertain. Everything felt wrong and backwards.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: A topsy-turvy kitchen with Dad cooking on an upside-down stove, cereal boxes hanging down, milk flowing upward, and Kira standing uncertainly while her parents act normal

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Rock formations that hang down from cave ceilings like icicles.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Long, pointed rock formations that grow downward from cave ceilings over thousands of years, created by dripping mineral-rich water.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The cave explorer used a flashlight to see the stalactites hanging above her head.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Moving in a smooth, beautiful way that looks easy and elegant.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Flowing with natural beauty and smoothness, like a dancer or swan, making difficult movements look effortless and lovely.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The graceful ballerina leaped across the stage like she was flying.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Went against the rules or did something that shouldn't be possible.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Challenged or resisted normal laws or expectations, doing something that seems impossible or breaks the usual way things work.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The magician defied gravity by making the coins float in mid-air.

pages.label: Page 5
pages.text: "Maybe we could have a picnic breakfast instead?" Mom suggested, spreading a checkered blanket in the middle of the floor-ceiling. Kira had never eaten breakfast on a blanket before, but her rumbling tummy convinced her to try. The pancakes tasted just as fluffy and sweet, even when eaten sitting cross-legged instead of at the proper table.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: The family sitting cross-legged on a checkered picnic blanket in the middle of the upside-down kitchen, eating pancakes together with Kira starting to look a little more relaxed

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Eating a meal outside on a blanket, usually on the ground.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: An outdoor meal where people sit on blankets or at tables in parks, yards, or other natural places to enjoy food together.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: We had a picnic by the lake with sandwiches and lemonade.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Having a pattern of squares in two different colors, like a checkerboard.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Decorated with alternating squares of different colors arranged in rows, creating a pattern like you see on racing flags or chess boards.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The chef wore a checkered hat with black and white squares.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Making low, rolling sounds like thunder or a hungry stomach.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Producing deep, continuous rolling sounds that you can often feel as well as hear, like distant thunder or an empty belly.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The rumbling truck drove past our house early in the morning.

pages.label: Page 6
pages.text: After breakfast, Kira discovered that sliding down the hallway on her sock feet was actually quite fun when gravity worked sideways. The smooth wooden floor became a perfect slide, and she giggled as she whooshed past upside-down family photos. Maybe this topsy-turvy world wasn't so scary after all – it was like living inside a playground.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Kira sliding joyfully down the hallway in her socks, arms outstretched for balance, with upside-down family photos on the walls and a big smile spreading across her face

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Moving smoothly across a surface without lifting your feet up much.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Gliding across a smooth surface with continuous contact, like ice skating or going down a playground slide without bouncing or jumping.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The kids were sliding across the frozen pond in their boots.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: The force that pulls things down toward the ground when you drop them.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: The invisible force that pulls all objects toward the earth, making things fall down instead of floating away into space.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: Gravity made the ball roll down the hill instead of up.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Moved very fast making a rushing sound through the air.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Traveled quickly with a swooshing sound, like wind blowing past your ears or a rocket shooting through the sky.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The race car whooshed past the cheering crowd at incredible speed.

pages.label: Page 7
pages.text: In the living room, Kira discovered she could walk on the furniture like stepping stones across a river. She hopped from the couch to the coffee table to Dad's favorite armchair, her arms spread wide for balance. "Look, I'm an acrobat!" she laughed, feeling proud and brave. Who knew upside-down could be so adventurous?
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Kira balancing confidently on furniture arranged like stepping stones, arms outstretched, with a proud smile and newfound confidence, furniture positioned at interesting angles

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Things like chairs, tables, and beds that you put in rooms.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: The movable objects in a home or building, like sofas, desks, and dressers, that make rooms comfortable and useful for living.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: We rearranged the furniture in the living room to make more space for dancing.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Flat rocks you step on to cross a river or stream.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Large, flat stones placed in water or gardens that you can step on one by one to cross safely without getting wet.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: We used stepping stones to cross the creek without falling in the water.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: A person who does flips, jumps, and balancing tricks for shows.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: A skilled performer who entertains audiences by doing amazing physical feats like somersaults, tightrope walking, and gravity-defying jumps.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The circus acrobat swung from trapeze to trapeze high above the crowd.

pages.label: Page 8
pages.text: When it was time for school, Kira worried about her backpack, her lunch, and her perfectly sharpened pencils. But then she realized she could try something completely new – she drew pictures with crayons that floated in the air and read books while lying on her back watching the words dance above her. Learning felt magical instead of ordinary.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Kira lying on her back in the upside-down house, reaching up toward floating crayons and books, with colorful drawings suspended in mid-air around her, looking wonder-struck

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Made pointed and ready to write or draw with clearly.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Ground to a fine point so the tip is sharp and precise, making clean marks when you write or draw with it.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The artist sharpened her colored pencils before starting the detailed drawing.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Colorful wax sticks used for drawing and coloring pictures.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Waxy colored drawing tools that come in many different colors and are perfect for making bright, bold artwork on paper.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The little boy used red and blue crayons to draw a picture of his house.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Special and wonderful, like something from a fairy tale with powers.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Enchanting and mysterious in a way that seems impossible, filled with wonder like the amazing things wizards do in stories.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The sunset painted the sky in magical colors of pink, orange, and purple.

pages.label: Page 9
pages.text: That evening, as Kira brushed her teeth with toothpaste that squirted in spirals and combed her hair while standing in a field of floating bobby pins, she realized something wonderful. She had spent the whole day trying new things and hadn't felt scared once. In fact, she felt excited and proud of herself.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Kira in the upside-down bathroom, surrounded by floating bobby pins like tiny stars, brushing her teeth with spiraling toothpaste, looking confident and happy with her reflection

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Curved lines that go around and around in circles, getting bigger or smaller.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Continuous curves that wind around a center point in ever-widening or tightening circles, like a seashell or tornado shape.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The dancer spun in spirals across the stage, her dress flowing behind her.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Doing something on purpose, not by accident or mistake.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Acting with careful intention and thought, making a conscious choice to do something specific rather than letting it happen by chance.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: She deliberately chose the red shoes instead of the black ones for the special occasion.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Feeling very happy and eager about something that's happening or will happen.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Filled with enthusiastic anticipation and joy, feeling energized and thrilled about an event, activity, or possibility that makes your heart beat faster.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The children were excited about going to the amusement park on Saturday.

pages.label: Page 10
pages.text: The next morning, Kira woke up in her perfectly normal, right-side-up bedroom. Everything was back in its proper place, but something inside her had changed forever. She looked at her neat rows of books and smiled, then deliberately messed up her hair and wore mismatched socks to breakfast. "What's different about you today?" Mom asked, noticing Kira's extra sparkle. "I learned that upside-down can be right-side-up wonderful," Kira grinned.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Kira in her normal bedroom but with a mischievous smile, deliberately wearing mismatched socks and tousled hair, looking confident and ready for new adventures, sunlight streaming through the window

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Two things that don't go together or look the same.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Items that don't match or coordinate with each other, like wearing different colored socks or mixing patterns that clash instead of complement.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: He wore mismatched mittens - one red and one blue - because he couldn't find the matching pairs.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: A bright, shiny quality that makes something look happy or special.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: A bright, glittering quality that catches light and attention, often describing someone's eyes when they're happy or something that shines with tiny flashes of light.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The fresh snow had a sparkle in the morning sunlight that made everything look like a winter wonderland.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Smiled really big in a happy, excited way showing your teeth.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Smiled widely with great joy or satisfaction, showing teeth in an expression that reveals genuine happiness, pride, or mischievous delight.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The little girl grinned proudly when her grandmother praised her artwork.

comprehensionQuestions.question: What made Kira feel comfortable and safe at the beginning of the story?

options.text: Having everything organized and following the same routine every day
Playing with her stuffed elephant Peanuts all the time
Sleeping in late and eating pancakes for breakfast

comprehensionQuestions.question: Why do you think Kira's parents acted like the upside-down house was perfectly normal?

options.text: They wanted to help Kira feel less scared and worried about the strange situation
They didn't notice that anything was different or upside-down
They were too busy cooking breakfast to pay attention to what was happening

comprehensionQuestions.question: How would you feel if you woke up and everything in your house was upside-down?

options.text: Scared and confused at first, but maybe excited to explore after a while
Worried about how to do normal things like eat breakfast and get ready
Curious and adventurous, ready to discover what fun things I could try

predictionPause.question: What do you think will happen as Kira spends more time in the upside-down house?

options.text: She'll start to enjoy the crazy upside-down world and have fun with it
She'll find a way to flip everything back to normal right away
She'll stay worried and scared and hide in her room until it's fixed

s7.title: Grandpa's Magic Mustache
s7.obstacle: his mustache has a mind of its own and keeps doing silly things

pages.label: Page 1
pages.text: Grandpa Lou had the most magnificent white mustache in the whole neighborhood. It was fluffy like cotton candy and curled at the ends like fancy ribbons. Every morning, he would comb it carefully while humming his favorite tune. But today, something very unusual was about to happen!
pages.scene: illustration prompt: A cheerful elderly man with a spectacular white mustache looking in his bathroom mirror, holding a small mustache comb. The bathroom has warm yellow lighting and colorful towels.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Something that looks really amazing and wonderful.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Something so beautiful and impressive that it makes you stop and admire it with wonder.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The castle on the hill looked magnificent in the sunset.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Hair that grows above a person's upper lip.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Facial hair that grows on the upper lip and can be styled in different shapes.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The circus ringmaster had a long, twisty mustache.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: The area around your house where you live.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: A community of homes and families living close together in the same area.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: We moved to a new neighborhood across town.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Something different from what normally happens every day.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Something strange or unexpected that doesn't happen in the ordinary course of things.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: It was unusual to see snow in the middle of summer.

pages.label: Page 2
pages.text: As Grandpa Lou walked to the kitchen for breakfast, his mustache began to wiggle and dance all by itself! It tickled his nose something fierce. "Achoo!" he sneezed so loudly that the salt and pepper shakers jumped. His mustache seemed to giggle with delight.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Grandpa Lou in his kitchen mid-sneeze, with his animated white mustache wiggling wildly. Salt and pepper shakers are jumping on the counter, and there's a big 'ACHOO!' cloud around his head.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: To move back and forth in a twisty way.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: To move with quick, small movements from side to side like a worm.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The puppy likes to wiggle when he's excited.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: When something lightly touches you and makes you laugh.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: A light, funny feeling on your skin that makes you want to giggle or scratch.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The feather tickled my arm when it fell from the pillow.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Very strong or powerful in a wild way.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Intense and strong, like a wild animal or a powerful storm that demands attention.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The fierce wind knocked over the garbage cans.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: A feeling of being very happy and pleased.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Pure joy and happiness that makes you feel warm and excited inside.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The children squealed with delight when they saw the birthday cake.

pages.label: Page 3
pages.text: At the grocery store, Grandpa's mustache had other ideas about shopping. It reached out and tickled Mrs. Henderson's ear while she picked apples. She turned around, looking puzzled and slightly annoyed. "Sorry!" Grandpa Lou said quickly, trying to hold down his mischievous mustache.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Inside a bright grocery store with apple displays. Grandpa's mustache is stretching toward a surprised elderly lady in a floral dress. Other shoppers are in the background with shopping carts.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: A store where you buy food and things to eat.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: A market that sells food, drinks, and household items that families need every day.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: Mom asked me to help carry the grocery bags from the car.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Feeling confused about what just happened or why.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: A confused feeling when something doesn't make sense and you're trying to figure it out.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: I felt puzzled when I couldn't find my backpack anywhere.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Feeling bothered or upset by something that happened.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: A frustrated feeling when something or someone interrupts you in an unwelcome way.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: She felt annoyed when her little brother kept interrupting her homework.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Acting naughty or playful in a tricky way.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Playfully causing small amounts of trouble or doing things you're not supposed to do.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The mischievous kitten knocked all the pencils off the desk.

pages.label: Page 4
