pages.text: With her heart beating like a tiny drum, Devi slowly unzipped her tent. She grabbed her flashlight and tiptoed toward the sounds. The beam of light danced across the flower petals as she moved closer to solve the mystery once and for all.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Devi tiptoeing through the moonlit garden with a flashlight beam illuminating flowers and plants, looking brave despite her nervousness.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Opened something by pulling the zipper down.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: To open something by sliding the zipper in the opposite direction from when it was closed.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: I unzipped my jacket because it was getting warm.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: A small light you can carry to see in the dark.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: A portable electric light that uses batteries to create a beam for seeing in darkness.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: We used a flashlight to find our way to the bathroom at night.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Walked very quietly on the tips of your toes.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: To walk very quietly and carefully on your toes so you don't make any noise.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: She tiptoed past the sleeping baby's room.

pages.label: Page 8
pages.text: Suddenly, her flashlight revealed the phantom visitor! A gentle brown cow stood among the roses, munching contentedly on the grass. The cow looked up with kind, dark eyes and let out a soft "Moo" as if to say hello.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: A friendly brown cow standing peacefully among rose bushes in the flashlight beam, with Devi looking surprised but delighted. The cow has gentle, kind eyes.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Showed something that was hidden before.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: To make something visible or known that was previously hidden or secret from view.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: Opening the box revealed a beautiful necklace inside.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: In a happy, satisfied way without wanting anything else.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: In a peaceful, satisfied manner, feeling completely happy with what you have or are doing.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The cat purred contentedly in the warm sunshine.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Chewing food in a noisy, crunchy way.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: To chew food steadily and often noisily, especially when eating something crunchy or tough.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: I was munching on carrots during the movie.

pages.label: Page 9
pages.text: Devi couldn't help but giggle! The phantom footprints belonged to Bessie, the neighbor's cow who had wandered through the loose fence boards. "You're not scary at all," Devi whispered, gently patting Bessie's warm, fuzzy head.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Devi laughing and gently petting the friendly cow's head while standing among the garden flowers, both looking happy and peaceful.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: A light, happy laugh that sounds like little bubbles.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: A soft, light laugh that shows you think something is funny or delightful in a gentle way.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The funny joke made all the kids giggle.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Were owned by someone or were part of something.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: To be owned by someone or to be the rightful property or part of something.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The red bike belonged to my older sister.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Walked around slowly without knowing exactly where to go.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: To walk around slowly and aimlessly, often getting lost or going places without a clear plan.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: We wandered through the forest looking at different trees.

pages.label: Page 10
pages.text: The next morning, Devi helped fix the fence and led Bessie home to her grateful owner. Grandma hugged her tight and said, "You were so brave to face the unknown." Devi smiled proudly - she had solved her very first mystery by being courageous!
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Devi walking alongside the cow toward a fence while Grandma watches proudly. The morning sun shines over the peaceful garden scene, and everyone looks happy.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Feeling thankful and happy about something good someone did.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Feeling thankful and appreciative when someone does something kind or helpful for you.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: I was grateful when my friend helped me with homework.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Being very brave and not giving up when things are scary.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Having the strength and bravery to face difficult or frightening situations without giving up.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The courageous firefighter saved the cat from the tree.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Something you don't know about or haven't seen before.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Something that is not familiar or understood, often mysterious and waiting to be discovered.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The dark cave led to an unknown place.

comprehensionQuestions.question: What did Devi find in the garden that made her curious?

options.text: Large muddy footprints
Broken flower stems
Missing strawberries

comprehensionQuestions.question: Why do you think Devi decided to sleep in the garden tent?

options.text: She wanted to see who was making the footprints
She was afraid to sleep inside the house
She wanted to pick strawberries early

comprehensionQuestions.question: How would you feel if you found mysterious footprints in your yard?

options.text: Excited to solve the mystery
A little scared but curious
Worried about what made them

predictionPause.question: What do you think Devi will discover when she shines her flashlight?

options.text: A friendly animal eating in the garden
A scary monster hiding behind the roses
A person sneaking around at night

m3.title: The Whispering Walls
m3.obstacle: the walls of the old library seem to be whispering clues

pages.label: Page 1
pages.text: Cassie pushed open the heavy wooden door of Willowbrook's old library. The hinges creaked a friendly hello as golden sunlight streamed through tall windows, casting dancing shadows on dusty shelves. Mrs. Chen, the librarian, had asked Cassie to help find the missing key to the rare book collection. "It's been lost for twenty years," Mrs. Chen had said with a hopeful smile. Cassie breathed in the sweet smell of old paper and leather bindings, ready for an adventure.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: A curious girl with short brown hair pushing open an ornate wooden door into a grand old library with tall windows, golden sunlight streaming in, and towering bookshelves filled with colorful books

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Metal pieces that let doors swing open and closed.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: The metal joints that connect a door to its frame, allowing it to swing smoothly back and forth.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The old gate's rusty hinges squeaked when I opened it.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Light flowing in like a bright river through windows.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: When light pours continuously through an opening, flowing like water in a steady, bright current.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: Morning light streamed through my bedroom curtains and woke me up.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Making shadows or shapes appear on surfaces with light.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: When light creates shadows or patterns by shining on objects and throwing their shapes onto other surfaces.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The campfire was casting dancing shadows on the tent walls.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: The covers and spines that hold book pages together.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: The outer covering of a book, including the spine and covers that protect and hold all the pages together.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The antique books had beautiful gold decorations on their leather bindings.

pages.label: Page 2
pages.text: As Cassie walked deeper into the library, she noticed something peculiar. The walls seemed to be making soft whispering sounds, like autumn leaves rustling in a gentle breeze. She pressed her ear against the smooth, cool stone wall near the history section. "Psst... look where time began..." the wall seemed to murmur. Cassie's eyes widened with excitement rather than fear. Could the walls really be trying to help her find the lost key?
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Cassie pressing her ear against an old stone wall between tall bookshelves, her expression curious and excited, with swirls of soft blue indicating whispered sounds around her

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Strange or odd in an interesting way that catches attention.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Something unusual or different from normal that makes you curious rather than worried or scared.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: My cat has a peculiar habit of sleeping in the sink.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: A soft crackling sound like leaves moving gently together.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: The gentle, whispery sound made when dry leaves or paper move and rub against each other softly.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: I heard rustling in the bushes where a rabbit was hiding.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: To speak very quietly in a soft, gentle voice.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: A way of speaking in low, soft tones that sound almost like a gentle whisper or hum.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The teacher began to murmur soothing words to calm the frightened child.

pages.label: Page 3
pages.text: Following the first clue, Cassie made her way to the ancient history section. The books here were older than any she'd seen before, with cracked leather covers and faded gold lettering. She ran her fingers along the spines, feeling the bumpy texture of aged leather. Suddenly, another whisper drifted from the wall behind the shelf: "Where stories sleep in forgotten dreams..." Cassie smiled. The walls were definitely trying to help her solve this mystery!
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Cassie examining old books with weathered leather covers in a dimly lit section of the library, her finger tracing along book spines, with warm amber lighting from an old lamp

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Very, very old from long, long ago in time.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Something that existed many, many years ago, often from times when your great-great-grandparents weren't even born yet.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: We visited ancient ruins from a castle built hundreds of years ago.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Having thin lines or splits where something has broken partly.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: When something has developed thin breaks or splits on its surface, usually from being very old or dry.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The old sidewalk was cracked from tree roots growing underneath it.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: The narrow backs of books where the titles are written.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: The edge part of a book that you see when it's sitting on a shelf, usually showing the title and author's name.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: I could read all the book titles by looking at their colorful spines on the shelf.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: How something feels when you touch it with your fingers.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: The way a surface feels to your touch, whether it's smooth, rough, bumpy, soft, or any other feeling.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The tree bark had a rough, bumpy texture that scratched my hand.

pages.label: Page 4
pages.text: The second clue led Cassie to the poetry section, where books of poems and fairy tales lived. She searched high and low, checking behind books and under reading chairs with soft, worn cushions. After an hour of looking, she hadn't found anything. Cassie felt her shoulders droop with disappointment. Maybe this was too hard. Maybe the key would never be found. Just as she was about to give up, she heard another gentle whisper: "Brave hearts never stop trying..."
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Cassie looking tired and slightly discouraged while searching through books in the cozy poetry section, with comfortable reading chairs and soft lighting, but small sparkles suggesting magic still present

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Special writings with rhythm and sometimes rhyming words that sound musical.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: A type of writing that uses rhythm, rhyme, and beautiful language to express feelings or paint pictures with words.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: We read funny poems about animals in our English class today.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Soft, puffy pillows that make chairs more comfortable to sit on.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Soft, padded coverings placed on chairs or benches to make them more comfortable and cozy for sitting.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: Mom put new floral cushions on our kitchen chairs.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: To hang down or sag when feeling tired or sad.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: When something bends or hangs down, often showing tiredness, sadness, or disappointment in body language.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The flowers began to droop because they needed water.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: The sad feeling when something doesn't work out like you hoped.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: The unhappy emotion you feel when something you were hoping for or expecting doesn't happen the way you wanted.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: There was disappointment on his face when the game was canceled due to rain.

pages.label: Page 5
pages.text: The encouraging whisper filled Cassie with new determination. She took a deep breath and listened more carefully to what the walls were telling her. This time, the whisper was clearer: "Where little hands once learned their letters, beneath the place where stories get better..." Cassie's heart skipped with excitement. She knew exactly where that was – the children's reading corner with its rainbow-colored carpet and tiny wooden chairs!
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Cassie standing with renewed energy and a bright smile, looking toward a colorful children's reading area with small chairs, bright cushions, and a rainbow carpet, sunlight highlighting her determined expression

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Giving someone hope and confidence to keep trying their best.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Supporting and inspiring someone with positive words or actions that help them feel brave and motivated to continue.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: My coach was encouraging when she told me I was getting better at soccer.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: The strong feeling of not giving up on something important.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: A powerful inner strength that keeps you working toward your goal, even when things get difficult or challenging.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: With determination, she practiced piano every day until she could play the song perfectly.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Under or below something else, in a lower place.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: In a position that is lower than or covered by something else, like being underneath or below.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The treasure was buried beneath the old oak tree in the backyard.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Having all the bright colors you see in a rainbow.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Decorated or painted with multiple bright colors like red, orange, yellow, green, blue, and purple all together.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: She wore a rainbow-colored scarf with stripes of every bright color.

pages.label: Page 6
pages.text: In the children's corner, Cassie knelt on the soft, rainbow carpet that had welcomed thousands of young readers over the years. She carefully moved the tiny wooden chairs, each one painted a different cheerful color – red, blue, yellow, and green. Under the smallest chair, painted sunshine yellow, she noticed a loose floorboard. Her heart raced as she gently lifted it up. The wood felt warm and smooth under her fingertips.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Cassie kneeling on a colorful rainbow carpet, carefully lifting a small yellow wooden chair to reveal a loose floorboard underneath, with other small painted chairs nearby and children's books scattered around

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Greeted warmly and made to feel happy and comfortable.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Made someone feel accepted, appreciated, and glad to be in a place with friendly, warm treatment.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The new student was welcomed by classmates who invited her to sit with them.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: A very large number, much more than hundreds.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: An extremely large amount, meaning many groups of one thousand added together over time.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: Thousands of people came to watch the parade downtown.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Happy and bright in a way that makes others smile.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Having a joyful, upbeat quality that spreads happiness and positive feelings to people around you.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The cheerful yellow flowers made the whole garden look more beautiful.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: A long piece of wood that makes up part of the floor.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: One of the wooden planks or strips that are fitted together to create the floor of a room or building.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The old floorboard creaked loudly whenever someone stepped on it.

pages.label: Page 7
pages.text: Beneath the loose floorboard was a small, wooden box covered in soft velvet that felt like a cat's fur. Cassie's hands trembled with excitement as she opened it carefully. Inside, nestled on a bed of faded purple silk, lay an ornate brass key. It was beautiful, with tiny flowers carved into its handle and a gentle shine despite its age. But there was something else – a yellowed piece of paper with elegant handwriting.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Cassie holding an ornate brass key that catches the light, with an open wooden box lined with purple silk beside her, and an old piece of paper with elegant writing visible

vocabWords.definition_4_7: A very soft, smooth fabric that feels nice to touch.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: A special type of cloth with a smooth, silky texture that feels luxurious and gentle against your skin.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The jewelry box was lined with red velvet to protect the precious rings inside.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Shook slightly because of excitement, fear, or strong feelings.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: When your body shakes a little bit, usually because you're feeling very excited, nervous, scared, or emotional.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: Her voice trembled with emotion as she opened the birthday gift.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Placed cozily and safely in a comfortable, protected spot.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Settled snugly and securely in a warm, sheltered place, like how a baby bird sits safely in its nest.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The kitten nestled comfortably in the soft blanket on my lap.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Decorated with beautiful, fancy designs and pretty details.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Having elaborate, decorative patterns and elegant details that make something look special, artistic, and expensive.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The princess wore an ornate crown covered with jewels and golden flowers.

pages.label: Page 8
pages.text: The note was written in beautiful, flowing script that reminded Cassie of her grandmother's handwriting. It read: "For the child who never gives up – this key opens more than locks. It opens the magic that lives in every story, every dream, every question that leads to wonder." Cassie felt warm happiness spread through her chest like hot chocolate on a winter day. The walls had helped her find something truly special.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Cassie reading an old note with beautiful handwriting, golden sunlight streaming over her shoulder to illuminate the paper, her face glowing with wonder and happiness

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Moving smoothly and gracefully like water in a gentle stream.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Moving in a smooth, continuous way that looks elegant and natural, like how water moves in a calm river.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: Her flowing dance moves made it look like she was floating across the stage.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: A style of fancy, connected handwriting that looks very neat.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: A type of elegant, cursive handwriting where the letters are connected and written in a flowing, artistic style.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The wedding invitation was written in beautiful script that was hard to read.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Beautiful in a graceful, fancy way that looks very special.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Having a refined, sophisticated beauty that appears graceful, stylish, and impressively well-designed or well-made.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The elegant ballroom had crystal chandeliers and marble floors.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: A feeling of amazement and curiosity about amazing things.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: The magical feeling of awe and curiosity you get when you see or think about something truly amazing or mysterious.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: She stared at the shooting stars with wonder, amazed by their beauty.

pages.label: Page 9
pages.text: Cassie hurried back to Mrs. Chen with the key and the note, her footsteps echoing cheerfully through the library halls. Mrs. Chen's eyes filled with happy tears when she saw what Cassie had found. "This key was hidden by the library's first children's librarian fifty years ago!" she exclaimed. Together, they unlocked the rare book collection, revealing treasures that would bring joy to many young readers. The walls seemed to hum with contentment.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Cassie and Mrs. Chen, a kind elderly librarian with silver hair and glasses, standing together at an ornate cabinet, using the brass key to unlock it, both smiling with joy and excitement

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Making sounds that bounce back and repeat in big spaces.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: When sounds bounce off walls and surfaces, creating repeating versions of the original sound that you can hear again.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: Our voices were echoing loudly as we shouted in the empty gymnasium.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Said something loudly with strong excitement or surprise in your voice.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Spoke with sudden emotion and energy, usually because you were very excited, surprised, or amazed by something.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: "Look at that huge rainbow!" she exclaimed when the storm clouds cleared away.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Opened something that was closed and protected by a lock.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Used a key or combination to open something that was secured and couldn't be opened without permission.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: Dad unlocked the front door when we got home from our vacation.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Very special and valuable things that people love and protect.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Precious objects or discoveries that are considered extremely valuable, either because they cost a lot or mean something special.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The pirates buried their treasures of gold coins and jewels on the island.

pages.label: Page 10
pages.text: As the sun set through the tall library windows, painting everything in soft orange and pink light, Cassie helped Mrs. Chen arrange the newly discovered books on their special shelf. She had learned something wonderful that day, when you don't give up, even the walls around you might whisper encouragement. From that day forward, whenever young visitors felt discouraged while searching for the perfect book, Cassie would tell them about the whispering walls and remind them that brave hearts never stop trying.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Cassie and Mrs. Chen arranging beautiful old books on shelves as golden sunset light streams through tall windows, the library glowing warmly around them, with subtle magical sparkles suggesting the whispering walls watching over them

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Covering or coloring something with beautiful colors like an artist.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Creating beautiful colors and patterns on a surface, or when light creates colorful scenes like an artist's masterpiece.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The sunset was painting the sky in shades of purple and gold.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Put things in neat, organized places where they look good.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Organize and position things in a careful, thoughtful way so they look nice and are easy to find or use.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: Mom helped me arrange my stuffed animals neatly on my bedroom shelf.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Feeling sad and like giving up when things seem too hard.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: The unhappy feeling when challenges make you want to quit because you think you can't succeed or do well.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: He felt discouraged after failing the test, but his teacher helped him study better.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Kind words and support that help someone feel brave and hopeful.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: The positive support, helpful words, and caring actions that give someone confidence and strength to keep trying their best.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: With her grandmother's encouragement, she learned to ride her bicycle without training wheels.

comprehensionQuestions.question: What was Cassie looking for in the old library?

options.text: A missing key to the rare book collection
Her favorite storybook from when she was little
Mrs. Chen's reading glasses

comprehensionQuestions.question: Why do you think the walls were trying to help Cassie?

options.text: Because the library wanted children to succeed and never give up
Because the walls were actually haunted by ghosts
Because Mrs. Chen was hiding and whispering the clues

comprehensionQuestions.question: Have you ever felt like giving up on something difficult but then found the strength to keep trying?

options.text: Yes, and I felt proud when I didn't give up
Yes, and someone's encouragement helped me continue
Yes, and I learned something important about myself

predictionPause.question: Cassie is feeling discouraged and wants to give up, but she just heard the walls whisper 'Brave hearts never stop trying...' What do you think will happen next?

options.text: Cassie will feel brave again and keep searching for the key
Cassie will ask Mrs. Chen for help with the mystery
The walls will give Cassie a clearer clue about where to look

m4.title: Who Took the Moon?
m4.obstacle: the moon has disappeared from the sky

pages.label: Page 1
pages.text: Owl loved the bright, round moon. Every night, she would hoot hello to her glowing friend in the dark sky.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: A friendly brown owl perched on a tree branch, looking up at a bright full moon in a starry night sky. The owl has big, kind eyes and the moon glows warmly yellow.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Something that gives off lots of light and shines.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Something that shines with strong, clear light that makes everything easy to see.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The bright sun made the flowers look beautiful.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: The special calling sound that owls make at night.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: The deep, hollow calling sound that owls make to communicate with each other.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: We heard the owl hoot from the old oak tree.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Something that gives off a warm, gentle light.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Something that shines with a soft, warm light that seems to come from inside.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The glowing campfire kept us warm all night.

pages.label: Page 2
pages.text: But tonight was different. Owl looked up and gasped. The moon was gone! Only dark, empty sky remained.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: The same owl on her branch looking up with surprise at a completely dark sky filled only with tiny stars. Her eyes are wide with worry and confusion.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Something that has changed from how it was before.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Something that is not like what you expected or what usually happens.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: Today feels different because it's snowing instead of sunny.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Breathed in quickly because you were very surprised.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Made a sharp intake of breath when something shocked or surprised you suddenly.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: She gasped when she saw the beautiful rainbow.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Something that was left behind or stayed in place.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Something that continued to be there even after other things had gone away.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: Only crumbs remained after we ate all the cookies.

pages.label: Page 3
pages.text: "Where is my moon friend?" hooted Owl sadly. She flew down to ask Rabbit, who was nibbling clover in the meadow.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Owl flying down toward a fluffy white rabbit who is eating green clover in a moonlit meadow. The rabbit looks up curiously as Owl approaches.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Called out with the deep sound that owls make.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Made the hollow, calling sound that is special to owls and other birds.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The owl hooted three times from the tall tree.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Eating something by taking very small, gentle bites.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Taking small, careful bites of food the way small animals often eat.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The mouse was nibbling on a piece of cheese.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: A plant with three round leaves that grows low.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: A small plant with three heart-shaped leaves that animals like rabbits love to eat.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: We found a four-leaf clover in the grass.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: An open field covered with grass and wildflowers.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: A large, open area of grassland where wildflowers grow and animals come to eat.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: Butterflies danced over the sunny meadow.

pages.label: Page 4
pages.text: "The moon is missing!" Owl told Rabbit. Rabbit's nose twitched with worry. "Let's ask Fox," said Rabbit. "She's very clever."
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Owl and Rabbit talking together in the grassy meadow. Rabbit's pink nose is twitching and both animals look concerned but determined to help.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Something that is not where it should be.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Something that has disappeared from its usual place and cannot be found anywhere.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: My favorite toy is missing from my toy box.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Made small, quick movements without meaning to.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Made small, jerky movements that happen automatically when you're nervous or excited.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: His eye twitched when he was concentrating hard.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Someone who is good at figuring things out.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Someone who uses their brain well to solve problems and think of good ideas.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The clever girl built a tower with her blocks.

pages.label: Page 5
pages.text: Fox was washing her red fur by the stream. "Missing moon?" she wondered. "Let's follow the silver path it makes on the water."
pages.scene: illustration prompt: A beautiful red fox sitting by a babbling stream, with Owl and Rabbit nearby. Moonbeams create a shimmering silver path on the water's surface.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Making something clean by using water to scrub it.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: The process of cleaning something by rubbing it with water to remove dirt.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: Dad is washing the dishes after dinner.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Asked yourself a question or thought about something puzzling.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Thought carefully about something that seemed mysterious or hard to understand.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: She wondered what was inside the wrapped present.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: A bright, shiny color like a mirror or coins.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: A bright metallic color that shines and reflects light like polished metal or moonbeams.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The silver spoon sparkled in the light.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Water that flows along the ground in a line.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: A narrow flow of water that moves continuously from one place to another.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: Fish swim in the clear mountain stream.

pages.label: Page 6
pages.text: The three friends walked along the stream together. Suddenly, they heard a tiny crying sound from behind some tall reeds.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Owl, Rabbit, and Fox walking together beside a winding stream with tall green reeds. They're all listening carefully, heads tilted toward the sound.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Something that happens very quickly without any warning.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Something that happens so quickly and unexpectedly that it surprises you completely.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The dog suddenly started barking at the mailman.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: The sounds someone makes when they feel sad or hurt.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: The sound of tears and sobbing that happens when someone feels very sad or upset.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The baby was crying because she was hungry.

vocabWords.definition_2_4: Tall grass that grows in water.
vocabWords.definition_4_7: Tall, thin plants that grow near water and rivers.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Long, slender plants that grow in wet places and sway gently in the wind.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The duck hid her nest among the reeds by the pond.

pages.label: Page 7
pages.text: Behind the reeds sat Little Bear, sniffling softly. "I was so scared of the dark," he whispered. "I wished the moon away."
pages.scene: illustration prompt: A small, fluffy brown bear cub sitting sadly behind tall green reeds, with tears in his eyes. The three friends are discovering him with gentle, understanding expressions.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Making quiet sounds through your nose when you're sad.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Making small snorting sounds with your nose that happen when you're trying not to cry.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: She was sniffling because she had a cold.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: The feeling you get when something might hurt you.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: The worried, frightened feeling that makes your heart beat fast when you think something bad might happen.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The little boy felt scared during the thunderstorm.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Spoke so softly that only people close by could hear.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Spoke in such a quiet voice that the words were barely louder than breathing.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: She whispered the secret in her friend's ear.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Hoped for something special to come true with magic.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Made a special hope in your mind that something wonderful would happen, often like magic.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: She wished for a puppy on her birthday.

pages.label: Page 8
pages.text: "Oh, Little Bear," said Owl gently. "But the moon gives us beautiful, soft light. It helps us see in the darkness."
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Owl speaking kindly to Little Bear while Rabbit and Fox gather around supportively. All the animals look caring and gentle in the starlight.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Doing something carefully and kindly without being rough.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Acting in a tender, careful way that shows you care about not hurting anyone's feelings.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: Mom gently tucked the blanket around the sleeping baby.

vocabWords.definition_2_4: Very pretty and nice to look at.
vocabWords.definition_4_7: Something that looks so lovely it makes you feel happy.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Something so lovely and pleasing to see that it fills you with joy and wonder.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The beautiful butterfly had colorful wings.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: The time when there is no light anywhere around.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: The deep, shadowy time when there is very little light to help you see things clearly.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The flashlight helped us see in the darkness.

pages.label: Page 9
pages.text: "I didn't know," said Little Bear. "I want to help bring it back!" All four friends closed their eyes and wished together.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: All four animal friends - Owl, Rabbit, Fox, and Little Bear - standing in a circle with their eyes closed, making a wish together under the starry sky.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Put their eyelids down so they couldn't see anything.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Brought their eyelids together tightly so no light could get into their eyes.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: She closed her eyes and made a birthday wish.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Doing something as a group at the same moment.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Acting as one group where everyone does the same thing at exactly the same time.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The whole class sang the song together.

pages.label: Page 10
pages.text: Slowly, a silver glow appeared in the sky. The moon smiled down at them, bright and beautiful once again!
pages.scene: illustration prompt: The four friends looking up in joy as the full moon reappears in the sky, glowing warmly and seeming to smile down at them. Their faces are filled with happiness and wonder.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Moving or happening at a gentle, unhurried speed.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Taking plenty of time to happen, moving at a calm pace without any rushing.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The turtle slowly walked across the path.

vocabWords.definition_2_4: Showed up where it wasn't before.
vocabWords.definition_4_7: Came into sight when it wasn't there before.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Became visible in a place where nothing could be seen just moments before.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: A rainbow appeared in the sky after the rain.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Made a happy face by turning up the corners.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Made a cheerful expression that showed happiness and made others feel good too.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The teacher smiled when she saw our drawings.

pages.label: Page 11
pages.text: "Thank you for helping me," said Little Bear. From that night on, all four friends would sit together under the gentle moonlight.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: All four animal friends sitting peacefully together on a hillside, bathed in soft moonlight. They look content and happy, having become good friends. The moon glows warmly above them.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Making things easier or better for another person.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Using your actions and kindness to make someone else's life better or solve their problems.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: She was helping her dad cook dinner.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Something that is calm, soft, and won't hurt you.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Something that is tender and peaceful, like a warm hug or a quiet breeze.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The gentle breeze made the flowers dance.

vocabWords.definition_2_4: Light that comes from the moon.
vocabWords.definition_4_7: The soft, silver light that the moon sends down.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: The beautiful, silvery glow that comes from the moon and lights up the night sky.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The cat walked across the garden in the moonlight.

m5.title: The Secret Note
m5.obstacle: a mysterious note in his locker leads to a treasure hunt

pages.label: Page 1
pages.text: Marco opened his bright blue locker on Monday morning. Inside, tucked between his math book and lunch box, was a folded piece of yellow paper. He had never seen it before. His heart began to beat a little faster.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: A young boy with dark hair opening a blue school locker, looking surprised at a yellow folded paper inside among books and a lunch box

vocabWords.definition_4_7: A small metal box at school where you keep your things.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: A personal storage compartment with a door and lock where students keep their belongings safe.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: She put her gym clothes in the locker at the swimming pool.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Hidden or placed carefully between other things.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Placed snugly and securely in a small space, often hidden from view.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The bird tucked its head under its wing to sleep.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Bent or pressed so one part covers another part.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Creased and bent so that sections of paper or fabric lie flat against each other.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: Dad folded the clean towels and put them away.

pages.label: Page 2
pages.text: Marco carefully unfolded the mysterious note. In purple crayon, someone had drawn a map of his school! There was a big red X marked by the old oak tree in the playground. At the bottom, the note said 'Follow the clues, brave explorer!'
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Marco holding up a yellow paper with a hand-drawn map in purple crayon, showing a school layout with trees and a red X mark

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Something strange and hard to understand or explain.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Puzzling and intriguing, like a secret that makes you curious to learn more.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The mysterious footprints in the sand led to the cave.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: A person who travels to discover new places and things.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Someone brave who ventures into unknown territories to make exciting discoveries and learn new things.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The explorer packed her backpack for the jungle adventure.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Hints that help you solve a puzzle or mystery.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Important pieces of information that guide you toward finding an answer or solution.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The detective found clues at the scene of the missing cookies.

pages.label: Page 3
pages.text: Marco felt nervous butterflies dancing in his stomach. Who had left this note? Was it safe to follow the map? He took a deep breath and remembered what his grandmother always said: 'Sometimes being brave means taking one small step forward.'
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Marco standing in the hallway, holding the note, with a thoughtful expression as he looks toward a window showing the playground outside

vocabWords.definition_4_7: A nervous feeling in your stomach when you're worried or excited.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: The fluttery, nervous sensation you feel in your belly when something important or scary is happening.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: She had butterflies before her first day at a new school.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Feeling worried or scared about what might happen next.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Anxious and uneasy, like when you're not sure if something will turn out okay.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The boy felt nervous before giving his speech to the class.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Your mom's or dad's mother who loves and cares for you.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: A special family member who is your parent's mother and often shares wisdom and hugs.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: My grandmother taught me how to bake chocolate chip cookies.

pages.label: Page 4
pages.text: At recess, Marco walked slowly toward the old oak tree. Its thick, rough bark felt warm from the morning sun. As he searched around the base, his fingers found something smooth and round tucked behind a root. It was a small wooden box!
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Marco kneeling by a large oak tree, reaching behind its roots where a small wooden box is hidden, with dappled sunlight filtering through the leaves

vocabWords.definition_4_7: A fun break time during school when you can play.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: A scheduled period during the school day when students take a break from lessons to play and relax.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: During recess, the children played tag on the grass field.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Having a bumpy surface that feels scratchy when you touch it.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Textured and uneven to the touch, not smooth, like sandpaper or tree bark.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The cat's tongue felt rough when it licked my hand.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: The part of a tree that grows underground to get water.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: The underground portion of a plant that anchors it and absorbs nutrients from the soil.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The carrot is actually the root of the carrot plant.

pages.label: Page 5
pages.text: Inside the wooden box was another note and a shiny brass key. The note had a riddle written in the same purple crayon: 'Where books sleep at night, and stories take flight, look high for your prize.' Marco's eyes grew wide with wonder.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Marco sitting under the tree, opening a small wooden box to reveal a brass key and another note, with sunlight streaming down on him

vocabWords.definition_4_7: A shiny yellow metal that looks like gold but isn't.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: A golden-colored metal alloy that gleams brightly and is often used for musical instruments and keys.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The marching band played bright brass trumpets in the parade.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: A tricky question or puzzle you have to think hard about.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: A challenging word puzzle that uses clever language to hide the answer you need to discover.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: Can you solve this riddle: What has hands but cannot clap?

vocabWords.definition_4_7: A feeling of excitement and curiosity about something amazing.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: The magical feeling of awe and curiosity you get when encountering something extraordinary or mysterious.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The child gazed at the rainbow with wonder in her eyes.

pages.label: Page 6
pages.text: Marco thought hard about the riddle. Where do books sleep at night? The library! But what did 'look high' mean? His heart thumped with excitement as he hurried back inside the school building, clutching the brass key tightly in his palm.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Marco walking quickly through the school hallway toward the library, holding the brass key, with determination on his face

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Beat loudly and fast, like when your heart is excited.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Pounded rhythmically with strong, heavy beats that you can feel and sometimes hear.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The rabbit's foot thumped on the ground as it hopped away.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Holding something very tightly in your hand or arms.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Gripping firmly with your fingers or hands, usually because something is precious or you're nervous.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The little girl was clutching her favorite stuffed animal.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: The inside part of your hand where your fingers start.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: The flat, inner surface of your hand between your wrist and fingers where you can hold things.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: She placed the tiny seed in the palm of her hand.

pages.label: Page 7
pages.text: In the cozy library, Marco looked up at the tall bookshelves that reached toward the ceiling. There, on the very top shelf, he spotted a small golden box that seemed to glow in the soft afternoon light. But how could he reach it?
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Marco looking up at very tall library bookshelves with a small golden box visible on the top shelf, warm light streaming through library windows

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Warm and comfortable, like a place you want to snuggle.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Pleasantly warm and inviting, creating a feeling of comfort and contentment like home.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The cozy cabin had a fireplace and soft blankets everywhere.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Tall furniture with shelves where you store lots of books.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Wooden or metal structures with horizontal shelves designed specifically for organizing and displaying books.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The library had bookshelves filled with stories about dragons and princesses.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: The top part of a room that covers everything like a roof.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: The overhead interior surface that forms the upper boundary of a room or building.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The artist painted beautiful stars and clouds on the ceiling.

pages.label: Page 8
pages.text: Marco found Mrs. Chen, the kind librarian with twinkling eyes. 'I'm on a treasure hunt,' he whispered bravely. She smiled and brought over a step ladder. 'Every brave explorer needs the right tools,' she said with a gentle wink.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: A friendly librarian helping Marco position a step ladder near the tall bookshelf, both of them smiling

vocabWords.definition_4_7: A person who works in the library and helps people find books.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: A trained professional who organizes books and helps people discover information and stories in libraries.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The librarian recommended a funny book about talking animals.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Shining with little sparkles of light that seem to dance.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Gleaming with small, flickering points of light that appear and disappear like stars in the sky.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The Christmas tree lights were twinkling in the dark room.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: A tall tool with steps that helps you climb up high.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: A portable climbing device with rungs or steps that allows you to reach elevated places safely.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The firefighter used a ladder to rescue the cat from the tree.

pages.label: Page 9
pages.text: Marco climbed carefully up the ladder, his hands steady and sure. The brass key fit perfectly into the golden box's tiny lock. Click! Inside, he discovered a beautiful compass and a note that made his heart sing with joy.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Marco at the top of the ladder, unlocking a golden box with the brass key, his face lit up with excitement and discovery

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Went up something tall using your hands and feet.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Ascended by using hands and feet to move upward on steps, rocks, or other surfaces.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The monkey climbed the tree to reach the ripe bananas.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Not shaking or moving, staying balanced and controlled.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Firm and stable without wobbling, showing good control and careful movement.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The doctor's steady hands helped her draw a perfect circle.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: A special tool with a needle that always points north.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: A navigation instrument with a magnetic needle that helps travelers find their direction and way.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The hiker used her compass to find the trail back to camp.

pages.label: Page 10
pages.text: The final note read: 'Dear Marco, you were brave enough to follow the mystery! This compass will always point you toward your next adventure. Love, Your Secret Friend (P.S. - Check your locker tomorrow!)' Marco grinned from ear to ear.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Marco holding the beautiful compass and reading the final note, with a huge smile on his face, golden afternoon light surrounding him in the library

vocabWords.definition_4_7: An exciting journey or experience that's fun and a little scary.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: A thrilling and often risky journey or experience that brings excitement, discovery, and memorable moments.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: Going camping in the mountains will be a great adventure.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Something puzzling that you don't understand but want to solve.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: An intriguing puzzle or secret that requires investigation and clever thinking to understand or solve.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The mystery of the missing socks was finally solved under the bed.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Smiled really big and wide because you're super happy.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Smiled broadly with great joy, showing your teeth in a expression of pure happiness and delight.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: She grinned when she saw the surprise birthday cake.

comprehensionQuestions.question: Where did Marco find the first mysterious note?

options.text: In his bright blue locker
Under the old oak tree
In the school library

comprehensionQuestions.question: Why do you think Marco felt nervous butterflies in his stomach?

options.text: He was hungry for lunch
He was worried about following a mysterious map
He was late for math class

comprehensionQuestions.question: How would you feel if you found a mysterious treasure map in your locker?

options.text: Excited and curious to solve the mystery
A little scared but brave enough to try
Happy that someone chose me for an adventure

predictionPause.question: Marco sees the golden box high up on the bookshelf but can't reach it. What do you think will happen next?

options.text: He'll ask the librarian for help getting it down
He'll stack up books to climb higher
He'll find a ladder or step stool to use

m6.title: The Vanishing Colors
m6.obstacle: all the colors are disappearing from the town one by one

pages.label: Page 1
pages.text: Priya loved her colorful town of Rainbow Valley. Every morning, she walked past the bright red roses in Mrs. Chen's garden and the cheerful yellow sunflowers by the library. The purple butterflies danced around the orange marigolds while green grass sparkled with morning dew.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: A cheerful girl with dark hair walking down a vibrant street filled with colorful flowers, butterflies, and houses painted in bright hues

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Having many bright and pretty colors all mixed together.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Full of many different bright colors that make something look cheerful and beautiful.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The colorful balloons at the party made everyone smile.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Looking happy and bright in a way that makes you smile.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Having a happy, bright appearance that makes people feel good when they see it.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The cheerful music made the children want to dance.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Shined with tiny points of light like little stars.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Glittered and shimmered with small flashes of light that danced and twinkled.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The snow sparkled in the sunlight on the mountain.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Bright orange or yellow flowers that grow in gardens.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Cheerful orange and yellow flowers that bloom in gardens and smell strong and spicy.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: Mom planted marigolds around the vegetable garden to keep bugs away.

pages.label: Page 2
pages.text: But one Tuesday morning, something strange happened. All the red things in town had turned gray! Mrs. Chen's roses looked like storm clouds, and the fire truck was the color of old concrete. "Where did all the red go?" Priya wondered aloud.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Priya looking puzzled at gray roses and a gray fire truck, with other colors still vibrant but all red items now dull gray

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Different from normal in a way that seems odd or weird.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Unusual or different from what you expect, making you feel curious or confused about it.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The strange noise in the attic made Dad go investigate.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: A hard gray material used to build sidewalks and buildings.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: A strong, hard building material that's gray in color and used for roads and foundations.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The workers poured concrete to make a new driveway.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Thought about something and wanted to know the answer.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Felt curious about something and spent time thinking about it, hoping to understand.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: She wondered what surprise was waiting inside the wrapped box.

pages.label: Page 3
pages.text: The next day, all the orange disappeared too! The marigolds looked pale and sad, and even the sunset seemed incomplete. Priya noticed her neighbors standing in their doorways, scratching their heads and whispering to each other about the missing colors.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Townspeople gathered outside looking concerned, pointing at gray marigolds and a pale sunset lacking orange hues

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Went away completely so you can't see it anymore.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Vanished completely from sight, as if it was never there in the first place.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The rabbit disappeared into its burrow when it saw the fox.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Missing parts so it's not finished or whole.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Not having all the pieces or parts needed to be complete and finished.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The puzzle looked incomplete because three pieces were missing.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Talking very quietly so only people nearby can hear.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Speaking in very soft, quiet voices so that others cannot easily overhear the conversation.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The students were whispering during the movie so they wouldn't disturb others.

pages.label: Page 4
pages.text: By Thursday, yellow had vanished! The sunflowers drooped like gray umbrellas, and the school bus looked like a traveling rain cloud. Priya decided she couldn't just watch her beautiful town lose its colors one by one. She needed to solve this mystery!
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Priya with a determined expression, standing next to gray sunflowers and a gray school bus, hands on her hips in a detective pose

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Disappeared completely like magic, gone without a trace.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Disappeared so completely and suddenly that it seemed like it was never there at all.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The cookies vanished from the jar before dinner time.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Hung down sadly like they were tired or sick.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Bent or hung downward in a sad, tired way, like they had lost their strength.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The flowers drooped because they needed water on the hot day.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Something strange that happened that you want to solve.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: A puzzling situation or problem that needs to be investigated and solved to understand what happened.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The mystery of the missing homework made the teacher very curious.

pages.label: Page 5
pages.text: Priya knocked on every door in town. "We need to work together to find our colors!" she announced. Soon, she had gathered Mrs. Chen, the librarian Mr. Lopez, firefighter Sam, and many other neighbors. They formed a rainbow search team.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Priya standing with a diverse group of townspeople of different ages, all looking hopeful and ready to help, gathered in the town square

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Said something important in a loud, clear voice.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Declared something important publicly so that everyone around could hear and understand the message.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The principal announced that school would end early due to snow.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Brought people together in one place for a reason.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Collected or brought people together in the same place so they could work as a group.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The family gathered around the table for Thanksgiving dinner.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: A person whose job is to help people in a library.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Someone who works in a library and helps people find books and information they need.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The librarian helped me find books about dinosaurs for my project.

pages.label: Page 6
pages.text: The team split up to search everywhere. They looked under park benches, inside mailboxes, and behind the grocery store. Mrs. Chen checked her garden shed while Mr. Lopez searched the library basement. Everyone had a job to do!
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Multiple small scenes showing different people searching in various locations - under benches, in sheds, through books, behind buildings

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Divided into smaller groups to do different jobs.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Separated into different groups so they could cover more ground and work more efficiently.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The class split into teams to play different games at recess.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Special boxes where mail carriers put letters and packages.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Container boxes where the postal service delivers mail and packages for people to collect.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The mail carrier filled all the mailboxes on our street with letters.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: The lowest room in a building, usually underground.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: The bottom floor of a building that's below ground level, often used for storage.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: Dad keeps his tools in the basement workshop.

pages.label: Page 7
pages.text: Just as green began to fade from the grass and trees, little Tommy Chen shouted, "I found something!" Behind the old art studio, he discovered a magical prism half-buried in the dirt. It looked cloudy and sad, nothing like the sparkling crystal it should be.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: A young boy pointing excitedly at a dull, cloudy crystal prism partially buried in dirt behind a small art studio building

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Said something very loudly because he was excited.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Called out in a loud, excited voice to get everyone's attention about his discovery.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The boy shouted with joy when he scored the winning goal.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: A special crystal that can split light into rainbow colors.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: A clear, triangular piece of glass or crystal that breaks white light into all the rainbow colors.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The prism in the window made colorful rainbows dance on the wall.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Partly covered with dirt but still showing a little bit.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Partially covered by soil or dirt, with only part of it visible above the ground.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The old treasure chest was half-buried in sand at the beach.

pages.label: Page 8
pages.text: "The town's color prism!" gasped Mr. Lopez. "My grandmother told stories about this magical crystal." The prism was covered in years of dust and grime. Priya had an idea. "If we all work together to clean it, maybe the colors will return!"
pages.scene: illustration prompt: The group gathered around the dirty prism, with Mr. Lopez explaining while Priya points to the crystal with excitement

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Took in a quick, surprised breath when shocked.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Breathed in suddenly and sharply because of surprise, shock, or amazement at what he saw.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: Everyone gasped when the magician made the rabbit appear.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: A clear, hard rock that can be very beautiful and sparkly.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: A naturally formed clear or colored mineral that's hard and often has flat surfaces that sparkle.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The crystal necklace caught the light and made pretty reflections.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Thick, dirty stuff that builds up over a long time.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: A layer of dirt, grease, and dust that accumulates over time and makes things look dull.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The old car was covered in grime from sitting in the garage for years.

pages.label: Page 9
pages.text: Everyone helped! Mrs. Chen brought soft cloths, Sam brought clean water, and Mr. Lopez found gentle soap. They took turns carefully cleaning the prism, working together like a well-practiced team. Slowly, the crystal began to sparkle and shine.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: All the townspeople working together, gently cleaning the crystal with cloths and water, everyone taking turns and helping each other

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Soft and careful, not rough or too strong.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Done with care and tenderness, being very careful not to cause damage or harm.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: She used gentle touches to pet the tiny kitten.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Doing something slowly and gently so nothing gets broken.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Working with great attention and caution to make sure everything is done safely and correctly.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: He carefully carried the birthday cake so it wouldn't fall.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Really good at something because they've done it many times.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Very skilled and coordinated from having worked together many times before, like a professional team.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The well-practiced band played the song perfectly without any mistakes.

pages.label: Page 10
pages.text: As the last speck of dirt disappeared, the prism burst into brilliant light! Colors danced and swirled around the town like ribbons in the wind. Red returned to the roses, orange glowed in the marigolds, and yellow brightened the sunflowers. Rainbow Valley was beautiful again!
pages.scene: illustration prompt: The crystal shooting beams of rainbow light across the town, with colors magically returning to flowers, buildings, and everything else, townspeople cheering with joy

vocabWords.definition_4_7: A tiny little piece of dirt, smaller than a dot.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: An extremely small particle or spot of something, so tiny it's barely visible to see.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: Mom found one last speck of dust on the clean table.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Suddenly exploded with bright light in every direction.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Suddenly erupted or exploded outward with tremendous energy and brilliant, dazzling illumination everywhere.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The fireworks burst into colorful stars across the night sky.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Extremely bright and shining, almost too bright to look at.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Intensely bright and dazzling, shining with such powerful light that it's almost blinding to see.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The brilliant lightning lit up the entire stormy sky.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Moved around and around in circles like a whirlpool.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Moved in spinning, circular motions, twisting and turning around each other in graceful patterns.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The autumn leaves swirled in circles when the wind picked them up.

pages.label: Page 11
pages.text: Priya smiled as she walked through her colorful town once more. The purple butterflies danced around the orange marigolds while Mrs. Chen's red roses smelled sweeter than ever. She had learned that the biggest mysteries are solved best when everyone works together.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Priya walking happily through the fully restored colorful town, with all the neighbors visible in the background tending to their vibrant gardens and waving to each other

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Big puzzles or strange things that need to be solved.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Complicated problems or puzzling situations that require investigation and teamwork to understand and solve.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The detective loved solving mysteries about missing pets in the neighborhood.

comprehensionQuestions.question: What was the first color that disappeared from Rainbow Valley?

options.text: Red
Orange
Yellow

comprehensionQuestions.question: Why do you think the prism stopped working and made colors disappear?

options.text: It got too dirty and dusty over the years
Someone broke it with a hammer
It was too old and needed batteries

comprehensionQuestions.question: How would you feel if all the colors in your neighborhood started disappearing?

options.text: Worried and sad like Priya
Curious and excited to solve the mystery
Ready to help my neighbors fix the problem

predictionPause.question: Tommy found something behind the old art studio! What do you think will happen next?

options.text: They'll discover it's magical and can bring colors back
They'll need to clean it to make it work again
Everyone will work together to fix the mysterious object

m7.title: The Clock That Stopped
m7.obstacle: the town clock stopped and strange things started happening

pages.label: Page 1
pages.text: Felix loved the old clock tower that stood in the center of Willowbrook. Every morning, he would wave to it on his way to school, listening to its cheerful chimes ring across the cobblestone square. The golden hands moved steadily, and the deep bronze bell sang out the hours like an old friend greeting the town. But one Tuesday morning, something was terribly wrong.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: A charming town square with a tall clock tower in the center. Felix, a curious boy with messy brown hair, walks across cobblestones carrying a school backpack, waving up at the ornate clock tower. Morning sunlight filters through the scene.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: A very tall building that reaches high into the sky.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: A tall, narrow structure that rises high above other buildings, often serving as a landmark.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The princess was locked in the tower of the castle.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Musical sounds made by bells ringing together in harmony.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Pleasant musical tones created when bells ring together, often marking the time or special occasions.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The wind chimes on the porch made beautiful sounds in the breeze.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Small round stones used to make old-fashioned roads and paths.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Rounded stones fitted together to create durable streets and walkways, common in historic towns.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The horse's hooves clicked against the cobblestone street.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: A brown-colored metal made by mixing copper and tin together.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: A durable, brownish metal alloy often used for making bells, statues, and decorative objects.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The statue in the park was made of shiny bronze.

pages.label: Page 2
pages.text: The clock had stopped at exactly 11:47. Its brass hands hung motionless, and no chimes echoed through the square. Felix rubbed his eyes and looked again, but the hands hadn't budged even a second. The silence felt strange and heavy, like a blanket had been thrown over the whole town. Even the pigeons seemed confused, tilting their heads as they perched on the quiet clock face.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Close-up of the ornate clock face showing 11:47, with confused pigeons perched on the hands. Felix stands below looking up with concern, his hand shading his eyes. The clock appears frozen and lifeless.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Not moving at all, staying completely still in one place.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Completely still and unmoving, like a statue frozen in time without any motion whatsoever.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The deer stood motionless when it heard the rustling leaves.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: A sound that bounces back and repeats after the original.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: When sounds bounce off surfaces and return to your ears, creating a repeating effect.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: Her voice echoed through the empty gymnasium.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Moved even a tiny bit from where it was before.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Shifted or moved slightly from its original position, even just a small amount.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The heavy rock wouldn't budge no matter how hard we pushed.

pages.label: Page 3
pages.text: As the day went on, Felix noticed other peculiar things happening around Willowbrook. Mrs. Henderson's flowers, which always faced the sun, were spinning in circles like tiny dancers. The fountain in the park was flowing upward instead of down, creating shimmering arcs in the air. Even the weathervane on top of the library was spinning wildly, unable to decide which direction to point. Felix knew these strange happenings had to be connected to the silent clock.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: A whimsical scene showing flowers spinning like pinwheels, water flowing upward from a fountain in graceful arcs, and a weathervane spinning rapidly. Felix observes these magical happenings with wonder and determination.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Strange or unusual, different from what you normally expect to see.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Odd or unusual in a way that makes you notice because it's different from normal.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The peculiar smell coming from the kitchen made everyone curious.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: A structure that shoots water up into the air beautifully.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: An ornamental water feature that sprays water upward in decorative patterns for people to enjoy.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: Children threw coins into the fountain and made wishes.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: A spinning arrow on top of buildings that shows wind direction.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: A rotating pointer mounted on rooftops that turns to show which direction the wind is blowing.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The rooster weathervane pointed east when the morning wind blew.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Sparkling and glowing softly with moving light that dances around.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Gleaming with a soft, wavering light that seems to dance and sparkle like gentle magic.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The lake was shimmering in the moonlight like scattered diamonds.

pages.label: Page 4
pages.text: Felix decided he had to investigate the clock tower, even though no one had been inside for years. He found the narrow wooden door at the base, painted deep blue and covered with ivy. The brass doorknob felt cold and heavy in his small hand. When he turned it, the door creaked open with a sound like an old ship, revealing a spiral staircase that disappeared into shadows above.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Felix standing before an old blue wooden door covered in green ivy at the base of the clock tower. His hand is on the brass doorknob, and warm light spills from the partially opened door onto the cobblestones.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: To look carefully and search for clues to solve a mystery.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: To examine something closely and systematically in order to discover the truth or find answers.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The detective will investigate the missing bicycle case.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: A curved shape that goes around and around getting bigger or smaller.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: A continuous curve that winds around a center point, either expanding outward or contracting inward.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The spiral shell was perfectly shaped like a twisted cone.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Made a squeaky, groaning sound like old wood or metal.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Produced a harsh, squeaking noise typically made by old hinges, floorboards, or wooden structures.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The old wooden stairs creaked under my feet as I climbed.

pages.label: Page 5
pages.text: The wooden steps groaned under Felix's feet as he climbed the winding staircase. Dust motes danced in the slanted sunbeams that streamed through small windows. Higher and higher he climbed, past old gears and pulleys that hadn't moved in hours. His legs grew tired, and he had to stop twice to catch his breath, but Felix refused to give up. The mystery of the stopped clock was waiting for him at the top.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Felix climbing the spiral wooden staircase inside the clock tower, with dust particles dancing in golden sunbeams. Old mechanical gears and pulleys are visible along the walls. Felix looks determined despite being tired.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Made a deep, complaining sound like something old and tired.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Produced a low, prolonged sound expressing strain or weariness, often from old materials under pressure.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The old tree groaned in the strong wind.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Twisting and turning like a snake as it goes up or down.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Following a twisting, serpentine path that curves back and forth rather than going straight.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The winding mountain road had many sharp turns and curves.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Tiny pieces of dust floating in the air that sparkle.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Small specks of dust or other particles that drift through the air, often visible in sunbeams.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: Dust motes danced in the sunlight streaming through the window.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Wheel-shaped tools with ropes that help lift heavy things more easily.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Mechanical devices with wheels and ropes or cables used to lift or move heavy objects efficiently.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The workers used pulleys to lift the heavy boxes to the second floor.

pages.label: Page 6
pages.text: At the top of the tower, Felix found the clock's great heart—a room filled with enormous brass gears, springs, and pendulums. Everything was covered in a thick layer of dust and cobwebs that sparkled like silver lace. But there, in the center of it all, Felix spotted the problem. A small family of mice had built their cozy nest right inside the main gear, and baby mice were sleeping peacefully among the soft cotton and paper scraps.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: A magical clockwork room with huge brass gears, springs, and pendulums covered in sparkling cobwebs. In the center gear, a tiny mouse family sleeps in a nest made of cotton and paper. Felix peers at them with gentle curiosity.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Much bigger than normal, huge and taking up lots of space.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Extraordinarily large in size, much bigger than what you would typically expect to see.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The enormous pumpkin at the fair weighed over 100 pounds.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Heavy objects that swing back and forth to help clocks work.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Weighted objects suspended from a fixed point that swing rhythmically to regulate clock mechanisms.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The grandfather clock's pendulums swung steadily back and forth all day.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Old spider webs covered in dust that look like lace.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Dusty, abandoned spider webs that have accumulated in corners and unused spaces over time.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The attic was full of cobwebs that hadn't been cleaned in years.

pages.label: Page 7
