pages.text: Felix's first thought was to move the mice right away, but then he heard their tiny squeaks and saw how small and helpless the babies were. He couldn't bear to disturb them from their warm home. But the town needed their clock back, and the strange happenings were getting worse. Felix sat down on the dusty floor to think, resting his chin in his hands. There had to be a way to help both the mice and the town.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Felix sitting cross-legged on the dusty floor of the clock room, chin in his hands, thinking deeply. The mouse family is visible in the gear nearby. Soft light filters through the room, showing his thoughtful, caring expression.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: To bother or interrupt someone when they need peace and quiet.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: To interrupt or interfere with someone's rest, peace, or normal activities in an unwelcome way.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: Please don't disturb your sister while she's doing homework.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Unable to take care of yourself and needing others for help.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Lacking the ability or strength to care for oneself and requiring assistance from others.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The helpless kitten needed its mother to feed and protect it.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: High-pitched little sounds like mice or doors that need oil make.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Short, high-pitched sounds typically made by small animals or objects that need lubrication.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The rubber duck squeaks when you squeeze it in the bathtub.

pages.label: Page 8
pages.text: Suddenly, Felix remembered the old birdhouse in his backyard that had been empty all summer. It was perfect! He carefully climbed back down the tower and ran home, his heart pounding with excitement. He cleaned the little wooden house until it sparkled, filled it with soft cotton from his mother's sewing box, and even added a few sunflower seeds. Then he hurried back to the clock tower, carrying his gift for the mouse family.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Felix in his backyard, polishing a small wooden birdhouse with a clean cloth. Cotton, sunflower seeds, and cleaning supplies are spread on a garden table. His face shows excited determination and kindness.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: A small wooden house built for birds to live in safely.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: A miniature house structure designed to provide shelter and nesting space for small birds.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: Dad hung the new birdhouse on the oak tree in our yard.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Shined with tiny points of bright light like stars or diamonds.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Glittered with small, bright flashes of light that twinkled and danced like precious gems.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The fresh snow sparkled in the morning sunlight.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: A happy, energetic feeling when something wonderful is about to happen.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: An eager, thrilled feeling of joy and anticipation when something special or important is occurring.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The children could barely contain their excitement before the carnival.

pages.label: Page 9
pages.text: Back in the clock room, Felix gently moved the mouse family to their new birdhouse home, whispering soft words of comfort to keep them calm. The mother mouse sniffed the fresh cotton and seemed to approve with a tiny squeak. Felix carefully placed the birdhouse in a quiet corner of the tower where the mice would be safe and warm. Then he held his breath and waited to see if his plan would work.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Felix kneeling in the clock room, gently placing the birdhouse in a cozy corner. The mother mouse peeks out approvingly while baby mice snuggle in the cotton. Warm light creates a tender, caring atmosphere.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Doing something softly and carefully so you don't hurt anyone.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Performing an action with tender care and delicate touch, showing kindness and consideration.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: She gently picked up the injured butterfly to help it.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Speaking very, very quietly so only someone close can hear you.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Talking in an extremely soft, hushed voice that can barely be heard by others nearby.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The librarian was whispering so she wouldn't wake the sleeping baby.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: To like something and think it's a good choice or idea.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: To accept or agree with something because you believe it is good, right, or satisfactory.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: Mom will approve of my plan to clean my room before playing.

pages.label: Page 10
pages.text: With a great grinding sound, the clock's gears began to turn again! The brass pendulum swung back and forth with a steady rhythm, and Felix could feel the whole tower coming alive beneath his feet. He raced to the window just as the clock struck twelve, its deep bronze chimes ringing out across Willowbrook once more. Down in the square, the fountain flowed properly again, the flowers turned toward the sun, and the weathervane pointed true north.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: The clock mechanism in full motion with turning gears and swinging pendulum. Felix at the tower window with arms raised in triumph as the clock hands move to 12. Through the window, the town square is visible with everything back to normal.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: A rough, scraping sound made when hard things rub together.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: A harsh, grating noise produced when rough surfaces or mechanical parts move against each other.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The old car made a grinding sound when the brakes needed repair.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: A heavy piece that swings back and forth inside a clock.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: A weighted object that swings rhythmically from side to side to keep time in clocks.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The pendulum in the old clock swung steadily every second.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: A steady beat or pattern that repeats over and over.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: A regular, repeated pattern of sounds or movements that creates a steady, predictable beat.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The drummer kept a steady rhythm throughout the entire song.

pages.label: Page 11
pages.text: Felix climbed down the tower stairs with a huge smile on his face. In the square, townspeople were gathering and pointing at the working clock with joy and relief. Mrs. Henderson waved to him from her garden, where her flowers were once again facing the morning sun. The fountain sparkled properly in the sunlight, and even the pigeons seemed happier as they flew around the moving clock hands. Felix had solved the mystery by never giving up and by caring for everyone—even the tiniest residents of Willowbrook.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Felix emerging from the clock tower into the sunny town square where happy townspeople are gathered, pointing and smiling at the working clock. Mrs. Henderson waves from her garden, the fountain sparkles normally, and pigeons fly joyfully around the clock.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Coming together in one place to meet as a group.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Assembling or collecting together in the same location for a common purpose or shared interest.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The family is gathering for dinner at grandmother's house tonight.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: A happy, calm feeling when something bad stops or gets better.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: A sense of comfort and peace that comes when worry, stress, or problems are finally resolved.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: I felt great relief when I found my lost homework assignment.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: People or animals who live in a certain place as home.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Individuals or creatures who make their permanent home and live within a particular area or community.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: All the residents of our apartment building know each other well.

options.text: He was kind and cared about all living things
He was afraid the mice would bite him
He thought the mice would fix the clock

comprehensionQuestions.question: How would you feel if you discovered a family of baby animals had made their home somewhere important?

options.text: I'd want to find them a better, safer home
I'd feel worried about what to do to help everyone
I'd feel happy that they trusted that place to be safe

options.text: The mice will love their new home and the clock will work again
The mice will be scared and run away from the tower
The clock will start working but make strange sounds

m8.title: The Giggling Attic
m8.obstacle: giggles come from the attic every night but nothing is up there

pages.label: Page 1
pages.text: Every night, Suki heard soft giggles floating down from the attic above her bedroom. The sounds were gentle and musical, like tiny bells ringing. But whenever Mama or Papa checked the dusty attic with their bright flashlight, they found nothing but old boxes and cobwebs.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: A cozy bedroom at night with a young girl lying in bed looking up at the ceiling, moonlight streaming through the window, with wavy lines suggesting giggles coming from above

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Happy laughing sounds that are light and cheerful.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Light, musical laughter that bubbles up when something is delightfully funny or joyful.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The baby made giggles when his sister played peek-a-boo.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: The space at the very top of a house.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: A room or storage space located directly under the roof of a house.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: We found old photo albums stored in the attic.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Sounding pretty and pleasant like music or singing.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Having a beautiful, melodic quality that sounds harmonious and pleasing to the ear.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The wind chimes made musical sounds in the breeze.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Old spider webs that are dusty and sticky.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Tangled, dusty webs left behind by spiders in corners and forgotten places.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The old barn was full of cobwebs hanging from the ceiling.

pages.label: Page 2
pages.text: "There's nothing up there, sweetpea," Mama would say, tucking Suki's rainbow quilt under her chin. But Suki knew what she heard. The giggles were real, and they happened every single night when the moon was high and the house was quiet.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Mama tucking Suki into bed with a colorful rainbow quilt, both looking up toward the ceiling, the room bathed in soft lamplight

vocabWords.definition_4_7: A loving nickname that means someone is sweet and special.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: An affectionate term of endearment used to show someone is cherished and beloved.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: Grandma called her grandson sweetpea when she hugged him.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Wrapping blankets snugly around someone in bed.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Carefully arranging covers around someone to make them warm, cozy, and secure.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: Dad was tucking his daughter into bed with her favorite blanket.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: A colorful arc in the sky with many bright colors.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: A beautiful curved band of colors that appears in the sky after rain.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: After the storm, a rainbow appeared over the mountains.

pages.label: Page 3
pages.text: One evening, Suki decided to solve the mystery herself. She put on her fuzzy purple slippers and grabbed her small pink flashlight. Her heart thumped like a drum, but she took a deep breath and tiptoed toward the hallway where the attic ladder waited.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Suki standing in her pajamas and purple slippers, holding a small pink flashlight, looking determined as she approaches a pull-down attic ladder in a dimly lit hallway

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Something puzzling that you want to figure out.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: A puzzling situation or problem that needs to be solved or explained.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The missing cookies from the jar was a mystery.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Soft and fluffy like fur or cotton.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Having a soft, fluffy texture that feels pleasant and cozy to touch.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The fuzzy kitten felt so soft when she petted it.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Beat hard and fast like a drum.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Pounded or beat with strong, rhythmic pulses that could be felt or heard.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: His heart thumped loudly after running up the stairs.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Walked very quietly on the tips of your toes.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Moved silently and carefully by walking on the very ends of your toes.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: She tiptoed past the baby's room so she wouldn't wake him.

pages.label: Page 4
pages.text: The wooden ladder creaked as Suki climbed up, up, up into the dark attic. Dust tickled her nose, and the air smelled like old books and forgotten treasures. She shined her flashlight around, seeing familiar boxes and trunks, but still heard those mysterious giggles echoing softly.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Suki climbing up wooden ladder steps with her flashlight beam cutting through the darkness, illuminating dusty boxes and old furniture in the attic space above

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Made a squeaky noise like old wood does.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Produced a groaning, squeaky sound that old wood makes when it bends.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The old door creaked when she opened it slowly.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Touched lightly and made you want to sneeze or laugh.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Lightly brushed against something in a way that caused a tingling sensation.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The feather tickled his arm and made him giggle.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Special things that are valuable and important to someone.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Precious objects or belongings that hold great value or special meaning.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The pirate buried his treasures on a secret island.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Strange and puzzling in a way that's hard to understand.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Full of mystery and wonder, difficult to explain or understand completely.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The mysterious footprints in the snow led to the forest.

pages.label: Page 5
pages.text: Following the gentle giggles, Suki walked carefully across the creaky attic floor. Her flashlight beam danced over old picture frames and Christmas decorations. The giggles grew louder and happier, coming from behind a tall, carved wooden trunk covered in a lacy white sheet.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Suki walking through the attic with her flashlight beam revealing old picture frames, holiday decorations, and a large trunk covered with a white lacy sheet in the background

vocabWords.definition_4_7: A bright line of light that shines from something.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: A strong ray or stream of light that travels in one direction.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The lighthouse beam helped ships find their way at night.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Moved around in a lively and graceful way.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Moved with rhythm and grace, often jumping or swaying in patterns.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The leaves danced in the wind outside her window.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Cut into wood or stone to make a shape or design.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Skillfully cut and shaped material to create decorative patterns or figures.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The artist carved a beautiful bird from a piece of wood.

pages.label: Page 6
pages.text: With trembling fingers, Suki pulled away the dusty sheet. Inside the open trunk, she discovered something wonderful - three tiny fairy dolls with gossamer wings and sparkling dresses. They were having a tea party with acorn caps and dewdrop refreshments, giggling merrily at their own jokes.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Suki pulling away a white sheet to reveal an open trunk containing three small, glittering fairy dolls having a miniature tea party with tiny cups and acorn caps, all sparkling in the flashlight beam

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Shaking a little bit because you're nervous or excited.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Shaking slightly with small, quick movements, often from emotion or anticipation.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: Her hands were trembling as she opened the surprise gift.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Very thin and light like spider webs or fairy wings.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Extremely delicate and fine, like the thinnest silk or morning spider webs.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The gossamer threads of the spider web sparkled with dew.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Shining with tiny points of light that twinkle and shine.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Glittering brightly with small flashes of light that dance and shimmer.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The sparkling snow looked like diamonds in the sunlight.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Small snacks and drinks served at a party or gathering.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Light food and beverages offered to guests to refresh and nourish them.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The birthday party had refreshments like juice and cookies.

pages.label: Page 7
pages.text: "Oh!" gasped Suki softly. The three fairies looked up with twinkling eyes and warm smiles. "We're sorry if we kept you awake," said the fairy in the blue dress. "We only come out to play when the house is peaceful and quiet."
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Close-up of Suki's surprised but delighted face looking down at three tiny fairies in colorful dresses (blue, pink, and yellow) who are looking up at her with friendly expressions, surrounded by a soft, magical glow

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Took a quick, surprised breath when something was shocking.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Drew in breath sharply and suddenly, usually from surprise or amazement.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: She gasped when she saw the beautiful rainbow after the storm.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Shining with little flashes of light like stars do.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Sparkling with quick, bright flashes of light that appear and disappear rapidly.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The twinkling stars filled the dark night sky.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Calm, quiet, and not disturbed by noise or trouble.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Tranquil and serene, free from disturbance or conflict, filled with calm.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The peaceful garden was a perfect place to read a book.

pages.label: Page 8
pages.text: "We've been living in this trunk for many, many years," explained the fairy in the pink dress, her voice like tinkling wind chimes. "Your grandmother used to visit us when she was a little girl, just like you. We've been hoping you'd be brave enough to find us."
pages.scene: illustration prompt: The pink-dressed fairy speaking animatedly while the other two fairies nod, with Suki listening intently, surrounded by a warm golden glow from the flashlight and fairy magic

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Told someone about something to help them understand it.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Described something clearly and in detail to make it easier to understand.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The teacher explained how plants grow from tiny seeds.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Making light, pretty sounds like small bells ringing.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Producing delicate, musical sounds like gentle bells or chimes in the breeze.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The tinkling sound of the music box made her smile.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Musical instruments that make pretty ringing sounds when touched.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Hollow metal or wooden tubes that create melodious tones when struck by wind.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The wind chimes on the porch made beautiful music.

pages.label: Page 9
pages.text: The fairy in the yellow dress clapped her tiny hands with joy. "Would you like to have tea with us sometime? We promise to giggle more quietly so you can sleep." Suki beamed with happiness and nodded eagerly. She had solved the mystery and made three magical new friends!
pages.scene: illustration prompt: All three fairies clapping happily while Suki smiles broadly, with the tea party setup visible between them, creating a scene of joy and friendship in the magical attic setting

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Put her hands together quickly to make a happy sound.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Struck her palms together repeatedly to show excitement, approval, or joy.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: Everyone clapped when the magician pulled a rabbit from his hat.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Smiled very big and bright because she was so happy.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Displayed a radiant, joyful smile that showed great happiness and delight.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: She beamed with pride when her drawing won the contest.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: In an excited way, really wanting something to happen.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: With great enthusiasm and anticipation, showing keen interest and excitement.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The children eagerly waited for the ice cream truck to arrive.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Special and wonderful like something from a fairy tale.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Possessing an enchanting, mysterious quality that seems supernatural and wonderful.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The magical sunset painted the sky in brilliant colors.

pages.label: Page 10
pages.text: From that night on, Suki would visit her fairy friends whenever she felt brave and curious. They shared stories, played gentle games, and yes, still giggled together - but now Suki giggled right along with them. The attic had become her favorite place for magical adventures.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Suki sitting cross-legged in the attic having a cozy tea party with the three fairies, all laughing together, with the scene bathed in warm, magical starlight streaming through a small attic window

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Wanting to learn about things and ask lots of questions.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Having a strong desire to explore, discover, and understand new things around you.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The curious cat investigated every corner of the new house.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Exciting experiences where you explore and discover new things.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Thrilling journeys or experiences filled with discovery, excitement, and wonder.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The family went on adventures hiking through the mountain trails.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: The thing you like best out of all your choices.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Something you prefer above all others because it brings you special joy.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: Chocolate chip cookies are my favorite treat to eat.

comprehensionQuestions.question: What did Suki find when she looked inside the wooden trunk?

options.text: Three tiny fairy dolls having a tea party
Old books and dusty picture frames
Christmas decorations and cobwebs

comprehensionQuestions.question: Why do you think Suki's parents couldn't find anything when they checked the attic?

options.text: The fairies only came out when the house was peaceful and quiet
The attic was too dusty for them to see clearly
They were looking in the wrong places

comprehensionQuestions.question: How do you think you would feel if you discovered magical fairies in your attic?

options.text: Excited and amazed to meet magical creatures
Happy to have new friends to play with
Curious about their magical world

predictionPause.question: What do you think Suki will discover when she pulls away the dusty sheet?

options.text: The source of the mysterious giggles
Something magical and wonderful
New friends waiting to meet her

m9.title: Paws and Clues
m9.obstacle: his dog keeps digging up strange old objects in the yard

pages.label: Page 1
pages.text: Jake loved playing in his backyard with his golden retriever, Buster. The warm summer sun filtered through the oak tree's green leaves, and the sweet smell of Mom's roses filled the air. But lately, Buster had been acting strange. Every morning, the playful dog would sniff around the yard with his nose pressed close to the ground, then start digging furiously in different spots.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: A sunny backyard with a large oak tree, colorful rose bushes, and a golden retriever sniffing the ground while a boy with messy brown hair watches nearby

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Light came through something in a soft gentle way.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: When light passes through something and becomes softer and more pleasant, like sunshine through leaves.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The moonlight filtered through the curtains into her bedroom.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: A type of dog that is good at fetching things.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: A friendly breed of dog that was originally trained to bring back things for hunters.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The retriever brought the tennis ball back to its owner.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Doing something very fast and with lots of energy.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Acting with intense speed and energy, like a whirlwind of activity that won't slow down.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: She furiously pedaled her bike to catch up with her friends.

pages.label: Page 2
pages.text: This morning was no different. Buster's tail wagged excitedly as he dug near the garden shed, dirt flying everywhere. "Buster, stop making such a mess!" Jake called out. But then something shiny caught his eye in the fresh hole. Jake knelt down and carefully brushed away the cool, damp earth. His fingers touched something smooth and round – an old brass button with intricate designs carved into its surface.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Jake kneeling by a hole in the ground, holding up an ornate brass button while Buster sits nearby with dirt on his paws and nose, looking proud

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Having lots of tiny details and fancy patterns.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Made with very detailed and complicated designs that show careful, skillful work by the creator.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The intricate snowflake had dozens of perfect tiny branches.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: A shiny yellow metal that looks like gold.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: A golden-colored metal made by mixing copper and zinc, often used for decorative objects.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The brass trumpet gleamed under the stage lights.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: The top part of something that you can touch.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: The outside layer or top of an object that you can see and feel with your hands.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The surface of the lake was perfectly smooth and calm.

pages.label: Page 3
pages.text: Jake ran inside to show his mom the mysterious button. She examined it carefully, turning it over in her palm. "This looks very old, sweetie," she said with a smile. "Maybe from the 1800s! I wonder what else might be buried out there." Jake's eyes lit up with curiosity. He decided to become a detective and solve the mystery of the buried treasures. But first, he needed to convince Buster to help him search more carefully.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Jake and his mom in a bright kitchen, both looking at the brass button on the table, with Mom pointing to details on it while Jake listens intently

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Looked at something very carefully to learn about it.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Studied something closely by looking at all its parts to understand what it is or how it works.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The doctor examined the patient's sore throat with a flashlight.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Something strange that you don't understand or know about.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Puzzling and hard to explain, like a secret that makes you curious and want to learn more.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The mysterious footprints in the snow led into the dark forest.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: The feeling of wanting to know more about something.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: A strong desire to learn and discover new things, like an inner spark that drives exploration.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: Her curiosity about dinosaurs led her to read every book about them.

pages.label: Page 4
pages.text: The next day, Jake armed himself with a small shovel, a brush, and a notebook to record his findings. He followed Buster around the yard, watching where the dog showed the most interest. Near the old wooden fence, Buster began pawing at the ground and whimpering softly. Jake carefully dug where Buster indicated and found a tarnished silver spoon with delicate flower patterns. "Good boy, Buster!" Jake praised, giving his furry partner a treat.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Jake sitting cross-legged on grass with a notebook and digging tools beside him, holding up a silver spoon while Buster wags his tail expectantly for a treat

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Got ready with tools or things you need.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Prepared yourself with the right equipment and supplies needed to accomplish a specific task successfully.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: She armed herself with warm clothes before going outside in the snow.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Showed or pointed to where something was.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Gave a sign or signal that pointed out a specific location or provided important information.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The compass indicated that north was in that direction.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Lost its shine and became dull or dark.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Became discolored and less shiny over time, usually because air and moisture changed the metal's surface.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The tarnished silver ring needed polishing to look bright again.

pages.label: Page 5
pages.text: Day after day, Jake and Buster continued their treasure hunt, but some days they found nothing at all. Jake felt frustrated when they spent hours digging empty holes. His knees got muddy, his hands grew sore, and doubt crept into his mind. "Maybe there's nothing else to find," he sighed, sitting on the back porch steps. But Buster nudged Jake's hand with his wet nose, as if encouraging him not to give up on their adventure.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Jake sitting discouraged on wooden porch steps with his chin in his hands, while Buster gently nuzzles his arm, with empty holes visible in the yard behind them

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Feeling upset when things don't work out right.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: The annoying feeling you get when you're trying hard but can't achieve what you want to accomplish.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: He felt frustrated when the puzzle piece wouldn't fit anywhere.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Not being sure if something is true or right.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: A feeling of uncertainty that makes you question whether something will work or is worth continuing.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: She had doubt about whether she could climb the tall mountain.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Helped someone feel better and want to keep trying.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Gave someone support and confidence to continue working toward their goal despite difficulties they faced.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The coach encouraged the team to practice harder before the big game.

pages.label: Page 6
pages.text: Inspired by Buster's determination, Jake decided to try a different approach. He went to the library and researched his neighborhood's history. The librarian, Mrs. Chen, helped him find old maps and photographs. Jake learned that a general store had stood on his property over 100 years ago! The faded photographs showed people in old-fashioned clothes standing in front of a wooden building right where his house now stood.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Jake and a kind elderly librarian looking at old black and white photographs and maps spread across a library table, with tall bookshelves in the background

vocabWords.definition_4_7: The strong feeling of not giving up on something.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: The powerful inner strength that keeps you working hard toward a goal even when things get difficult.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: With determination, she practiced piano every day until she could play the song perfectly.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Came closer to something or tried a different way.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Either moved toward something physically or tried a new method for solving a problem or completing a task.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: She approached the shy kitten slowly so she wouldn't scare it away.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Old things made by people long ago.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Objects created by humans in the past that help us understand how people used to live and work.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The museum displayed artifacts from ancient Egypt, including pottery and jewelry.

pages.label: Page 7
pages.text: Armed with this new knowledge, Jake returned home with renewed excitement. He studied the old photographs carefully and compared them to his backyard. If the store had been here, there might be more artifacts buried around the foundation! Jake marked several promising spots on a hand-drawn map. Buster seemed to sense Jake's enthusiasm and began sniffing around the areas Jake had marked, his tail wagging with anticipation.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Jake in his backyard holding an old photograph, comparing it to his surroundings, while Buster explores the areas Jake has marked with small flags or sticks

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Feeling very excited and happy about doing something.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: A burst of joyful energy and excitement that makes you eager to dive into an activity or project.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: Her enthusiasm for soccer made her wake up early for every practice.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: The bottom part of a building that goes underground.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: The solid base structure buried beneath a building that supports all the weight of the construction above it.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: Workers dug deep holes and poured concrete for the house's foundation.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: The excited feeling of waiting for something good.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: The thrilling emotion you feel when you're eagerly looking forward to something wonderful that's about to happen.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: She waited in anticipation for her birthday party to begin.

pages.label: Page 8
pages.text: The breakthrough came on a cloudy Saturday morning. Near where the old store's back door would have been, Buster began digging frantically, his excitement contagious. This time, Jake found something much larger – a small wooden box, weathered but still intact. His heart pounded as he carefully lifted it from the earth. The box felt surprisingly heavy, and he could hear something rattling softly inside when he gently shook it.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Jake carefully holding a weathered wooden box while kneeling beside a deeper hole, with Buster sitting alertly nearby and gray clouds overhead

vocabWords.definition_4_7: An important discovery that solves a big problem.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: A major success or discovery that happens after working hard and opens up exciting new possibilities.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The scientist made a breakthrough that could help cure the disease.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Doing something very fast because you're worried or excited.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Acting with wild, hurried movements driven by strong emotions like fear, excitement, or desperate urgency.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: She frantically searched through her backpack for her lost homework.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Something that spreads from one person to another.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: A feeling or mood that easily spreads to other people around you, like happiness or excitement jumping between friends.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: Her laughter was so contagious that soon everyone in the room was giggling.

pages.label: Page 9
pages.text: With trembling hands, Jake slowly opened the creaky lid. Inside, wrapped in faded cloth, were more treasures: old coins, a child's marble, a small brass key, and a folded piece of paper. Jake carefully unfolded the brittle paper and gasped. It was a hand-written note that read: "For whoever finds this treasure box, these belonged to the Murphy family store, 1887. May they bring you as much joy as they brought us."
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Jake opening the wooden box with various old treasures visible inside, holding up the old note while Buster looks on curiously, golden afternoon light streaming through trees

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Shaking because you're nervous, scared, or excited.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: When your body shakes slightly from strong emotions like fear, excitement, or cold that you can't control.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: His hands were trembling as he opened the important letter.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Something old and dry that breaks easily.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: So old, dry, and fragile that it might crack or crumble into pieces if you're not very gentle with it.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The brittle autumn leaves crumbled when she stepped on them.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Changed and worn down by wind, rain, and sun.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Damaged and aged by years of exposure to outdoor elements like storms, sunshine, and changing seasons.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The weathered barn looked old and gray after decades of harsh winters.

pages.label: Page 10
pages.text: Jake realized that by never giving up, even when the digging got tough and the holes came up empty, he had uncovered a real piece of history. He and Buster had solved the mystery together! That evening, Jake carefully cleaned each treasure and arranged them in a display case. He learned that persistence and teamwork – even with a four-legged partner – could lead to amazing discoveries. Buster curled up beside him, tired but happy from their successful adventure.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Jake in his room arranging the cleaned treasures in a display case on his desk, with Buster sleeping contentedly on a rug nearby, warm lamplight making everything cozy

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Never giving up even when things get really hard.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: The quality of continuing to work toward your goal no matter how many obstacles or setbacks you face.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: Through persistence, she finally learned to ride her bike without training wheels.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Working together with others to get something done.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: The cooperative effort of people combining their different skills and strengths to achieve a shared goal successfully.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The class project required teamwork from every student to build the solar system model.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: New things you find or learn that nobody knew before.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Exciting findings or knowledge that reveal something previously unknown and open up new understanding about the world.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The explorer's discoveries in the cave included rare crystals and underground rivers.

comprehensionQuestions.question: What did Jake find first when Buster was digging in the backyard?

options.text: An old brass button with carved designs
A tarnished silver spoon with flowers
A wooden box full of treasures

comprehensionQuestions.question: Why do you think Buster knew where to dig for the buried items?

options.text: Dogs have an excellent sense of smell and could detect the old metal
Buster had seen the items being buried there
Someone had trained Buster to find treasure

comprehensionQuestions.question: How would you feel if you discovered buried treasure in your own backyard?

options.text: Amazed and excited like a real detective
Curious about who buried it there
Proud that I didn't give up searching

predictionPause.question: Jake found a heavy wooden box buried in the ground - what do you think might be inside it?

options.text: Old coins and jewelry from the general store
A treasure map leading to even more buried items
Letters and photographs from the Murphy family

m10.title: The Mixed-Up Mail
m10.obstacle: all the mail in the forest got mixed up and nobody got the right letter

pages.label: Page 1
pages.text: Possum loved to help. Every morning, he sorted mail for all his forest friends. The letters smelled like pine trees and sunshine.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: A friendly gray possum wearing a small mail carrier hat, sitting beside a wooden mailbox surrounded by tall pine trees, with golden morning sunlight streaming through the branches

vocabWords.definition_2_4: Put things in the right places.
vocabWords.definition_4_7: Putting things in order or the right groups.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Organizing items by separating them into categories or putting them where they belong.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: Mom sorted the toys into different bins.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: When things are spread out everywhere in messy ways.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Items that have been thrown or blown around so they're lying in different places.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The leaves scattered across the yard when the wind blew.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: A type of evergreen tree with sharp needle leaves.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: A cone-bearing tree that stays green all year and has thin, pointed leaves called needles.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: We found pinecones under the tall pine tree.

pages.label: Page 2
pages.text: Oh no! The wind blew hard last night. All the letters scattered everywhere! They stuck to trees and bushes.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Letters and envelopes scattered all around the forest, stuck to tree branches, caught in bushes, and lying on the mossy ground with leaves swirling around

vocabWords.definition_4_7: When things get spread out all over the place.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Items that have been thrown around or spread widely in different directions by force.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The papers scattered when I dropped my folder.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: When something is attached and won't come off easily.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Fixed in place so firmly that it's difficult or impossible to remove or move.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: My shoe got stuck in the mud.

vocabWords.definition_2_4: Small plants with lots of leaves.
vocabWords.definition_4_7: Small woody plants that are smaller than trees.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Low-growing plants with multiple stems and branches that are bigger than flowers but smaller than trees.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The berries grew on bushes in our backyard.

pages.label: Page 3
pages.text: Possum found the letters. But they were all mixed up! Rabbit got Bear's letter. Squirrel got Owl's letter.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Possum holding up different colored envelopes, looking puzzled, with a small pile of mixed-up mail at his feet on the soft forest floor

vocabWords.definition_4_7: When things get put together in the wrong way.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Combined or jumbled together so that items are no longer in their proper order or place.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The puzzle pieces got mixed up in the box.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: A small furry animal with long ears that jumps.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: A small mammal with soft fur, long ears, and powerful hind legs for hopping around.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The rabbit ate carrots from our garden.

vocabWords.definition_2_4: A small animal with a big fluffy tail.
vocabWords.definition_4_7: A small animal that climbs trees and eats nuts.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: A small, quick mammal with a bushy tail that lives in trees and collects nuts for winter.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The squirrel buried acorns in the yard.

pages.label: Page 4
pages.text: Rabbit felt sad. Her letter was not for her. 'This is Bear's birthday party invitation,' she said softly.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: A brown rabbit with droopy ears looking disappointed while holding a large envelope, sitting on a tree stump with wildflowers around her

vocabWords.definition_2_4: A special letter asking you to come.
vocabWords.definition_4_7: A letter that asks someone to come to a party.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: A written request asking someone to attend a special event like a party or celebration.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: I got an invitation to my friend's birthday party.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: The special day each year when you celebrate getting older.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: The anniversary of the day someone was born, usually celebrated with cake and presents each year.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: We sang songs at grandpa's birthday celebration.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Speaking or doing something in a very quiet way.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Done with a gentle, quiet manner that is barely loud enough to hear clearly.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: She softly sang a lullaby to the baby.

pages.label: Page 5
pages.text: Squirrel felt worried too. 'This letter has big words I cannot read,' he chattered. His fluffy tail drooped down.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: A red squirrel with a bushy tail looking confused while holding an official-looking letter, perched on a oak branch with acorns scattered nearby

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Feeling nervous or upset about what might happen.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Having anxious thoughts or concerns about something that might go wrong or cause problems.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: Dad looked worried when it started raining during our picnic.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Talked quickly in short, fast bursts of words.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Spoke rapidly and continuously, often in an excited or nervous way with quick, short sounds.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The children chattered excitedly about their field trip.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Bent or hung down because of being sad or tired.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Sagged or hung downward in a limp way, often showing sadness or lack of energy.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The flowers drooped when we forgot to water them.

pages.label: Page 6
pages.text: Possum had an idea! 'I will help you,' he said kindly. 'Let's find everyone's right letters together.'
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Possum smiling warmly with his arms outstretched in a welcoming gesture, standing between Rabbit and Squirrel in a sunny forest clearing

vocabWords.definition_2_4: A thought about what to do.
vocabWords.definition_4_7: A new thought or plan about how to solve something.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: A mental picture or plan that comes to mind as a possible solution to a problem.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: I had a great idea for making our fort stronger.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Done with care and gentleness toward other people.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: In a warm, caring manner that shows consideration and goodness toward others' feelings.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The teacher kindly helped me tie my shoes.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Working or being with other people at the same time.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: In unity or cooperation with others, sharing the same space or activity at once.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: We built the sandcastle together at the beach.

pages.label: Page 7
pages.text: They walked through the forest. Possum looked at each name carefully. He gave Rabbit her seed catalog with pretty flowers.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Possum, Rabbit, and Squirrel walking along a winding forest path, with Possum holding up a colorful catalog showing flower pictures to a happy Rabbit

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Doing something slowly and paying close attention to details.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Taking time and effort to be precise and thorough while avoiding mistakes or harm.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: She carefully carried the eggs so they wouldn't break.

vocabWords.definition_2_4: A book with pictures of things to buy.
vocabWords.definition_4_7: A book that shows pictures and information about products.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: A booklet or magazine containing organized listings of items with pictures and descriptions for shopping.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: Mom looked through the toy catalog to find my birthday present.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: The colorful, beautiful parts that grow on plants and trees.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: The reproductive parts of plants that are often brightly colored and fragrant to attract insects.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: We picked flowers from the garden for grandma.

pages.label: Page 8
pages.text: Next, Possum found Squirrel's letter. It was from his grandma! She sent warm cookies and hugs in her words.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Squirrel jumping with joy while holding a sweet letter, with Possum smiling nearby, and you can almost see cookie crumbs and heart shapes floating around the letter

vocabWords.definition_4_7: The mother of one of your parents, a special older lady.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Your parent's mother who often gives hugs, tells stories, and shares wisdom with grandchildren.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: Grandma taught me how to bake chocolate chip cookies.

vocabWords.definition_2_4: Sweet treats that are round and yummy.
vocabWords.definition_4_7: Small, sweet baked treats that are usually round and crispy.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Small, flat baked goods made with flour, sugar, and other ingredients that taste sweet and delicious.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: We shared warm cookies and milk after dinner.

vocabWords.definition_2_4: Wrapping your arms around someone with love.
vocabWords.definition_4_7: When you put your arms around someone to show you care.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: A physical embrace where people wrap their arms around each other to express love and comfort.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: Mom gives the best hugs when I feel sad.

pages.label: Page 9
pages.text: Bear got his party invitation. Owl got her important news. Everyone had their right letters at last!
pages.scene: illustration prompt: A big brown bear holding a colorful party invitation and a wise owl with glasses reading an official letter, both looking very pleased in the dappled forest light

vocabWords.definition_2_4: A special note asking you to come play.
vocabWords.definition_4_7: A message that asks someone to come to a special event.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: A formal or friendly request for someone to attend a gathering, party, or important occasion.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The invitation had colorful balloons drawn on it.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Something that really matters and needs attention right away.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Having great significance or value, deserving serious consideration because it affects important things in life.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: It's important to look both ways before crossing the street.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: New information about things that are happening around us.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Current information about recent events, changes, or developments that people want or need to know.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: Dad shared the good news that we're going to the zoo.

pages.label: Page 10
pages.text: All the forest friends gathered around Possum. 'Thank you for being so kind!' they said. Possum felt warm inside.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: All the forest animals - Rabbit, Squirrel, Bear, Owl, and others - forming a circle around a beaming Possum in a sunny meadow filled with colorful wildflowers

vocabWords.definition_4_7: When people or things come together in the same spot.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Assembled or collected in one location, bringing separate individuals or items into a unified group.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: All the children gathered around the storyteller.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: In the area near something or forming a circle.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Positioned in the vicinity of something or arranged in a circular pattern surrounding an object.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: We sat around the campfire to stay warm.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: The way you feel deep in your heart and body.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: An internal feeling or sensation that comes from within your emotions, heart, or inner self.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: I felt happy inside when I helped my little brother.

sc1.title: The Seed That Grew Overnight
sc1.obstacle: she planted a seed and it grew into something enormous overnight

pages.label: Page 1
pages.text: Daisy found a tiny seed in her garden. It was brown and smooth in her small hand.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: A young girl with pigtails kneeling in a sunny garden, holding a small brown seed in her open palm, with colorful flowers and green grass around her

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Something so small you can barely see it.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: So small that it's much smaller than most things you see every day.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The tiny ant carried a crumb across the sidewalk.

vocabWords.definition_2_4: A little thing that grows into a plant.
vocabWords.definition_4_7: The small part of a plant that grows into a new plant.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: A small hard part that contains everything needed to grow into a whole new plant.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: We planted pumpkin seeds in our classroom garden.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: A special place where people grow flowers and vegetables.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: An outdoor area where people plant and take care of flowers, vegetables, or other plants.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: Grandpa grows tomatoes and carrots in his garden.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Something that feels flat and soft when you touch it.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Having a surface that feels even and pleasant to touch, without any bumps or rough spots.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The smooth stone felt nice in my pocket.

pages.label: Page 2
pages.text: She dug a little hole with her finger. Down went the seed into the soft, dark dirt.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Daisy poking a small hole in rich, dark soil with her finger, the tiny seed visible next to the hole, garden tools scattered nearby

vocabWords.definition_2_4: Made a hole in the dirt.
vocabWords.definition_4_7: Used your hands or tools to make a hole in the ground.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Moved dirt away with your hands or a tool to create a hollow space in the earth.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The dog dug a hole to bury his bone.

vocabWords.definition_2_4: One of the long parts on your hand.
vocabWords.definition_4_7: One of the five long parts that stick out from your hand.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: One of the five slender parts at the end of your hand that you use for touching and grabbing.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: I pointed with my finger at the airplane in the sky.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Something that feels gentle and comfortable when you touch it.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Having a texture that feels pleasant and yields easily when you press or touch it.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The kitten's fur was soft like a fluffy pillow.

pages.label: Page 3
pages.text: Daisy sprinkled water on the dirt. The drops sparkled like tiny diamonds in the sun.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Daisy holding a small watering can, water drops catching sunlight as they fall onto the planted spot, creating sparkles and tiny puddles

vocabWords.definition_2_4: Put little drops of water on something.
vocabWords.definition_4_7: Scattered small amounts of something gently over an area.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Lightly scattered small drops or bits of something over a surface in a gentle way.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: Mom sprinkled cheese on top of the pizza.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Small round pieces of water that fall or sit on things.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Tiny round portions of liquid that form when water falls or gathers in small amounts.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: Rain drops made puddles on the playground.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Gave off tiny flashes of bright light that twinkled.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Reflected light in quick, bright flashes that created a twinkling or glittering effect.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The snow sparkled in the moonlight like glitter.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Special stones that shine and sparkle with beautiful light.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Precious gems that are very clear and reflect light in brilliant, dazzling ways.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The princess wore a crown with diamonds that caught the light.

pages.label: Page 4
pages.text: That night, Daisy went to sleep. She dreamed of green leaves and yellow flowers.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Daisy sleeping peacefully in her bed by a window, with a thought bubble showing green plants and bright yellow flowers, moonlight streaming through the window

vocabWords.definition_2_4: Saw pictures in her mind while sleeping.
vocabWords.definition_4_7: Had thoughts and pictures in her mind while she slept.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Experienced images and stories in her mind during sleep that felt like watching a movie.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: Last night I dreamed I could fly like a bird.

vocabWords.definition_2_4: The green flat parts that grow on plants.
vocabWords.definition_4_7: The flat green parts that grow on branches and stems.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: The flat, usually green parts of plants that grow from stems and help make food from sunlight.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: In fall, the leaves turn orange and red before falling down.

vocabWords.definition_2_4: The pretty colored parts of plants.
vocabWords.definition_4_7: The colorful, beautiful parts that bloom on plants and smell nice.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: The colorful, often fragrant parts of plants that bloom to attract insects and eventually make seeds.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: We picked flowers to make a bouquet for teacher.

pages.label: Page 5
pages.text: In the morning, Daisy ran outside. Something HUGE was in her garden!
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Daisy in pajamas running out her back door, eyes wide with surprise, looking at an enormous plant towering over her house and garden

vocabWords.definition_2_4: The time when you wake up and it gets light.
vocabWords.definition_4_7: The early part of the day when the sun comes up.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: The time of day from when the sun rises until about noon when people start their day.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: We eat breakfast every morning before going to school.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Much much bigger than normal things you usually see.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Extremely large, much bigger than you would expect something to be in normal situations.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The elephant at the zoo was huge compared to the rabbits.

vocabWords.definition_2_4: Not inside a house or building.
vocabWords.definition_4_7: In the open air, not inside a building or room.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: In the outdoor area beyond the walls of a house, building, or enclosed space.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: We played outside in the backyard after lunch.

pages.label: Page 6
pages.text: A giant plant reached up to the clouds! Its leaves were bigger than umbrellas.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: A massive beanstalk-like plant stretching high into fluffy white clouds, with enormous green leaves casting shadows over the garden and house below

vocabWords.definition_2_4: Something way way bigger than normal.
vocabWords.definition_4_7: Much bigger than things usually are, like in fairy tales.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Extremely large in size, much bigger than what you would normally expect to see.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The giant pumpkin at the fair was bigger than our car.

vocabWords.definition_2_4: Went up high to touch something.
vocabWords.definition_4_7: Stretched or extended up high to get to something far away.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Extended upward or outward to touch or get to something that was far away or high up.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: I reached up high to get the book from the tall shelf.

vocabWords.definition_2_4: The fluffy white things in the sky.
vocabWords.definition_4_7: The white or gray fluffy things that float in the sky.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Collections of tiny water drops that float high in the sky and sometimes bring rain.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The airplane flew through the white puffy clouds.

vocabWords.definition_2_4: Things that keep rain off you.
vocabWords.definition_4_7: Round things you hold over your head to stay dry.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Collapsible canopies on long handles that people hold overhead to protect themselves from rain or sun.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: We opened our umbrellas when it started to rain.

pages.label: Page 7
pages.text: Birds made nests in the big branches. Squirrels played on the giant leaves.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Various birds building nests in the huge branches while gray squirrels chase each other across the enormous leaves, creating a bustling animal community

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Round homes that birds build in trees for their babies.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Cozy homes that birds construct from twigs and soft materials to lay eggs and raise their young.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: We found a bird nest with three blue eggs in our apple tree.

vocabWords.definition_2_4: The long arms that stick out from trees.
vocabWords.definition_4_7: The thick arms that grow out from tree trunks and hold leaves.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: The woody arms that extend from a tree's trunk and divide into smaller parts that support leaves and fruit.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The monkey swung from branch to branch in the jungle.

vocabWords.definition_2_4: Furry animals with big fluffy tails.
vocabWords.definition_4_7: Small furry animals that live in trees and eat nuts.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Quick, bushy-tailed animals that live in trees and are known for gathering and hiding nuts for winter.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The squirrels buried acorns in our yard for winter food.

pages.label: Page 8
pages.text: The plant was so tall it blocked the sun. Daisy's other flowers looked sad and droopy.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: The giant plant casting a large shadow over the garden, with smaller flowers wilting and drooping in the shade, looking pale and sad

vocabWords.definition_2_4: Got in the way of something.
vocabWords.definition_4_7: Stopped something from getting through by being in the way.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Prevented something from passing through by creating a barrier or obstacle in its path.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The big truck blocked the road so no cars could pass.

vocabWords.definition_2_4: Hanging down sadly like they're tired.
vocabWords.definition_4_7: Hanging down low because they don't have enough water or sun.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Bending or hanging downward in a sad way, usually because plants need more water or sunlight.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The houseplant looked droopy because we forgot to water it.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Having a height that is much more than usual.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Having great height, extending upward much more than is typical for similar things.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The giraffe is so tall it can eat leaves from high tree branches.

pages.label: Page 9
pages.text: Daisy patted the giant stem gently. 'You need more space to grow,' she whispered softly.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Daisy placing her small hand tenderly on the massive plant stem, speaking to it with a caring expression, surrounded by the shadowed garden

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Touched something gently several times with her open hand.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Lightly touched something repeatedly with the flat of her hand in a caring, gentle way.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: She patted the puppy's head to show she liked him.

vocabWords.definition_2_4: The main part of a plant that holds it up.
vocabWords.definition_4_7: The thick main part of a plant that holds up the leaves.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: The main supporting structure of a plant that carries water and nutrients from roots to leaves.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The rose had thorns all along its green stem.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: In a soft, careful way that doesn't hurt anything.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: In a tender, careful manner that shows kindness and avoids causing any harm or discomfort.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The nurse gently cleaned the cut on my knee.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Spoke in a very soft, quiet voice that was hard to hear.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Spoke in an extremely quiet voice, barely making any sound so only someone very close could hear.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: Mom whispered 'good night' so she wouldn't wake the baby.

pages.label: Page 10
pages.text: With Mom's help, they moved the plant to the big field. Now everyone had room to grow!
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Daisy and her mother carefully transplanting the giant plant to a wide open field, with the sun shining on both the huge plant and the recovering garden flowers

vocabWords.definition_2_4: Took something from one place to another place.
vocabWords.definition_4_7: Changed the location of something by carrying it somewhere else.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Transported something from its current position to a different location by lifting and carrying it there.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: We moved our toys from the living room to the playroom.

vocabWords.definition_2_4: A big open space with grass.
vocabWords.definition_4_7: A large open area of land covered with grass or crops.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: A wide, open area of land that is usually flat and covered with grass or used for growing plants.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The cows graze in the green field behind the barn.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: An empty area where there is room for things to fit.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: An area or region that is available or empty, providing room for something to exist or move freely.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: There wasn't enough space in the garage for both cars.

sc2.title: Robot Best Friend
sc2.obstacle: the robot he built doesn't understand feelings

pages.label: Page 1
pages.text: Zain loved building things with his colorful blocks and shiny metal pieces. Today, he had a special project in his sunny bedroom. With careful hands, he connected wires and bolts to create something amazing. "I'm going to build my very own robot friend!" he said with excitement.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: A cheerful boy with dark hair sitting on a colorful rug in his bedroom, surrounded by building blocks, metal pieces, and wires. Sunlight streams through the window as he works on his creation.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Having lots of different bright colors mixed together.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Something that has many different bright colors that make it look cheerful and interesting.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The butterfly had colorful wings with orange, yellow, and purple spots.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Joined two things together so they stick or work together.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Put two or more things together so they attach and can work as one piece.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: She connected the train cars so they could move together on the track.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: The happy feeling you get when something really fun happens.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: The wonderful, energetic feeling you have when you're looking forward to something special.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The children felt excitement when they saw the circus coming to town.

pages.label: Page 2
pages.text: After hours of careful work, Zain's robot friend came to life! The robot had bright blue lights for eyes and smooth silver arms. "Beep beep! I am R2-Friend," said the robot in a cheerful mechanical voice. Zain clapped his hands with joy at his wonderful creation.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: A small, friendly-looking silver robot with glowing blue eyes standing next to Zain. The robot has rounded edges and a kind expression. Zain is clapping excitedly with a big smile.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Sounding like it comes from a machine instead of a person.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Having the kind of sound that machines make, different from how people naturally talk.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The toy dinosaur made mechanical roaring sounds when you pressed the button.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Something new that you made or built with your hands.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Something special that you made or invented that didn't exist before you created it.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The artist's latest creation was a beautiful sculpture made from clay.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Something that feels flat and soft when you touch it.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Having a surface that feels even and pleasant to touch, without any rough spots.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The river rocks were smooth from years of water flowing over them.

pages.label: Page 3
pages.text: "Let's play together!" Zain suggested, bouncing a red rubber ball. R2-Friend caught the ball perfectly every time with precise robotic movements. But when Zain missed a catch and felt sad, R2-Friend just stood there. "Why are you making that face?" the robot asked, tilting his head curiously.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Zain looking disappointed as he misses catching a red ball, while the silver robot stands nearby looking confused and tilting his head. The robot's expression is puzzled.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: When you give someone an idea about what to do.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Offered an idea or plan to someone in a friendly way for them to think about.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: Mom suggested we have a picnic in the park on the sunny day.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Done exactly right without making any mistakes at all.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Very careful and exact, doing something perfectly the same way every single time.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The surgeon made precise cuts during the operation to help the patient.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: In a way that shows you want to know more.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Acting interested and wanting to learn or understand something that seems puzzling or new.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The cat looked curiously at the new toy mouse on the floor.

pages.label: Page 4
pages.text: The next day, Zain scraped his knee on the playground's rough concrete. Tears rolled down his cheeks as he limped over to R2-Friend. "I am hurt and it makes me sad," Zain explained. But R2-Friend just scanned him with a red light and said, "No broken parts detected."
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Zain sitting on playground equipment with a small bandage on his knee, tears in his eyes. R2-Friend is scanning him with a red beam of light, looking analytical rather than caring.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: When your skin gets hurt by rubbing against something rough.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Got a small injury where the top layer of skin came off from hitting something hard.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: I scraped my elbow when I fell off my bicycle onto the sidewalk.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: A very hard gray material used to make sidewalks and buildings.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: A strong, hard building material made from cement that's used for roads and walkways.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The workers poured wet concrete to make a new driveway for our house.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Found something by looking or checking very carefully for it.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Discovered or noticed something that might be hard to see using special tools or senses.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The smoke detector detected fire and made a loud warning sound.

pages.label: Page 5
pages.text: Zain felt lonely even though his robot was right beside him. R2-Friend was perfect at games and never made mistakes, but something important was missing. "When I'm sad, I need comfort," Zain explained gently. "And when I'm happy, I want to share the feeling with you."
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Zain sitting on his bed looking thoughtful and a bit sad, with R2-Friend standing nearby. The robot looks confused by Zain's explanation, with question marks almost visible around his head.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: When someone helps you feel better when you're sad or hurt.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: The warm, safe feeling you get when someone cares for you during difficult times.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The teddy bear gave comfort to the little girl during the thunderstorm.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: When something important is not there that should be there.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Something that should be present but isn't there, making things feel incomplete or wrong.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The puzzle was missing three pieces so we couldn't finish the picture.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Doing something in a soft and kind way that doesn't hurt.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Acting with care and tenderness, being soft and careful not to cause any harm.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: She gently picked up the baby bird and placed it back in the nest.

pages.label: Page 6
pages.text: Zain had a wonderful idea! He opened his laptop and began typing new code with clicking keys. "I need to teach you about feelings," he said to R2-Friend. He programmed instructions about happiness, sadness, and caring. His fingers moved quickly across the warm keyboard as he worked.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Zain sitting at a desk with an open laptop, typing code while R2-Friend watches over his shoulder with curious blue eyes. The screen shows colorful programming code about emotions.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: A small computer that you can carry and use anywhere.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: A portable computer that folds up like a book and can be taken places easily.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: Dad brought his laptop to the coffee shop to work on his presentation.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Put special instructions into a computer to make it work.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Wrote computer code or instructions that tell a machine exactly what to do and how.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The engineer programmed the robot to water the plants every morning.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: The steps that tell you exactly how to do something right.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Clear directions that explain each step needed to complete a task or learn something new.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: Follow the instructions on the box to build the toy airplane correctly.

pages.label: Page 7
pages.text: "Feelings help friends take care of each other," Zain explained as he uploaded the new program. R2-Friend's blue eyes flickered as he processed the information. "When someone is hurt, good friends offer comfort. When someone is happy, friends celebrate together!" Zain said with a warm smile.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: R2-Friend with flickering blue eyes as new information downloads, while Zain explains with animated gestures. Small heart and smiley face symbols seem to float between them representing the emotion concepts.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Put information from one computer into another computer or robot.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Transferred data or programs from one device to another through a computer connection.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: She uploaded her vacation photos to share them with her grandparents online.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: When lights go on and off really fast like they're blinking.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Flashed or blinked quickly with an unsteady light that goes bright and dim repeatedly.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The candle flickered in the wind before it went out completely.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: When a computer thinks about information to understand it better.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Worked through information step by step to understand, organize, or change it in some way.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The bank processed my mom's check so the money went into our account.

pages.label: Page 8
pages.text: The next time Zain felt sad about losing a game, something wonderful happened. R2-Friend's eyes glowed softly and he gently patted Zain's shoulder with his smooth metal hand. "I understand you feel disappointed," R2-Friend said kindly. "Would you like to try again together?"
pages.scene: illustration prompt: R2-Friend gently patting Zain's shoulder with a caring expression, his eyes glowing warmly. Zain looks surprised but pleased by his robot's new understanding and kindness.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: The sad feeling when something you hoped for doesn't happen.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Feeling let down and sad because something you really wanted or expected didn't work out.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: Tim felt disappointed when the baseball game was cancelled because of rain.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Something really amazing and great that makes you feel happy.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Extremely good and special in a way that fills you with joy and amazement.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: We had a wonderful time at the zoo seeing all the different animals.

pages.label: Page 9
pages.text: When Zain built an amazing tower of blocks, R2-Friend's lights flashed in colorful celebration! "Congratulations, friend! Your creation is wonderful!" the robot cheered with excitement. Together, they did a happy dance around the tall, wobbling tower, laughing and celebrating Zain's success.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Zain and R2-Friend dancing joyfully around a tall tower of colorful blocks. R2-Friend's lights are flashing in rainbow colors, and both characters look delighted and celebratory.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: A happy party or special time when good things happen.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: A joyful event or activity to show happiness about something special or important that happened.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The whole family came for a celebration when grandpa turned 80 years old.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Moving back and forth like it might fall down soon.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Shaking or moving unsteadily from side to side because it's not balanced very well.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The stack of books was wobbling and almost fell off the table.

pages.label: Page 10
pages.text: Now Zain and R2-Friend were truly best friends. They played games, shared feelings, and took care of each other every day. R2-Friend had learned that being a good friend means understanding feelings and caring about others. "Thank you for teaching me," R2-Friend said, giving Zain a gentle robot hug.
