vocabWords.definition_7_10: Drooping or hanging loosely instead of being stiff or upright, like something that bends easily.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: My dog has one floppy ear that always hangs to the side.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: In a strong, sure way that shows you really mean it.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Speaking or acting in a confident, determined way that shows you're serious about what you believe.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: She firmly told her brother to stop teasing the cat.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: What you're planning to do or what you want to happen.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Your plans or purposes for doing something, especially whether you mean to help or cause harm.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: My intentions were good when I tried to help clean up the mess.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Hung down sadly, like when you feel disappointed or tired.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Sagged or bent downward, often showing sadness or disappointment through body language.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The flower drooped because it needed water.

pages.label: Page 6
pages.text: Fern took a deep breath and decided to be completely honest. "I understand why you're scared," she said gently. "Some foxes do chase rabbits, and I know you don't have any reason to trust me yet. But I promise I only came because your garden smells so wonderful. I've never learned how to grow vegetables, and I was hoping maybe you could teach me." Her golden eyes were sincere and hopeful.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Fern sitting politely at the garden edge with sincere expression, speaking to the rabbits who are now cautiously listening from among the vegetables

vocabWords.definition_4_7: All the way, with nothing left out or hidden.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Totally and fully, without keeping anything back or leaving any part undone.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: I completely forgot to bring my lunch to school today.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Telling the truth and not lying or hiding things.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Being truthful and straightforward, not trying to trick people or hide important information.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: I was honest with my mom about breaking the vase.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: In a soft, kind way that won't hurt anyone.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: In a careful, tender manner that shows kindness and won't cause fear or harm.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: She gently petted the scared kitten until it felt safe.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Really meaning what you say, not pretending or lying.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Genuinely honest and truthful, showing real feelings without any pretending or fake behavior.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: His sincere apology made me feel better about the mistake.

pages.label: Page 7
pages.text: The smallest rabbit's nose twitched with curiosity. "You want to learn about gardening?" she asked, her spotted fur catching the sunlight. "Most foxes only care about hunting." Fern nodded eagerly. "I love exploring and discovering new things. Growing beautiful vegetables like yours seems much more interesting than chasing anyone." The three rabbits looked at each other, whispering in their quiet voices as they considered her words.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: The small spotted rabbit looking curious while the three rabbits huddle together whispering, with Fern waiting hopefully nearby among the garden rows

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Going to new places to see what's there and learn.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Traveling around and investigating new places to discover interesting things and gain new experiences.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: We spent the day exploring the old castle ruins.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Finding out about something new that you didn't know before.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Learning about or finding something for the first time, especially something exciting or important.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The scientist was discovering new facts about how plants grow.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: In an excited way because you really want something to happen.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: With enthusiasm and excitement, showing that you're very interested and can hardly wait.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The children eagerly opened their birthday presents.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Thought carefully about something before deciding what to do.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Took time to think through different possibilities and options before making up their minds.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: She considered both choices before picking her favorite ice cream flavor.

pages.label: Page 8
pages.text: To show she was serious, Fern slowly backed away from the garden. "I'll prove I'm trustworthy," she said. "I'll come back tomorrow and sit right here without coming any closer. I'll do this every day until you believe that I really just want to be friends and learn about gardens." The brown rabbit's floppy ear perked up with surprise at her thoughtful gesture.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Fern backing away from the garden respectfully while the three rabbits watch with growing interest, showing the distance she's keeping as a sign of trust

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Really meaning what you say, not joking or playing around.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Being genuine and determined about something important, showing that you're not just pretending.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: I'm serious about practicing piano every day this month.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Someone you can count on to do the right thing.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Reliable and honest, someone who keeps promises and won't let you down or hurt you.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: My trustworthy friend always keeps secrets when I tell her something private.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Being polite and kind, not bothering or hurting others.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Showing good manners and consideration for others by giving them space and treating them nicely.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The respectful student waited quietly while the teacher helped another child.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Thinking about others' feelings and trying to be kind to them.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Caring about how other people feel and doing nice things to show you understand what they need.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: It was thoughtful of him to bring soup when his neighbor was sick.

pages.label: Page 9
pages.text: For the next week, Fern kept her promise exactly. Every morning, she would sit at the same respectful distance and watch the rabbits tend their garden. She never tried to come closer, even when delicious vegetables were within easy reach. The rabbits began to realize that this fox was different from the ones in their grandmother's stories. Her actions matched her honest words perfectly.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: A montage showing Fern sitting patiently in the same spot over several days while the rabbits work in their garden, with changing light showing the passage of time

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Saying you will definitely do something and really meaning it.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Making a commitment to do exactly what you say you'll do, and keeping your word no matter what.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: I made a promise to help my sister with her homework.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: In the same way every time, without changing anything at all.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Precisely and perfectly, doing something the exact same way without any differences or mistakes.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: She followed the recipe exactly like her grandmother taught her.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Took good care of something to help it grow and stay healthy.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Carefully looked after plants or animals by giving them what they needed to thrive and be well.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The farmer tended his chickens by feeding them every morning.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Suddenly understood something important that you didn't know before.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Came to understand or figure out something new, often something that changed how you thought about a situation.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: I realized I had been holding the book upside down.

pages.label: Page 10
pages.text: On the eighth day, the spotted rabbit hopped over to where Fern sat waiting. "Would you like to help us plant some new carrot seeds?" she asked with a shy smile. Soon, all three rabbits were teaching Fern how to make small holes in the soil and gently place seeds inside. As they worked together, sharing stories and laughter, Fern realized that being honest had given her something much better than vegetables – true friends.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Fern and the three rabbits working happily together in the garden, planting seeds with gardening tools, all smiling and comfortable with each other

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Having small round marks or dots of different colors on something.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Covered with small patches or dots of color that make a pretty pattern, like polka dots.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The spotted cow had black and white patches all over her body.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Giving some of what you have to other people too.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Dividing things with others so everyone can enjoy them, or telling stories and experiences together.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The children were sharing their crayons so everyone could color.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: The happy sound people make when something is funny or fun.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: The joyful sounds and giggles that come from being happy, amused, or having a wonderful time together.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The room filled with laughter during the funny movie.

comprehensionQuestions.question: Why were the rabbits afraid of Fern at first?

options.text: They thought all foxes chase rabbits
She was too big for their garden
She was making too much noise

comprehensionQuestions.question: What showed the rabbits that Fern was different from other foxes?

options.text: She kept her promise to stay away for a week
She was smaller than most foxes
She had prettier fur than other foxes

comprehensionQuestions.question: How do you feel when someone doesn't trust you at first?

options.text: Sad but understanding
Patient and hopeful
Determined to prove myself

predictionPause.question: What do you think will happen next? Will the rabbits ever trust Fern?

options.text: The rabbits will invite Fern to help with the garden
Fern will give up and find a different garden
The rabbits will move to a new place

an8.title: Little Bear's First Snow
an8.obstacle: seeing snow for the very first time and not knowing what it is

pages.label: Page 1
pages.text: Little Bear lived in a cozy cave with Mama Bear. He loved to play outside in the warm sunshine. Today felt different though. The air was cold and still.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: A small brown bear cub standing at the entrance of a warm, cozy cave with his mother bear behind him, looking out at a gray winter sky

vocabWords.definition_2_4: Warm and comfortable like a snugly place.
vocabWords.definition_4_7: A place that feels warm, soft, and safe to be in.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Something that makes you feel warm, comfortable, and happy like being wrapped in a soft blanket.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The little girl made a cozy fort with pillows and blankets.

vocabWords.definition_2_4: A big hole in a rock where animals live.
vocabWords.definition_4_7: A hollow space inside a mountain or rock where animals sleep.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: A natural hollow space inside rocks or mountains that provides shelter for animals and people.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The explorer used a flashlight to see inside the dark cave.

vocabWords.definition_2_4: Bright warm light from the sun.
vocabWords.definition_4_7: The warm bright light that comes from the sun during day.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: The cheerful golden light and warmth that streams down from the sun on clear days.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The flowers grew tall in the warm sunshine by the window.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: When everything is quiet and nothing is moving around.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: A peaceful moment when there is no sound or movement, like everything is waiting quietly.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The pond was so still that it looked like a mirror.

pages.label: Page 2
pages.text: Little Bear stepped outside. Something white was falling from the sky! 'What is that?' he wondered. The white things landed on his nose.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Little Bear with his head tilted up, eyes wide with wonder, as the first snowflakes fall around him, one landing on his black nose

vocabWords.definition_2_4: Put his foot down to walk.
vocabWords.definition_4_7: When you lift your foot and put it down somewhere.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: The action of carefully placing your foot down in one spot while walking or moving.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: She stepped over the puddle to keep her shoes dry.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: When something drops down from a higher place to lower.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Moving downward through the air from a higher place to the ground below.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The colorful leaves were falling from the tree in autumn.

vocabWords.definition_2_4: Thinking about something he didn't know.
vocabWords.definition_4_7: When you think about something and want to know more.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Having curious thoughts about something you don't understand and wanting to find the answer.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The little boy wondered why the stars only came out at night.

vocabWords.definition_2_4: Came down and stopped on something.
vocabWords.definition_4_7: When something falls down and stops on a place.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: The moment when something that was falling or flying comes to rest on a surface.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The butterfly landed gently on the red flower.

pages.label: Page 3
pages.text: The white things were cold! They tickled his fur. Little Bear backed away toward the cave. 'I don't know what this is,' he said to Mama.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Little Bear backing toward the cave entrance, looking uncertain, with snowflakes on his brown fur and Mama Bear watching from inside

vocabWords.definition_2_4: Made a funny feeling that made him giggle.
vocabWords.definition_4_7: When something touches you in a way that feels funny.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: A light touching sensation that makes you want to laugh or feels pleasantly surprising.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The feather tickled the baby's cheek and made her smile.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: When you walk backwards to move away from something.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Moving in reverse direction, usually to get away from something or return to safety.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The dog backed away when it saw the big truck coming.

vocabWords.definition_2_4: Going in the direction of something.
vocabWords.definition_4_7: Moving closer to a person, place, or thing nearby.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Moving in the direction of something specific, getting closer to reaching that place or person.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The cat walked toward the bowl of milk on the kitchen floor.

pages.label: Page 4
pages.text: Mama Bear smiled. 'That's snow, Little Bear. It's soft and fun. Would you like to try playing in it?' Little Bear peeked outside again.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Mama Bear with a gentle, encouraging smile, nuzzling Little Bear as he peers cautiously out at the falling snow

vocabWords.definition_2_4: Made a happy face with her mouth.
vocabWords.definition_4_7: When your mouth turns up at the corners showing happiness.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: A warm facial expression where the corners of your mouth curve upward to show joy or kindness.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The teacher smiled when the children sang the birthday song.

vocabWords.definition_2_4: White soft cold stuff that falls from clouds.
vocabWords.definition_4_7: White fluffy pieces of ice that fall from the sky.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Soft white frozen water that falls from clouds in winter and covers the ground like a blanket.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The children built a snowman when the snow was deep enough.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: When you look at something quickly or secretly.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Taking a quick, careful look at something, often while being cautious or curious about it.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The little girl peeked under the bed to look for her lost toy.

pages.label: Page 5
pages.text: Little Bear took one small step. Then another. The snow crunched under his paws. It made a fun sound! He took a bigger step.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Little Bear taking careful steps in the fresh snow, looking down at his paws with growing curiosity and interest

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Made a loud breaking sound when he stepped on it.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Created a crisp, breaking sound when something hard or frozen gets crushed under pressure.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The dry leaves crunched loudly under her feet as she walked.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: The feet of animals like cats, dogs, and bears.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: The soft padded feet of furry animals that have toes and claws for walking and climbing.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The puppy left muddy paw prints on the clean kitchen floor.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: When something is larger than it was before.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Having grown in size or importance, becoming more than what it was originally.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: His bigger brother could reach the cookies on the high shelf.

pages.label: Page 6
pages.text: Now Little Bear was walking in the snow! It was cold but soft. He made little paw prints everywhere he went. This was fun!
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Little Bear walking more confidently through the snow, leaving a trail of small paw prints behind him, with a slight smile on his face

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Moving from one place to another by taking steps.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: The steady movement of putting one foot in front of the other to travel somewhere.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: They enjoyed walking through the park and feeding the ducks.

vocabWords.definition_2_4: Marks his feet made in the snow.
vocabWords.definition_4_7: The marks that feet or paws leave behind somewhere.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: The impressions or marks left behind when feet press into soft surfaces like sand or snow.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: We could see the bird's tiny prints in the wet sand.

vocabWords.definition_2_4: In all the places he went.
vocabWords.definition_4_7: In every place or spot that you can think of.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: In all possible places and directions, covering every spot or location you can imagine.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: After the party, there were colorful balloons everywhere in the room.

pages.label: Page 7
pages.text: Little Bear started to run! He rolled in the fluffy white snow. He caught snowflakes on his tongue. They tasted like cold water drops.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Little Bear joyfully rolling in the snow with his tongue out catching snowflakes, snow covering his fur, looking delighted

vocabWords.definition_4_7: When you begin doing something you weren't doing before.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: The moment when you begin a new activity or action that you weren't doing previously.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: She started reading her book right after dinner was finished.

vocabWords.definition_2_4: Turned over and over on the ground.
vocabWords.definition_4_7: When something turns over and over while moving around.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Moving by turning completely over repeatedly, like a ball spinning down a hill.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The ball rolled down the hill and into the neighbor's yard.

vocabWords.definition_2_4: Very soft and light like a cloud.
vocabWords.definition_4_7: Something that feels very soft, light, and full of air.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Having a soft, airy texture that feels light and gentle, like cotton or a pillow.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The fluffy kitten felt like a soft cotton ball when I petted it.

vocabWords.definition_2_4: Tiny pieces of snow with pretty shapes.
vocabWords.definition_4_7: Small pieces of snow that have beautiful patterns and shapes.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Individual tiny crystals of frozen water that fall from clouds, each with its own unique pattern.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: Each snowflake that landed on her mitten had a different beautiful design.

vocabWords.definition_2_4: The part inside his mouth that tastes food.
vocabWords.definition_4_7: The soft part inside your mouth that helps you taste.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: The flexible muscle inside your mouth that helps you taste food, swallow, and speak clearly.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The ice cream was so cold it made her tongue feel frozen.

pages.label: Page 8
pages.text: Little Bear played until his paws were cold. He ran back to Mama Bear. 'I love snow!' he said. 'I'm glad I tried it!'
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Little Bear running excitedly back to Mama Bear at the cave entrance, both bears smiling, snow covering Little Bear's fur

vocabWords.definition_4_7: When you spend time having fun with games or activities.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Spending time doing enjoyable activities or games that make you happy and entertained.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The children played on the swings at the playground all afternoon.

vocabWords.definition_2_4: Up to the time when something happened.
vocabWords.definition_4_7: Continuing to do something up to a certain time.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Doing something continuously up to the point when a specific event or time arrives.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: We stayed at the beach until the sun began to set.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Feeling happy and pleased about something that happened.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: A feeling of happiness and satisfaction when something turns out well or makes you pleased.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: I'm glad we remembered to bring umbrellas before it started raining.

pages.label: Page 9
pages.text: Mama Bear hugged him tight. 'You were very brave to try something new,' she said. Little Bear felt warm and proud inside his heart.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Mama Bear giving Little Bear a big, warm hug at the cave entrance, both looking happy and content, snow falling gently around them

vocabWords.definition_2_4: Put her arms around him to show love.
vocabWords.definition_4_7: When someone puts their arms around you to show love.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Wrapping your arms around someone and holding them close to show affection and care.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: Grandma hugged me tightly when I visited her house yesterday.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: When something is held very close without any space.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Held firmly and closely with no loose space, creating a secure and snug feeling.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: She held the rope tight so she wouldn't fall off the swing.

vocabWords.definition_2_4: Not scared to try something new.
vocabWords.definition_4_7: When you do something even though you feel a scared.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Having courage to face something difficult or unknown despite feeling nervous or uncertain about it.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The brave firefighter rescued the kitten from the tall tree.

vocabWords.definition_2_4: Feeling good about something he did.
vocabWords.definition_4_7: Feeling happy and good about something you accomplished.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Having a sense of satisfaction and happiness about an achievement or something you did well.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: She felt proud when she learned to ride her bike without help.

pages.label: Page 10
pages.text: That night, Little Bear dreamed of snow. He couldn't wait for tomorrow to play again. Trying new things made everything more fun!
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Little Bear sleeping peacefully in the cozy cave, with dream bubbles above his head showing him playing happily in the snow

vocabWords.definition_2_4: When it gets dark and time to sleep.
vocabWords.definition_4_7: The dark time after the sun goes down.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: The period of darkness between sunset and sunrise when most people sleep and rest.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The stars twinkled brightly in the clear night sky above us.

vocabWords.definition_2_4: Had pictures in his mind while sleeping.
vocabWords.definition_4_7: When you see pictures and stories in your mind while sleeping.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Experiencing images, thoughts, and stories in your mind during sleep that feel real but aren't.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: Last night I dreamed I could fly like a bird over the clouds.

vocabWords.definition_2_4: The day that comes after today.
vocabWords.definition_4_7: The day that will come right after this day ends.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: The next day that will arrive after today is finished and you wake up again.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: Tomorrow is my birthday party and all my friends will come.

an9.title: The Singing Whale
an9.obstacle: her song is different from all the other whales and she feels alone

pages.label: Page 1
pages.text: Deep in the sparkling blue ocean lived a young whale named Melody. Every morning, she would swim to her favorite coral reef where colorful fish danced between the swaying seaweed. The water felt cool and gentle against her smooth skin.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: A beautiful young whale swimming near a vibrant coral reef with tropical fish, morning sunlight filtering through the clear blue water

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Shining with lots of tiny bright lights that twinkle.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Gleaming with countless small points of light that dance and shimmer like scattered diamonds.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The sparkling snow covered the playground like glitter.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Hard colorful things that grow underwater where fish live.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Rocky structures built by tiny sea creatures that create homes for many ocean animals.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The diver saw bright coral growing on the ocean floor.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Moving back and forth gently like dancing in the wind.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Rocking smoothly from side to side in a rhythmic, graceful motion like a gentle dance.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The tall grass was swaying in the summer breeze.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Long green plants that grow in the ocean water.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Underwater plants that grow from the sea floor and wave in the ocean currents.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: Seaweed washed up on the beach after the storm.

pages.label: Page 2
pages.text: When the whale pod gathered to sing their ancient songs, Melody felt excited. She opened her mouth wide and let her voice flow freely through the water. But instead of the deep, rumbling sounds the others made, Melody's song was high and sweet like tiny silver bells.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: A group of large whales singing together underwater, with one smaller whale (Melody) in the center producing visible sound waves that look different from the others

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Very, very old from a long time ago.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Something that existed many, many years before we were born, from long ago times.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The ancient castle was built hundreds of years ago.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Making deep, low sounds that you can feel vibrating.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Creating deep, rolling sounds that seem to shake and vibrate through your whole body.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The rumbling thunder scared the little puppy during the storm.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Move smoothly like water running in a river.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Move in a steady, continuous stream without stopping or getting stuck anywhere.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: Maple syrup will flow slowly from the bottle onto pancakes.

pages.label: Page 3
pages.text: The other whales stopped singing and stared at Melody with surprise. Her grandmother's song was low and powerful like rolling thunder. Her mother's voice was rich and warm like ocean currents. But Melody's song sounded nothing like theirs.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Several large whales looking curiously at Melody, their expressions showing gentle surprise, underwater scene with rays of light

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Looked at something for a long time without looking away.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Gazed at someone or something with wide eyes for a long time without blinking.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The cat stared at the bird sitting on the fence.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Very strong and able to do big things.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Having great strength and force that can make big changes or move heavy things.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The powerful elephant could lift heavy logs with its trunk.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: The loud booming sound that comes after lightning flashes.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: The deep, rumbling noise that rolls across the sky during storms after lightning strikes.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The thunder was so loud it made the windows rattle.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Water that moves in one direction like a river.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Streams of water that flow steadily in the same direction through oceans or rivers.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The leaf floated away on the stream's gentle currents.

pages.label: Page 4
pages.text: "Why does my voice sound so different?" Melody asked her mother sadly. Her heart felt as heavy as a sunken treasure chest. She watched the other young whales practice their deep, booming songs while she floated alone behind a large rock.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Melody hiding behind a large underwater rock formation, looking sad, while other young whales practice singing in the distance

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Not the same as other things you're comparing it to.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Unlike other things in a way that makes it special or stand out from the group.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: Each snowflake has a different shape from all the others.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Special valuable things like gold coins and pretty jewels.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Precious items like gold, silver, and gems that are worth lots of money or are very special.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The pirates buried their treasure chest on a secret island.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Making very loud, deep sounds that echo everywhere.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Creating powerful, thunderous noises that seem to fill the air and vibrate all around.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The booming fireworks lit up the night sky with colors.

pages.label: Page 5
pages.text: Day after day, Melody tried to make her voice sound like the others. She sang as low as she could until her throat felt scratchy and sore. But no matter how hard she tried, her song still came out bright and musical like ocean wind chimes.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Melody alone, concentrating hard with her mouth open, trying to sing, with frustrated expression, bubbles around her

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Feeling rough and uncomfortable like sandpaper touching your skin.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Having a rough, irritated feeling that makes you want to cough or clear your throat.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: My sweater feels scratchy against my neck when I wear it.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Sounding like pretty music that you enjoy hearing.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Having a pleasant, melody-like quality that sounds harmonious and beautiful to listen to.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The musical sound of rain on the roof helped me sleep.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Things that hang and make pretty ringing sounds in the wind.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Musical instruments that create gentle, tinkling melodies when the wind moves them around.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The wind chimes on the porch played soft music all afternoon.

pages.label: Page 6
pages.text: Feeling lonely and discouraged, Melody swam far away from her family. She found a quiet underwater canyon where tall kelp forests swayed like emerald curtains. "I'll never fit in," she whispered to herself, her voice echoing softly off the canyon walls.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Melody swimming alone through a deep underwater canyon with towering kelp forests, looking small and lonely, green kelp swaying around her

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Feeling sad and wanting to give up trying something.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Feeling disappointed and losing hope that things will get better or work out well.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: Sam felt discouraged when his tower of blocks kept falling down.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: A deep space between tall rocks or mountains.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: A deep valley with steep rocky walls on both sides, carved out over many years.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The hikers looked down into the deep canyon from the trail.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: A beautiful bright green color like precious stones.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: A rich, brilliant green color like the valuable gemstones found in jewelry and crowns.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The emerald grass in spring looked so fresh and pretty.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Cloth that hangs down to cover windows or doorways.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Fabric panels that hang vertically and can be moved to block light or create privacy.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: Mom opened the curtains to let sunshine into the living room.

pages.label: Page 7
pages.text: Suddenly, Melody heard a faint sound drifting down from above. She swam upward through the blue-green water until she could see the golden sunlight dancing on the ocean's surface. There, caught in a fishing net, was a baby dolphin calling for help!
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Melody swimming upward toward bright sunlight, seeing a small dolphin tangled in a fishing net near the surface, rays of golden light streaming down

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Moving slowly and gently like a cloud floating by.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Floating or moving gradually without any force pushing it in a particular direction.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The balloon was drifting high above the trees in the sky.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: The top part of something you can see and touch.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: The outer layer or top side of something, like the top of water or a table.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The duck floated on the surface of the calm pond.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: A smart sea animal that breathes air and jumps playfully.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: An intelligent marine mammal with a long nose that lives in the ocean and is very friendly.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The dolphin jumped high out of the water at the aquarium show.

pages.label: Page 8
pages.text: The baby dolphin was too far away to hear the deep whale songs, but Melody's high, clear voice traveled perfectly through the water. She sang her sweet song as loudly as she could. The dolphin heard her immediately and called back with grateful clicks and whistles.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Melody singing with her mouth open wide, sound waves traveling upward to reach the dolphin in the net, both animals looking hopeful

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Went from one place to another place far away.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Moved across distance from a starting point to reach a destination somewhere else.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The letter traveled across the country to reach my grandma.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Done exactly right without any mistakes or problems.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Completed in the most ideal way possible, without any flaws or things that could be better.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The puzzle pieces fit together perfectly to make the picture.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Feeling thankful and happy because someone helped you.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Feeling deeply thankful and appreciative when someone does something kind or helpful for you.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: I felt grateful when my friend shared lunch with me.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: High sounds you make by blowing air through your lips.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Sharp, high-pitched sounds created by forcing air through a small opening in your mouth.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The referee blows whistles to stop the soccer game.

pages.label: Page 9
pages.text: Using her gentle song, Melody guided a school of helpful fish to nibble through the net's ropes. Soon the baby dolphin was free! "Thank you!" squeaked the dolphin. "Your beautiful voice saved me! I've never heard anything so lovely in all the ocean."
pages.scene: illustration prompt: The baby dolphin swimming free from the broken net, surrounded by colorful fish, nuzzling Melody gratefully near the sunny ocean surface

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Showed someone the way to go to get somewhere.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Led or directed someone along the right path to help them reach their destination safely.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The teacher guided the children across the busy street.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Take very small bites of something with your teeth.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Eat or bite something in tiny pieces, taking small amounts at a time with gentle bites.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The rabbit likes to nibble on fresh carrots from the garden.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Made a high, short sound like a tiny mouse.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Created a sharp, high-pitched noise that sounds quick and small, like a toy being squeezed.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The door squeaked loudly when we opened it slowly.

pages.label: Page 10
pages.text: When Melody returned home and told her family what happened, they all smiled with pride. "Your special voice is a wonderful gift," said her mother warmly. "The ocean needs all kinds of songs." From that day on, Melody sang with joy, knowing her unique voice made the sea a more beautiful place.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Melody surrounded by her whale family in a circle, all looking proud and happy, with Melody in the center singing confidently, underwater scene with warm lighting

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Feeling really good and happy about something you did well.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: A warm, happy feeling you get when you or someone you care about does something wonderful.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: Dad felt pride when I learned to ride my bike by myself.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Different from others in a really good and important way.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Unlike anything else in a way that makes it valuable, meaningful, or particularly wonderful.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: My grandmother's special cookies taste better than any others.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: One of a kind, not like anything else anywhere.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Something that is completely different and special because there's nothing else exactly like it in the world.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: Every person's fingerprints are unique and different from everyone else's.

comprehensionQuestions.question: What made Melody's song different from the other whales' songs?

options.text: Her song was high and sweet like silver bells
Her song was deep and powerful like thunder
Her song was quiet and could not be heard

comprehensionQuestions.question: Why was Melody able to save the baby dolphin when other whales couldn't?

options.text: Her high, clear voice could travel far through the water
She was the strongest swimmer in her family
She was the only whale who saw the dolphin

comprehensionQuestions.question: How do you think Melody felt when her family smiled with pride at the end?

options.text: Happy and proud that her voice was special
Excited to help more animals in the ocean
Grateful that her family loved her just as she was

predictionPause.question: Oh no! There's a baby dolphin caught in a fishing net! What do you think Melody will do to help?

options.text: Use her special high voice to call for help
Try to break the net with her strong tail
Swim away to get her family to come help

an10.title: Migration Day
an10.obstacle: it's time for her first migration south but she's afraid of the long journey

pages.label: Page 1
pages.text: High in the rocky cliffs of the northern mountains, young Swift stretched her golden wings wide to catch the crisp autumn breeze. The leaves below had turned brilliant shades of red and orange, creating a colorful carpet across the valley. Soon, her parents said, it would be time for her very first migration south. But Swift felt a flutter of worry deep in her chest whenever she thought about the long, long journey ahead.
pages.scene: A young golden eagle named Swift perched on a rocky cliff ledge, wings outstretched, overlooking a stunning autumn valley filled with red and orange trees

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Very tall, steep rocks that go straight up.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Towering rock walls that rise dramatically from the ground, like nature's skyscrapers.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The ocean waves crashed against the white cliffs by the sea.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Air that feels fresh, cool, and clean.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Fresh, cool air that feels sharp and clean when you breathe it in.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The crisp morning air made Sarah want to run outside and play.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Very bright and colorful, almost glowing.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: So bright and vivid that colors seem to shine and glow with their own light.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The fireworks made brilliant flashes of color across the night sky.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: A long journey animals take to find warmer places.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: A long journey that animals make together to find food, warmth, or safety.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: Every winter, the whales begin their migration to warmer ocean waters.

pages.label: Page 2
pages.text: "Migration season is almost here, Swift," called her father, soaring gracefully toward their nest with a silvery fish in his talons. Her mother nodded, her dark eyes twinkling with excitement. "We'll fly thousands of miles together, following the warm winds all the way to the sunny valleys of the south." Swift tried to smile, but her stomach did little flip-flops. What if she got tired? What if she couldn't keep up with the other eagles?
pages.scene: Swift's father landing at their mountain nest with a fish, while her mother looks on proudly, and Swift appears thoughtful and slightly worried

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Flying high and smooth without flapping wings much.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Gliding through the air gracefully, riding the wind currents with outstretched wings.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The airplane was soaring above the clouds on a clear day.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Moving in a smooth, beautiful way.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Moving with smooth, elegant motions that look effortless and beautiful to watch.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The dancer moved gracefully across the stage during the performance.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Sharp, curved claws that birds use for catching things.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: The sharp, powerful claws that eagles and hawks use to catch and hold their prey.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The hawk gripped the branch tightly with its sharp talons.

pages.label: Page 3
pages.text: The next morning, Swift watched her friend River practice his diving techniques near the sparkling mountain lake. "Aren't you excited about migration?" River asked, his feathers gleaming in the sunlight as he climbed higher into the sky. Swift shuffled her feet on the rocky perch. "I'm just... wondering if I'm really ready," she admitted quietly. The vast blue sky suddenly seemed much bigger and more daunting than ever before.
pages.scene: Swift sitting thoughtfully on a rock while her friend River practices flying maneuvers above a crystal-clear mountain lake

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Special ways of doing something well.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Specific methods or skills that help you do something better and more successfully.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The chef taught us new techniques for making perfect cookies.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Shining with lots of tiny, bright lights.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Glittering and twinkling with many small flashes of light, like scattered diamonds.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The sparkling snow looked magical in the moonlight.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Something that seems scary or too hard to do.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: So challenging or overwhelming that it makes you feel worried about whether you can do it.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: Learning to ride a bike seemed daunting until Mom helped me practice.

pages.label: Page 4
pages.text: That evening, Swift's grandmother, Luna, settled beside her on their favorite watching rock. Luna's wise old eyes had seen many migrations, and her silver-tipped feathers caught the golden sunset light. "You know, little one," Luna said gently, "I was frightened before my first journey too. The sky seemed endless, and I felt so very small." Swift looked up in surprise – brave, strong Luna had been scared?
pages.scene: An elderly eagle with silver-tipped feathers sitting next to young Swift on a rock outcropping, with a golden sunset painting the sky behind them

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Sat down in a comfortable, cozy way.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Got comfortable in a spot and made yourself at home there for a while.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The cat settled into the warm blanket for a long nap.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Having silver color on the ends.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Decorated with shiny silver color at the tips, like they were dipped in liquid silver.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The Christmas tree had silver-tipped branches that sparkled in the light.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Going on and on without ever stopping.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Stretching so far that it seems like it will never come to an end.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The endless line at the ice cream truck made us wait forever.

pages.label: Page 5
pages.text: "What helped you feel brave?" Swift asked, snuggling closer to her grandmother's warm, soft feathers. Luna smiled and pointed one wing toward the twinkling stars beginning to appear in the darkening sky. "I remembered that every great journey begins with just one wingbeat, then another, then another. And I knew my family would be right there beside me, riding the same winds and sharing the same adventure."
pages.scene: Luna and Swift looking up at the early evening sky with the first stars appearing, Luna's wing pointing upward, both eagles close together

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Cuddling up close to someone warm and soft.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Nestling close to someone you love to feel cozy, warm, and safe together.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The puppy was snuggling with its mother by the fireplace.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Shining with little flashes of light that blink on and off.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Sparkling with tiny lights that seem to dance and flicker like magical glitter.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The twinkling lights on the house made it look like a fairy tale.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: One flap of a bird's wings up and down.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: A single powerful movement of a bird's wings as it pushes against the air to fly.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The hummingbird's wingbeat was so fast it made a buzzing sound.

pages.label: Page 6
pages.text: The morning of Migration Day arrived with cool, perfect flying weather and gentle southern winds. Swift felt the familiar butterfly feeling in her stomach, but Luna's words echoed in her mind. All around their mountain home, eagle families were gathering, their excited calls filling the crisp air. Swift took a deep breath and spread her wings wide, feeling the wind lift beneath her golden feathers for the very first time that day.
pages.scene: Multiple eagle families gathering on clifftops and in the air on a clear morning, with Swift spreading her wings in the foreground, mountains and clouds in the background

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Something you know well because you've felt it before.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: A feeling or experience that you recognize because it has happened to you many times.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The familiar smell of cookies reminded him of his grandmother's kitchen.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Repeated over and over in your mind.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Kept coming back to her thoughts like a voice bouncing off canyon walls.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: His teacher's encouraging words echoed in his head during the test.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Coming together in the same place.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Meeting and collecting together in one spot, like friends coming to a party.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: All the children were gathering in the playground for recess.

pages.label: Page 7
pages.text: "Stay close, Swift," her father called as the great migration began. Dozens of eagles rose into the sky together, their powerful wings catching the warm currents that would carry them south. Swift pushed off from her rocky home and felt her heart leap as the wind lifted her higher and higher. One wingbeat, then another, then another – just like Luna had said. She was really doing it!
pages.scene: A magnificent scene of many eagles taking flight together, with Swift among them, soaring above the mountain peaks in formation

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Moving streams of air that help birds fly.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Rivers of air that flow through the sky, giving birds a boost as they soar.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The sailboat used the ocean currents to move faster through the water.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Very strong and able to do big things.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Having great strength and force, capable of accomplishing amazing and difficult tasks.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The powerful storm knocked down trees all over the neighborhood.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Moving at the same speed without stopping or changing.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Continuing at a regular, unchanging pace without wobbling, rushing, or slowing down.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The rain fell steadily all afternoon without getting heavier or lighter.

pages.label: Page 8
pages.text: Hours passed as they soared over forests, rivers, and rolling hills painted in autumn colors. Swift's wings grew tired, but when she looked around, her parents flew steadily beside her, and River gave her an encouraging call from nearby. The other eagles moved like a graceful cloud across the sky, all heading toward the same welcoming destination. Swift realized she wasn't small and alone – she was part of something wonderful and big.
pages.scene: Eagles flying in formation over a beautiful landscape of forests, rivers, and autumn-colored hills, with Swift flying confidently among her family and friends

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Moving up and down like gentle waves.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Rising and falling in smooth, rounded shapes like waves frozen in the landscape.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The rolling waves at the beach were perfect for surfing.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Making someone feel brave and confident they can do it.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Giving support and hope to help someone believe in themselves and keep trying.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: My coach's encouraging words helped me score the winning goal.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: The place you're trying to get to.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: The final stop or goal at the end of your journey, where you're heading.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: After driving all day, we finally reached our destination at the beach.

pages.label: Page 9
pages.text: As the sun began to set, painting the sky in shades of pink and gold, the eagles spotted their destination – a warm valley filled with tall trees and a winding river. Swift felt a surge of pride and joy as she realized she had done it! Her first migration was nearly complete. The scary, endless journey had become an amazing adventure filled with beauty, family, and the discovery of her own courage.
pages.scene: The flock of eagles approaching a lush, welcoming valley with tall trees and a winding river, the sky painted in sunset colors of pink and gold

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Saw something from far away.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Noticed or caught sight of something, especially when looking carefully or from a distance.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: We spotted a deer drinking from the stream in the woods.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Curving and twisting like a snake.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Twisting and turning in gentle curves, following a path that bends back and forth.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The winding mountain road curved around every hill and valley.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: A sudden, strong feeling that fills you up.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: A powerful rush of emotion or energy that comes over you all at once.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: She felt a surge of excitement when she saw the surprise party.

pages.label: Page 10
pages.text: That night, as Swift settled into a cozy tree in their new winter home, she felt proud and brave and ready for whatever adventures lay ahead. The journey that had seemed so frightening had taught her something wonderful – she was stronger than she knew. Luna was right: every great adventure really did begin with just one wingbeat, and the best journeys were always better when shared with the ones you love.
pages.scene: Swift perched contentedly in a large, comfortable tree in the southern valley, with her family nearby, stars twinkling in the peaceful night sky above

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Warm, comfortable, and safe feeling.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Snug and comfortable in a way that makes you feel perfectly warm and content.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The cozy cabin had a fireplace and soft blankets everywhere.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Something that makes you feel scared or worried.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Causing fear or anxiety, making you feel nervous about what might happen next.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The loud thunder was frightening, but Mom said we were safe inside.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Exciting trips or experiences that are new and fun.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Thrilling journeys or experiences that take you to new places and teach you new things.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The summer camp promised many outdoor adventures like hiking and canoeing.

comprehensionQuestions.question: What was Swift worried about before her first migration?

options.text: Getting tired and not keeping up with the other eagles
Finding enough fish to eat along the way
Getting lost and flying in the wrong direction

comprehensionQuestions.question: How do you think Luna's advice helped Swift feel braver?

options.text: It reminded her that big journeys happen one step at a time
It told her exactly which way to fly south
It promised the weather would be perfect

comprehensionQuestions.question: Can you think of a time when you felt nervous about trying something new?

options.text: Starting at a new school or meeting new friends
Learning to ride a bike or swim
Performing in front of other people

predictionPause.question: What do you think will happen when Migration Day actually arrives?

options.text: Swift will feel brave and ready to fly with her family
Swift will still feel scared but will try anyway
Swift will decide to stay home this year

sp1.title: The Kickball Kid
sp1.obstacle: he's the smallest kid on the team and nobody passes to him

pages.label: Page 1
pages.text: Carlos loved kickball more than anything in the whole world. Every morning, he practiced in his backyard, kicking the red rubber ball against the wooden fence. The ball made a satisfying THUMP each time it bounced back to him.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: A small boy with curly dark hair kicking a bright red kickball in a sunny backyard, with a wooden fence and green grass

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Did something over and over to get better at it.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Repeated an activity many times to improve skills and become more confident at it.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: She practiced piano every day before her recital.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Something that feels really good and makes you happy inside.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: A feeling of contentment and pleasure when something meets your expectations perfectly.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: Finishing the puzzle was very satisfying after working on it all day.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: A bouncy material that stretches and snaps back into shape.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: A flexible material that can stretch and bounce back, often used for balls and toys.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The rubber band stretched far before snapping back.

pages.label: Page 2
pages.text: At school, Carlos was always picked last for kickball teams. He was the smallest kid in his class, with sneakers that barely reached the other kids' ankles. "Don't worry, you can play right field," the team captain would say with a shrug.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Carlos standing alone as other taller children are chosen for teams on a playground, looking hopeful despite being the smallest

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Chosen to be on a team or group.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Selected or chosen from among several people, usually for a team or special purpose.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: Maria was picked to be the line leader today.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: The leader of a team who makes important decisions.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: A person who leads a team and helps make decisions about how to play or work together.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The ship's captain steered through the stormy weather.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Soft shoes made for running and playing sports.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Athletic shoes with rubber soles designed for comfort and movement during sports and activities.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: I put on my sneakers before going to the playground.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Lifting your shoulders up and down to show you don't care.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: A gesture where you raise your shoulders briefly to show you don't know or don't care much.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: When asked about dessert, he gave a shrug and said whatever.

pages.label: Page 3
pages.text: During games, Carlos would wave his arms and shout, "I'm open! Pass to me!" But the ball never came his way. His teammates always kicked to the bigger kids, while Carlos stood ready in the bright afternoon sunshine, waiting for his chance.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Carlos with his arms raised high, calling out on a grassy field while other children play kickball in the distance

vocabWords.definition_4_7: People who play on the same team as you.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Other players who work together with you toward the same goal in a sport or game.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: My soccer teammates and I practiced passing the ball.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: The bright warm light that comes from the sun.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: The cheerful light and warmth that comes from the sun on a clear day.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The flowers grew tall in the warm sunshine.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: The time of day after lunch but before dinner.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: The part of the day that comes after noon and lasts until evening time.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: We had a picnic in the park this afternoon.

pages.label: Page 4
pages.text: One cloudy Tuesday, something different happened. The opposing team's best kicker sent a high, spinning ball sailing toward right field. All the children held their breath as the red ball arced through the gray sky, heading straight for Carlos.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: A red kickball flying high through cloudy sky toward Carlos, who is positioned in right field with his hands ready to catch

vocabWords.definition_4_7: When the sky is covered with gray or white clouds.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: A sky that is filled with clouds, making the day look gray and less sunny.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The cloudy weather made it perfect for staying inside and reading.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: The other team that you are playing against.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Belonging to the team or group that you are competing against in a game or contest.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The opposing army set up camp across the river.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Turning around and around very fast while moving.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Rotating rapidly while moving through the air, creating a twisting motion like a top.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The spinning washing machine made the clothes very clean.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Moved through the air in a curved path like a rainbow.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Traveled in a curved line through the air, like the shape of half a circle.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The basketball arced perfectly through the hoop.

pages.label: Page 5
pages.text: Carlos's heart pounded like a drum in his chest. This was his moment! He positioned his small hands carefully and caught the ball with a solid SMACK. "Out!" called the teacher. His teammates cheered and ran toward him with huge smiles.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Carlos holding the red ball triumphantly with a big smile, while his teammates run toward him cheering with their arms in the air

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Beat very fast and hard, like when you're excited or scared.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Beat rapidly and forcefully, usually describing a heart when someone feels strong emotions.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: Her heart pounded with excitement before the school play began.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Put something exactly where it needed to be.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Placed or arranged something carefully in just the right spot for the best result.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The photographer positioned the camera for the perfect shot.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: A very important time when something special happens.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: A brief but significant period of time when something meaningful or exciting occurs.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The moment the baby took her first steps was magical.

pages.label: Page 6
pages.text: "Nice catch, Carlos!" called Maya, the team captain. "You saved the game!" Carlos beamed with pride, the red ball still warm in his hands. For the first time, he felt like a real part of the team, not just the smallest kid in right field.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Maya and other teammates patting Carlos on the back and smiling, with Carlos glowing with happiness and pride

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Smiled very big and bright because you feel so happy.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Showed a huge, radiant smile that comes from feeling proud and joyful inside.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: She beamed when her grandma said her drawing was beautiful.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: The good feeling you get when you do something really well.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: A warm feeling of satisfaction and self-respect that comes from achieving something important.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: He felt pride when he helped his little sister learn to ride a bike.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Shouted happy things to show someone you think they did great.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Called out with excitement and enthusiasm to celebrate someone's success or encourage them.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The crowd cheered when the firefighter rescued the cat.

pages.label: Page 7
pages.text: The next inning, something magical happened. When Carlos came to kick, Maya called out, "Come on, Carlos! Show them what you've got!" The smooth rubber ball sat waiting on home plate, and Carlos took a deep breath of the fresh spring air.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Carlos standing at home plate ready to kick, with the ball on the ground and Maya encouraging him from the sidelines

vocabWords.definition_4_7: A part of the game when each team gets a turn.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: One round of play in a ball game where both teams get chances to kick and field.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: Our baseball team scored three runs in the fifth inning.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Something so wonderful it feels like magic happened.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Something so special and amazing that it feels like it could only happen by magic.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The first snowfall of winter was magical to watch.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Having a surface that feels flat and soft to touch.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Having an even surface without bumps or rough spots, pleasant to touch or look at.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The smooth stone felt nice in her hand.

pages.label: Page 8
pages.text: Carlos ran toward the ball with determination in his bright brown eyes. His small foot connected perfectly, sending the ball soaring over the infield. "Run, Carlos, run!" his teammates shouted as he sprinted toward first base with joy in every step.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Carlos mid-kick with the ball flying off his foot, his face showing determination and joy, with teammates cheering in the background

vocabWords.definition_4_7: The strong feeling of really wanting to do something and not giving up.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: A powerful inner strength that helps you keep trying hard even when things are difficult.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: With determination, she practiced violin until she could play the song perfectly.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: When your foot touched the ball just right.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Made solid contact between two things, like when a foot hits a ball perfectly.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The hammer connected with the nail and drove it into the wood.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Flying high up in the air really fast.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Moving upward through the air with grace and speed, like a bird in flight.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The eagle was soaring above the mountain peaks.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Ran as fast as you possibly could.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Ran at top speed for a short distance, using all your energy and strength.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: She sprinted to catch the bus before it left the stop.

pages.label: Page 9
pages.text: From that day forward, Carlos was no longer just "the small kid." His teammates passed to him, counted on him, and celebrated with him. He learned that being the smallest didn't mean being the weakest – it meant being quick, determined, and full of heart.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Carlos playing happily with his teammates in a circle, all smiling and including him as an equal part of the group

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Had a happy party or special time because something good happened.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Showed joy and happiness together with others because of a special achievement or event.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: We celebrated my grandmother's birthday with cake and presents.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: The one with the least strength or power.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Having the smallest amount of physical or inner strength compared to everyone else.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: Even the weakest seedling can grow into a mighty tree.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Really wanting to do something and not giving up easily.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Having a strong will and refusing to quit even when facing challenges or difficulties.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The determined ant kept climbing up the hill despite falling down.

pages.label: Page 10
pages.text: Every morning, Carlos still practiced in his backyard, kicking the red ball against the fence. But now he kicked with confidence, knowing that believing in himself had made all the difference. The morning sun felt warmer, and his smile stretched wider than ever before.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Carlos kicking the ball in his backyard with a huge confident smile, golden morning sunlight streaming down, showing his growth and happiness

vocabWords.definition_4_7: The good feeling of knowing you can do something well.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: A strong belief in yourself and your abilities that helps you feel brave and capable.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: With confidence, she raised her hand to answer the difficult question.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Thinking something is true and feeling sure about it.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Having strong faith or trust that something is real, true, or will happen.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: Believing in yourself is the first step to reaching your dreams.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: When something changes and becomes better or not the same as before.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: A change that makes things better or creates a new and improved situation from before.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: Studying every day made a big difference in her test scores.

comprehensionQuestions.question: Why was Carlos always picked last for kickball teams?

options.text: He was the smallest kid in his class
He didn't like playing kickball
He always came late to school

comprehensionQuestions.question: What do you think made Carlos feel different after he caught the ball?

options.text: He felt proud and like a real teammate
He wanted to quit playing kickball
He felt scared of catching more balls

comprehensionQuestions.question: How do you feel when you try something hard and it works out well?

options.text: Happy and proud of myself
Excited and ready to try again
Confident that I can do more things

predictionPause.question: The ball is coming straight toward Carlos! What do you think will happen next?

options.text: Carlos will catch the ball and make a great play
Carlos will miss the ball and feel sad
Another player will run over and catch it instead

sp2.title: Swim Day
sp2.obstacle: she's afraid to put her face in the water at swim class

pages.label: Page 1
pages.text: Ruby loves swim day! She puts on her bright pink swimsuit. The pool water is warm and blue.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: A happy little girl with curly hair putting on a bright pink swimsuit in a colorful changing room, with swimming goggles hanging nearby

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Special clothes you wear when you go swimming in water.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: A special outfit designed to wear in the water when you swim or play at the pool.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: Dad packed his swimsuit for the beach trip.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Something that has lots of color and light.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Having vivid colors or giving off lots of light that makes things easy to see.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The bright yellow sun made me squint my eyes.

vocabWords.definition_2_4: A place full of water for swimming.
vocabWords.definition_4_7: A big area filled with water where people swim.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: A large container or area filled with water where people can swim and play safely.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The ducks splashed in the pool at the park.

pages.label: Page 2
pages.text: Ruby kicks her legs in the water. Splash! She moves her arms back and forth. Swimming is fun!
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Ruby sitting on the pool edge, kicking her legs in the crystal blue water with small splashes around her feet, smiling brightly

vocabWords.definition_4_7: To move your legs quickly back and forth.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: To push or move your legs in a quick, strong motion through the water or air.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The soccer player kicks the ball into the goal.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: The sound and movement when water jumps around.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: The noise and spray that happens when something hits water or water moves quickly around.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The dog made a big splash when he jumped in the lake.

vocabWords.definition_2_4: Moving in water with your body.
vocabWords.definition_4_7: Moving through water using your arms and legs.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: The activity of moving through water by coordinating movements of your arms, legs, and body.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: Swimming in the ocean is different from swimming in a lake.

pages.label: Page 3
pages.text: Coach Sarah says, 'Time to put your face in the water!' Ruby feels scared. The water looks so deep.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: A kind swimming instructor with a whistle around her neck talking to Ruby, who looks worried while standing in shallow pool water

vocabWords.definition_2_4: A teacher who helps you learn.
vocabWords.definition_4_7: A person who teaches you how to do sports.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Someone whose job is to teach and help people learn new skills in sports or activities.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The coach taught us how to throw the baseball correctly.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: When you feel afraid or nervous about something happening.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: The feeling you get when you think something might be dangerous or make you uncomfortable.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: I felt scared during the thunderstorm last night.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Something that goes way down from the top.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Extending far down from the surface, like a hole or water that goes way below.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The deep hole in our yard was perfect for planting the big tree.

pages.label: Page 4
pages.text: Ruby watches her friend Ben. He puts his face down. Bubbles come up! But Ruby shakes her head no.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: A boy putting his face in the water with bubbles floating up, while Ruby stands nearby looking hesitant but curious

vocabWords.definition_4_7: To look at something or someone for a while.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: To observe something carefully by looking at it closely to see what happens or how it works.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: Mom watches the birds eating seeds in our backyard.

vocabWords.definition_2_4: Little balls of air in water.
vocabWords.definition_4_7: Round balls of air that float up in water.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Small spheres of air that form in liquid and rise to the surface, often making a popping sound.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: I love blowing soap bubbles that float in the air.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: To move something quickly from side to side.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: To move rapidly back and forth or up and down, often to show disagreement or emphasis.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The dog shakes his wet fur after his bath.

pages.label: Page 5
pages.text: Coach Sarah holds Ruby's hand. 'Let's try together,' she says. 'Just your chin first.' Ruby nods slowly.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Coach Sarah gently holding Ruby's hand in the shallow water, both of them smiling, with the coach demonstrating by putting just her chin in the water

vocabWords.definition_4_7: To keep something in your hands or arms.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: To grasp or support something firmly with your hands, arms, or body to keep it secure.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: Grandpa holds my hand when we cross the street.

vocabWords.definition_2_4: At the same time with someone.
vocabWords.definition_4_7: Doing something at the same time with another person.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Being with someone else or doing an activity at the same time as another person or group.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: We built the puzzle together on the kitchen table.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: The bottom part of your face below your mouth.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: The rounded part of your face that sits below your mouth and above your neck.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: I have a small cut on my chin from falling off my bike.

pages.label: Page 6
pages.text: Ruby puts her chin in the water. It feels cool and tickly! 'That's not so scary,' she thinks.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Close-up of Ruby carefully putting just her chin in the clear blue water, with small ripples around her face and a look of pleasant surprise

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Something that feels a little bit cold but nice.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Having a temperature that is lower than warm but not freezing, often feeling refreshing and pleasant.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The cool breeze felt good on the hot summer day.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: A feeling that makes you want to giggle or laugh.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: A light, gentle sensation on your skin that creates a funny feeling and makes you want to laugh.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The feather felt tickly when it touched my arm.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Something that makes you feel worried or frightened.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Causing feelings of fear or nervousness because it seems dangerous or unknown to you.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The scary movie had lots of loud noises and dark scenes.

pages.label: Page 7
pages.text: Now Ruby tries her mouth. She closes it tight. The water feels funny but nice. She giggles.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Ruby with her mouth just touching the water surface, eyes wide with surprise and delight, tiny bubbles near her lips

vocabWords.definition_2_4: The part of your face that eats.
vocabWords.definition_4_7: The opening in your face where you eat and talk.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: The opening in your face with lips and teeth that you use for eating, drinking, and speaking.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: I brush my teeth to keep my mouth clean and healthy.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: To shut something so it's not open anymore.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: To bring parts together so that an opening becomes shut and nothing can get in or out.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: Dad closes the door when he leaves for work each morning.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: To laugh in a light, happy way with small sounds.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: To laugh quietly with short, light sounds that show you find something funny or delightful.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The baby giggles when I make funny faces at her.

pages.label: Page 8
pages.text: Ruby takes a big breath. She puts her whole face in! Under water, everything is quiet and blue.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Ruby with her entire face under the crystal clear water, her curly hair floating around her head, peaceful expression visible underwater

vocabWords.definition_2_4: Air that goes in and out.
vocabWords.definition_4_7: The air that goes into and out of your body.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: The air that flows into your lungs when you breathe in and flows out when you breathe out.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: I can see my breath in the cold winter air.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: All of something, not just a part of it.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: The complete thing or entire amount, with nothing missing or left out from the total.
