vocabWords.exampleSentence: I ate the whole apple, even the skin and core.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Without noise or sounds, very peaceful and still.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Making very little or no sound at all, creating a peaceful and calm atmosphere around you.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The library is always quiet so people can read and study.

pages.label: Page 9
pages.text: Ruby lifts her head up. 'I did it!' she shouts. Coach Sarah claps. Ben cheers for Ruby too.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Ruby popping her head up from the water with a huge triumphant smile, water droplets sparkling around her, Coach Sarah and Ben clapping in the background

vocabWords.definition_4_7: To move something up from where it was before.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: To raise or move something upward from a lower position using strength or effort.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The crane lifts heavy boxes onto the truck.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: To say something in a very loud voice.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: To speak or call out with a loud, strong voice so that people far away can hear you clearly.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The coach shouts instructions to the players during the game.

vocabWords.definition_2_4: Hits hands together to make noise.
vocabWords.definition_4_7: To hit your hands together to make a sound.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: To strike your palms together repeatedly to make noise, usually to show that you like something.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: Everyone claps when the music ends at the concert.

pages.label: Page 10
pages.text: Ruby puts her face in again and again. She was brave! Now swimming is even more fun.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Ruby confidently swimming with her face in the water, creating a trail of bubbles, looking completely comfortable and joyful in the bright blue pool

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Doing the same thing one more time or many times.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Repeating an action or activity that you have already done before, often multiple times in a row.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: Can you read that story again before bedtime?

vocabWords.definition_2_4: Not scared to try hard things.
vocabWords.definition_4_7: Being strong and not afraid when something seems scary.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Having courage to do difficult or frightening things even when you feel nervous or worried inside.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The brave firefighter ran into the building to help people.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Something that is more or better than it was before.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Used to show that something is greater, stronger, or more impressive than what came before it.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The cake tastes even better with ice cream on top.

sp3.title: The Big Race
sp3.obstacle: he trips and falls during the school race

pages.label: Page 1
pages.text: Jasper bounced excitedly in his bright red sneakers. Today was the big school race, and colorful ribbons fluttered from the starting line. The morning air smelled fresh and crisp, perfect for running. All the children gathered on the soft green grass, stretching their legs and warming up.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: A cheerful boy with curly hair wearing red sneakers stands on a grassy field with other children. Colorful ribbons and banners decorate a starting line. The school building is visible in the background under a bright blue sky.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Moved up and down quickly because you're excited or happy.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Jumped or moved with quick, energetic movements, like a ball bouncing on the ground.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The basketball bounced high after hitting the concrete.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Moved back and forth gently in the wind or air.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Moved lightly and quickly back and forth, like butterfly wings or flags in a breeze.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The butterfly's wings fluttered as it landed on the flower.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Fresh, clean, and a little bit cool air.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Fresh and cool, with a clean feeling that makes you feel awake and energetic.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The crisp autumn leaves crunched under my feet.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Came together in the same place as a group.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Came together from different places to meet in one spot, like flowers gathered in a bouquet.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The family gathered around the dinner table to eat.

pages.label: Page 2
pages.text: Jasper had been practicing every day after school. He loved the feeling of wind whooshing past his ears as he ran. His mom had packed his favorite granola bar for energy, and his dad waved from the sidelines with a big smile.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Jasper running on a track during practice, his hair flowing in the wind. His parents stand nearby cheering, with his mom holding a lunch bag and his dad waving enthusiastically.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Doing something over and over to get better at it.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Repeating an activity many times to improve your skills and become better at it.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: She was practicing piano every day before her recital.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Making a fast, rushing sound as air moves quickly.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: The sound of air or wind moving very fast past something, like a swoosh.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The car made a whooshing sound as it drove past us.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: A healthy snack made of oats, nuts, and sweet things.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: A crunchy food made from rolled oats, nuts, and honey that gives you energy.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: I sprinkled granola on top of my yogurt for breakfast.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: The area next to a sports field where people watch.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: The space along the edge of a playing area where spectators stand to cheer.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The coach shouted instructions from the sidelines during the soccer game.

pages.label: Page 3
pages.text: The whistle blew with a sharp, clear sound. All the runners took their positions at the starting line. Jasper's heart thumped like a drum in his chest. He took a deep breath and focused on the finish line ahead, where yellow and blue flags danced in the breeze.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Children lined up at a starting line in running positions. A teacher holds a whistle. Jasper is in the center, focused and ready. Finish line flags wave in the distance.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: A small thing that makes a loud, sharp sound when blown.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: A small instrument that creates a high, piercing sound when you blow air through it.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The lifeguard blew her whistle to get the swimmers' attention.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: The places where people stand or sit to be ready.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: The specific spots or places where people need to be for an activity to start.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The dancers got into their positions before the music began.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Beat hard and fast, like your heart when you're excited.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Made a strong, rhythmic beating sound, like a drum or an excited heartbeat.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The rabbit's foot thumped on the ground as it hopped away.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Looked at something carefully and thought only about that thing.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Concentrated all your attention on one thing, blocking out everything else around you.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: She focused on her homework and finished it quickly.

pages.label: Page 4
pages.text: "Ready, set, GO!" shouted the teacher. Jasper's red sneakers pounded against the track as he sprinted forward. The crowd cheered loudly, and Jasper felt like he was flying. He was running faster than he ever had before, keeping up with the front runners.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Jasper and other children racing down a track, with Jasper in the lead group. His red sneakers are clearly visible as he runs. Spectators cheer from the sidelines with raised hands and happy faces.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Hit the ground hard and fast while running or walking.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Struck the surface repeatedly with force, like feet hitting the ground during a run.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The rain pounded on the roof all night long.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Ran as fast as you possibly can for a short time.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Ran at top speed with maximum effort, like a cheetah chasing its prey.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The dog sprinted across the yard to catch the frisbee.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Shouted happy, encouraging words to support someone you like.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Yelled out loud with excitement and joy to show support for someone doing well.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The crowd cheered when the home team scored a goal.

pages.label: Page 5
pages.text: Halfway through the race, Jasper was still doing great. The sun felt warm on his face, and his breathing was steady and strong. Other runners were beside him, but he felt confident and happy. The finish line was getting closer and closer with each step.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Jasper running confidently in the middle of the race, with the sun shining down. Other children run nearby. The track curves ahead, and the finish line is visible in the distance.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: In the middle of something, not at the start or end.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: At the midpoint of a journey or activity, exactly between the beginning and the finish.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: We stopped halfway up the mountain to rest and drink water.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Taking air into your lungs and letting it out through your nose.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: The process of drawing air into your body and releasing it to stay alive and energized.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: After climbing the stairs, his breathing was heavy and fast.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Not changing, staying the same in a calm, even way.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Remaining constant and controlled, without shaking or changing speed unexpectedly.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: She held the camera steady to take a clear picture.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Feeling sure about yourself and believing you can do something well.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Having strong belief in your abilities and feeling certain that things will go well.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: She felt confident about her spelling test because she studied hard.

pages.label: Page 6
pages.text: Suddenly, Jasper's foot caught on a small bump in the track. Down he went with a thud, landing on his hands and knees! The rough track scraped his palms, and his red sneakers got dusty. He watched as other runners zoomed past him toward the finish line.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Jasper falling forward on the track, his hands reaching out to break his fall. His red sneakers are visible, and dust kicks up around him. Other runners continue past in the background.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Something that happens very fast without any warning at all.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: In an instant, without warning or time to prepare, like a surprise that catches you off guard.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: Suddenly, it started raining and everyone ran for cover.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Got stuck on something or grabbed by something you didn't expect.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Became trapped or snagged on something, like getting your sleeve caught on a branch.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: My kite caught in the tree and wouldn't come down.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: A heavy, dull sound when something falls down hard.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: A deep, muffled sound made when something heavy hits the ground or another surface.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The book fell off the shelf and hit the floor with a thud.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Got hurt when your skin rubbed against something rough and hard.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Rubbed against a rough surface, causing the skin to get scratched or slightly injured.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: I scraped my knee when I fell off my bicycle.

pages.label: Page 7
pages.text: Jasper sat on the warm track for a moment, feeling disappointed. His knees stung a little, and his hands were dirty from the fall. He could hear the crowd still cheering, but now they seemed so far away. Maybe he should just stay here and wait for the race to end.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Jasper sitting on the track looking sad and dusty, examining his scraped hands. The other runners are much further ahead now. The crowd is visible but distant in the background.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Feeling sad because something didn't go the way you wanted.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Feeling let down and unhappy when something you hoped for doesn't happen as expected.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: She was disappointed when her birthday party got canceled.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Hurt with a sharp, burning feeling that goes away slowly.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Caused a sharp, burning pain that feels prickly and uncomfortable for a while.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The soap stung my eyes when I washed my face.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Covered with dust, mud, or other messy stuff that needs cleaning.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Not clean, with soil, dust, or grime covering the surface that needs to be washed off.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: After playing in the garden, my clothes were dirty and needed washing.

pages.label: Page 8
pages.text: Then Jasper heard his dad's voice calling out, "You can do it, Jasper!" His mom was clapping and smiling encouragingly. Jasper looked at the finish line ahead and remembered how much he loved running. The race wasn't over yet, and neither was he!
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Jasper looking up from the track toward his parents who are cheering him on. His dad has his hands cupped around his mouth, calling out. His mom is clapping with a warm, encouraging smile.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Shouting someone's name loudly so they can hear you from far away.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Yelling out to someone in a loud voice to get their attention or send a message.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: Mom was calling my name from the kitchen when dinner was ready.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Hitting your hands together to make noise and show you're happy.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Striking your palms together repeatedly to make sound and show approval or excitement.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: Everyone was clapping after the amazing magic show ended.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: In a way that helps someone feel brave and want to try.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: In a supportive manner that gives someone confidence and hope to keep going forward.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The teacher smiled encouragingly when I raised my hand to answer.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: The strong feeling of wanting to finish something no matter what.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: The powerful inner strength that keeps you going even when things get difficult or challenging.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: With determination, she practiced violin until she could play the song perfectly.

pages.label: Page 9
pages.text: Jasper brushed the dust off his red sneakers and stood up tall. His legs felt strong again, and his heart filled with determination. He started running, one step at a time, then faster and faster. The wind felt wonderful in his hair as he picked up speed.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Jasper standing up and dusting off his red sneakers, then beginning to run again. His expression shows determination and renewed energy. His hair flows behind him as he gains speed.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Cleaned something off by wiping it away with your hand.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Swept away dirt or debris with a gentle wiping motion using your hand or a tool.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: She brushed the crumbs off the table after eating her sandwich.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Tiny pieces of dirt that float in the air and land on things.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Fine particles of earth or other matter that settle on surfaces and make them look dirty.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: I had to dust the bookshelf because it was covered in gray powder.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Really, really good and making you feel very happy inside.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Extremely pleasant and delightful, causing feelings of joy and amazement.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: We had a wonderful time at the beach building sandcastles.

pages.label: Page 10
pages.text: Jasper crossed the finish line with a huge smile! He didn't win first place, but he finished the race. His parents rushed over with big hugs and told him how proud they were. The other runners cheered for him too, and Jasper felt like a champion.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Jasper crossing the finish line with arms raised in celebration, a big smile on his face. His parents run toward him with open arms. Other children and spectators are clapping and cheering in the background.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Went from one side to the other side of something.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Moved from one side of a line or area to the opposite side, completing a journey.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: We crossed the street carefully at the crosswalk.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Moved very quickly to get to someone or somewhere fast.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Hurried with great speed and urgency, like water rushing down a river.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: Dad rushed to the store before it closed to buy milk.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Feeling really good about something you or someone else did well.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Having a feeling of deep satisfaction and happiness about an achievement or good behavior.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: I felt proud when I helped my little sister learn to ride her bike.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Someone who wins or is the very best at something important.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: A person who achieves victory or shows exceptional skill and courage in competition or life.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The champion swimmer won three gold medals at the competition.

pages.label: Page 11
pages.text: That evening, Jasper looked at his slightly scuffed red sneakers and grinned. He had learned something important today: falling down doesn't mean giving up. Tomorrow, he would practice again, because running made him happy, and he was proud that he never stopped trying.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Jasper at home in the evening, sitting on his bed and looking at his red sneakers with a content smile. Soft lamplight fills the cozy room. Through the window, the sun is setting with warm orange and pink colors.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: The time when the day ends and it starts getting dark.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: The later part of the day when the sun goes down and night begins to arrive.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: We ate dinner together in the evening after Dad came home from work.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: A little bit scratched or worn from being used and bumped around.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Marked with scratches or worn spots from use, but still good and usable.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: My scuffed backpack showed all the adventures I'd been on.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Smiled really big and wide to show you're very happy.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Showed a broad, joyful smile that stretched across your face from ear to ear.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: She grinned when she saw the surprise birthday cake.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Stopping trying to do something because it feels too hard.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Deciding to quit or stop working toward a goal when things become difficult or challenging.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: Even when the puzzle was hard, I kept trying instead of giving up.

comprehensionQuestions.question: What happened to Jasper during the race that made him fall down?

options.text: His foot caught on a bump in the track
He tripped over another runner
His shoelaces came untied

comprehensionQuestions.question: Why do you think Jasper decided to get up and keep running after he fell?

options.text: He heard his parents cheering and remembered he loved running
He wanted to win first place in the race
The teacher told him he had to finish

comprehensionQuestions.question: How would you feel if you fell down during something important to you?

options.text: Sad and disappointed, but I'd want to try again
Frustrated, but proud if I kept going
Nervous, but brave enough to finish

predictionPause.question: What do you think Jasper will do next?

options.text: Get back up and keep running in the race
Stay sitting and wait for someone to help him
Walk slowly to the finish line

sp4.title: Skateboard Dreams
sp4.obstacle: she keeps falling trying to learn a new skateboard trick

pages.label: Page 1
pages.text: Maya rolled her bright purple skateboard back and forth on the smooth concrete of Sunset Skate Park. The morning sun painted golden streaks across the empty ramps and rails. She had been practicing basic tricks for months, but today felt different. Today, she was determined to land her first kickflip. Maya took a deep breath, feeling the cool morning breeze on her face, and pushed off toward the practice area.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Maya standing at a colorful skate park in golden morning light, holding her purple skateboard, looking determined and excited

vocabWords.definition_4_7: A hard, gray material used to make sidewalks and buildings.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: A sturdy building material made from cement, sand, and rocks that hardens into a solid surface.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The workers poured concrete to make a new driveway for our house.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Really wanting to do something and not giving up easily.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Having a strong will and firm purpose to achieve a goal, even when it's difficult.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: Sarah was determined to finish reading the entire book before bedtime.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: A skateboard trick where the board spins under your feet.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: A skateboarding move where you flip the board with your foot and catch it while jumping.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The skater practiced kickflips at the local skate park every weekend.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Long, thin lines of light or color across something.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Narrow bands or strips that appear to stretch across a surface, often made of light.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: Lightning streaks flashed across the dark storm clouds.

pages.label: Page 2
pages.text: Maya positioned her feet carefully on the skateboard, just like her older brother Jake had shown her. Left foot near the front bolts, right foot on the tail. She bent her knees, focused on the trick in her mind, and flicked her front foot. The board spun beneath her, but she couldn't catch it. THUD! Maya tumbled to the ground, her knee guards scraping against the rough concrete.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Maya mid-fall with her purple skateboard spinning in the air above her, knee pads visible, determined expression on her face

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Put something in exactly the right place or spot.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Carefully placed or arranged something in a specific location for a particular purpose.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The photographer positioned the camera to get the best shot of the sunset.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Metal screws that hold parts of something together tightly.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Strong metal fasteners with threads that secure different pieces of equipment or machinery together.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: Dad used bolts to attach the swing set to the wooden frame.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Made a quick, sharp movement with your finger or hand.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Moved something quickly and lightly with a sudden snap or quick motion of the fingers.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: Mom flicked the light switch to turn on the kitchen lights.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Fell down in a rolling or messy way.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Lost balance and fell in an uncontrolled way, often rolling or bouncing while falling.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The puppy tumbled down the grassy hill while chasing the ball.

pages.label: Page 3
pages.text: "Ow," Maya muttered, brushing dust off her bright green helmet. The fall stung a little, but not enough to stop her. She picked up her skateboard and examined the colorful grip tape her mom had helped her choose. The rainbow stripes reminded her why she loved skating so much. Maya rolled her shoulders, adjusted her wrist guards, and set up for another try.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Maya sitting on the ground examining her rainbow-striped skateboard, wearing a bright green helmet and protective gear, with a slight smile

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Said something quietly in a grumpy or annoyed way.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Spoke in a low voice, usually when feeling frustrated or talking to yourself quietly.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: Jake muttered complaints about having to clean his messy bedroom.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Looked at something very carefully to see all the details.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Studied or inspected something closely to understand it better or check its condition.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The doctor examined my throat to see why it was sore.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Changed or moved something to make it fit better.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Made small changes to something's position or settings to improve how it works or feels.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: I adjusted my bike seat to make it the perfect height.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Lines of different colors that go across something.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Bands or strips of contrasting colors arranged in parallel lines across a surface.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The zebra's black and white stripes help it blend in with the herd.

pages.label: Page 4
pages.text: This time, Maya tried to flick her foot harder. She pushed off, gained some speed, and attempted the kickflip again. The skateboard spun wildly, but in the wrong direction. Maya's arms windmilled as she tried to keep her balance, but gravity won. She landed on her side with a soft thump on the smooth concrete, her elbow pads cushioning the impact.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Maya with arms outstretched trying to balance as her skateboard spins incorrectly beneath her, motion lines showing movement

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Tried to do something, especially something difficult or challenging.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Made an effort to accomplish or achieve something, often requiring skill or courage.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The mountain climber attempted to reach the summit before the storm arrived.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: In a crazy, out-of-control way without any plan.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Moving or behaving in an uncontrolled, chaotic manner without direction or restraint.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The leaves blew wildly in the strong wind during the thunderstorm.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Moved arms around in big circles like windmill blades.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Rotated the arms in large circular motions, resembling the spinning blades of a windmill.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The swimmer windmilled her arms to practice her backstroke technique.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Making something softer so it doesn't hurt when you hit it.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Providing a soft protective layer that absorbs impact and prevents injury or damage.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The thick mattress was cushioning my fall when I jumped on the bed.

pages.label: Page 5
pages.text: Maya sat up and noticed other skaters starting to arrive at the park. She watched a girl about her age effortlessly glide up a small ramp, her long braids flowing behind her. Maya felt a flutter of doubt in her stomach. Maybe kickflips were too hard for her. She picked up her skateboard and considered heading home, where her cozy room and art supplies waited for her.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Maya sitting on the ground looking uncertain, watching another girl with braids skateboarding smoothly in the background

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Doing something easily without trying hard or struggling at all.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Performing an action smoothly and naturally, making it look simple even when it requires skill.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The dancer moved effortlessly across the stage like she was floating on air.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: A quick, light feeling that moves around inside you.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: A brief, trembling sensation or nervous feeling that seems to move lightly within you.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: I felt a flutter of excitement when I saw my name on the winner's list.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Hair that is twisted together in long, rope-like sections.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Strands of hair woven together by crossing sections over and under in a repeating pattern.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: My sister wore two braids decorated with colorful ribbons to school.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Warm, comfortable, and nice to be in or around.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Having a pleasant feeling of warmth, comfort, and safety that makes you want to stay.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: We built a cozy fort with blankets and pillows in the living room.

pages.label: Page 6
pages.text: Just then, Maya remembered what her grandmother always said: "Every expert was once a beginner, mija." She thought about her first day on the skateboard, when she couldn't even push without falling. Now she could cruise around the entire park! Maya stood up, dusted off her favorite tie-dye t-shirt, and gripped her skateboard tighter. One more try, she decided. Maybe ten more tries.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Maya standing up with renewed determination, holding her skateboard, with a thoughtful and hopeful expression

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Someone who is really good at doing something special.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: A person who has developed exceptional skill and knowledge in a particular area through practice.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The cooking expert taught us how to make perfect chocolate chip cookies.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Someone who is just starting to learn how to do something.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: A person who is new to an activity and is still learning the basic skills.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: As a beginner at piano, I practice simple songs with both hands.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Move along smoothly and easily without much effort or worry.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Travel or move in a relaxed, steady manner, often with confidence and control.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: We like to cruise around the neighborhood on our bikes after dinner.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: A way of making colorful swirly patterns on clothes.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: A technique for creating colorful designs on fabric by tying sections and dyeing them different colors.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: We made tie-dye shirts at summer camp using bright blues and greens.

pages.label: Page 7
pages.text: Maya rolled forward again, this time focusing on staying calm. She bent her knees, kept her shoulders straight, and flicked her front foot with just the right amount of force. The skateboard spun beneath her feet like it was supposed to! For a split second, Maya felt like she was flying. Then she missed the landing and stumbled forward, catching herself with her hands.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Maya in mid-air above her spinning skateboard with an excited expression, arms out for balance, almost landing the trick

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Paying attention to one thing and not thinking about other things.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Concentrating your thoughts and energy on a specific task while ignoring distractions around you.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: I was focusing so hard on my math homework that I didn't hear the doorbell.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: The amount of strength or power you use to push something.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Physical strength or energy applied to move, change, or affect something in a particular way.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: You need just the right amount of force to open a jar without breaking it.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: A very short amount of time, like when you blink.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: An extremely brief moment that passes so quickly it's almost impossible to measure.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: For a split second, I thought I saw a shooting star flash across the sky.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Almost fell down but caught yourself before hitting the ground.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Lost your balance and nearly fell, often taking awkward steps to avoid falling completely.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: I stumbled over the tree root but managed to stay on my feet.

pages.label: Page 8
pages.text: "I almost had it!" Maya exclaimed, her eyes sparkling with excitement. That attempt felt completely different from the others. She could feel the trick coming together, like pieces of a puzzle clicking into place. The girl with braids skated over and gave Maya a thumbs up. "You're getting close!" she called out cheerfully. Maya beamed and set up for another attempt, her confidence growing.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Maya looking excited and proud, with the girl with braids giving her a thumbs up in the background, both smiling

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Said something loudly because you were excited or surprised.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Spoke with strong emotion and enthusiasm, often shouting or speaking louder than usual.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: Anna exclaimed with joy when she found her lost teddy bear under the bed.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Shining brightly with tiny flashes of light that twinkle.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Glittering or gleaming with small points of light that seem to dance and shine brilliantly.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The lake was sparkling in the sunlight like it was covered with diamonds.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: A game with pieces that fit together to make a picture.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: A challenging problem or game where different parts must be arranged correctly to create a solution.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: We worked on a 500-piece puzzle of a beautiful mountain landscape.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Feeling sure that you can do something well and succeed.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: A strong belief in your own abilities and the feeling that you can handle challenges successfully.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: Playing violin in front of my family helped build my confidence for the school concert.

pages.label: Page 9
pages.text: Maya took a deep breath and pushed off once more. This time, everything felt perfect. Her feet knew exactly where to go, her body stayed balanced, and when she flicked her front foot, the skateboard spun beautifully beneath her. Maya caught the board with both feet and rode away smoothly, her heart pounding with pure joy. She had done it! Her first kickflip was complete!
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Maya successfully landing on her skateboard after completing the kickflip, arms raised in triumph, huge smile on her face

vocabWords.definition_4_7: In a lovely, pretty way that makes you happy to look at.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: In an attractive, graceful manner that creates a sense of wonder and visual pleasure.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The ballet dancer moved beautifully across the stage in her flowing costume.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Staying steady and not falling over to one side.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Maintaining stability and control by keeping your weight evenly distributed and centered.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The tightrope walker balanced carefully as she crossed high above the circus crowd.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Moving easily without bumps, jerks, or sudden stops along the way.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Flowing or operating in an even, continuous manner without interruption or rough movements.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The ice skater glided smoothly across the rink without wobbling or stumbling.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Beating very fast and hard, like your heart when excited.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Beating rapidly and forcefully, often due to strong emotions like excitement or fear.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: My heart was pounding with nervousness before I gave my presentation to the class.

pages.label: Page 10
pages.text: "YES!" Maya shouted, pumping her fist in the air as she rolled to a stop. The girl with braids and several other skaters cheered for her, their applause echoing across the park. Maya couldn't stop grinning as she looked at her trusty purple skateboard. All those falls had been worth it. She had learned that the best tricks come to those who never give up trying, no matter how many times they tumble along the way.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Maya celebrating with other young skaters cheering around her, the skate park full of life and color, everyone smiling and clapping

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Moving your arm up and down quickly to show you're excited.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Making repeated forceful movements, often with the arm or fist, to express triumph or victory.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The winning team was pumping their fists in celebration after scoring the goal.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: The sound of people clapping their hands to show they liked something.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Enthusiastic clapping by a group of people to express approval, appreciation, or congratulations.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The audience burst into loud applause when the magician made the rabbit appear.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: A sound that bounces back and repeats itself in a big space.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Sound waves that reflect off surfaces and return, creating a repeated version of the original noise.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: Our voices were echoing in the empty gymnasium as we called to each other.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Something you can always count on to work well for you.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Reliable and dependable, something that consistently performs well and never lets you down.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: My trusty backpack has carried my books to school every day for three years.

comprehensionQuestions.question: What trick was Maya trying to learn at the skate park?

options.text: A kickflip
A handstand
A cartwheel

comprehensionQuestions.question: Why do you think Maya kept trying even after falling several times?

options.text: She remembered her grandmother's advice about not giving up
She was afraid of what other skaters would think
She wanted to impress her brother Jake

comprehensionQuestions.question: How do you feel when you're trying to learn something new and it's really hard?

options.text: Frustrated but determined to keep trying
Excited about the challenge ahead
Sometimes wanting to give up but then finding courage

predictionPause.question: Maya felt like she was flying for a split second! What do you think will happen on her next attempt?

options.text: She'll finally land the kickflip perfectly
She'll fall again but feel more confident
She'll decide to try a different trick instead

sp5.title: Catch and Throw
sp5.obstacle: he can't catch the ball no matter how hard he tries

pages.label: Page 1
pages.text: Benny has a red ball. It is soft and bouncy. He loves to throw it up high!
pages.scene: illustration prompt: A happy little boy with curly hair holding a bright red rubber ball in a sunny backyard with green grass

vocabWords.definition_2_4: Something that jumps up and down.
vocabWords.definition_4_7: When something can bounce or spring back up after hitting something.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Having the ability to bounce back quickly and energetically when it hits a surface.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The trampoline is bouncy and fun to jump on.

vocabWords.definition_2_4: To send something through the air.
vocabWords.definition_4_7: When you use your arm to send something flying through the air.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: To propel something forward through the air using force from your arm and hand.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: I like to throw stones into the pond.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Something that feels gentle and squishy when you touch it.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Having a texture that yields easily to pressure and feels pleasant to touch.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The kitten's fur is soft and fluffy.

pages.label: Page 2
pages.text: Daddy throws the ball to Benny. "Catch it!" says Daddy. The ball goes BONK on the ground.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: A kind father tossing a red ball toward little Benny, who has his arms outstretched but the ball is bouncing on the grass beside him

vocabWords.definition_4_7: When someone uses their arm to send something flying toward you.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: The action of propelling an object through the air using arm movement and force.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The pitcher throws the baseball to home plate.

vocabWords.definition_2_4: To grab something flying toward you.
vocabWords.definition_4_7: When you use your hands to grab something coming at you.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: To successfully grasp or capture something that is moving through the air toward you.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: I tried to catch the butterfly with my net.

vocabWords.definition_2_4: The dirt and grass we walk on.
vocabWords.definition_4_7: The surface of the earth that we stand and walk on.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: The solid surface of the earth, typically covered with soil, grass, or pavement.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The leaves fell to the ground from the tree.

pages.label: Page 3
pages.text: Benny tries again. He holds out his hands. The ball rolls away. "Oops!" says Benny.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Benny reaching for the red ball but it's rolling past his small hands toward some colorful flowers

vocabWords.definition_2_4: When you work hard to do something.
vocabWords.definition_4_7: Making an effort to do something even if it's hard.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Making an attempt or effort to accomplish something, especially when it requires practice.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: She tries to ride her bike without training wheels.

vocabWords.definition_2_4: To keep something in your hands.
vocabWords.definition_4_7: When you use your hands to keep something in place.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: To grasp or support something firmly with your hands or arms in position.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: Mom holds my hand when we cross the street.

vocabWords.definition_2_4: When something moves by turning over and over.
vocabWords.definition_4_7: When something moves along by turning around and around on the ground.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: The motion of something moving forward by rotating continuously along a surface or path.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The wheel rolls down the hill very fast.

pages.label: Page 4
pages.text: Sister throws the ball. Benny jumps up high! The ball falls down with a soft thud.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Benny jumping in the air with arms up while his big sister watches, the red ball falling to the soft grass below

vocabWords.definition_2_4: When you push up with your feet to go high.
vocabWords.definition_4_7: Using your legs to push yourself up into the air quickly.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: To propel yourself upward or forward through the air by pushing off with your legs.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The frog jumps from lily pad to lily pad.

vocabWords.definition_2_4: When something goes down to the ground.
vocabWords.definition_4_7: When something drops down from a high place to a low place.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: The action of dropping or coming down due to gravity from a higher position.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: Rain falls from the clouds onto the flowers.

vocabWords.definition_2_4: A dull, heavy sound when something hits.
vocabWords.definition_4_7: The deep, muffled sound something makes when it hits the ground.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: A low, heavy sound produced when something solid strikes a surface with force.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The book made a thud when it dropped on the floor.

pages.label: Page 5
pages.text: Benny's hands feel sad. His eyes have little tears. "I can't catch," he whispers.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Close-up of Benny looking down at his small hands with a pouty expression, the red ball sitting nearby on green grass

vocabWords.definition_2_4: Water that comes from your eyes when you're sad.
vocabWords.definition_4_7: The salty water that comes out of your eyes when you cry.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Drops of clear, salty liquid that flow from the eyes when feeling sad or upset.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: Happy tears rolled down her face at the surprise party.

vocabWords.definition_2_4: To talk very quietly and softly.
vocabWords.definition_4_7: When you speak so quietly that only people close can hear you.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: To speak in a very soft, quiet voice that can barely be heard by others.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: She whispers the secret in her friend's ear.

vocabWords.definition_2_4: What your body tells you about things.
vocabWords.definition_4_7: When your body or heart tells you about something touching or happening.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: To experience something through touch or to have an emotion or physical sensation.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: I can feel the warm sunshine on my face.

pages.label: Page 6
pages.text: Mommy sits down. "Let's try something new," she says. She rolls the ball very slowly.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: A gentle mother sitting cross-legged on the grass, slowly rolling the red ball toward Benny who is sitting nearby

vocabWords.definition_2_4: Not fast, taking a long time.
vocabWords.definition_4_7: Moving or doing something at a slow speed, not in a hurry.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: At a reduced speed or pace, taking more time than usual to complete an action.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The turtle walks slowly across the garden path.

vocabWords.definition_2_4: A thing, but we don't know what it is yet.
vocabWords.definition_4_7: A thing or idea that exists but isn't named or described yet.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: An unspecified object, idea, or situation that will be revealed or explained later.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: I have something special to show you after dinner.

vocabWords.definition_2_4: When something moves by turning over and over.
vocabWords.definition_4_7: When something moves along by turning around and around on the ground.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: The motion of something moving forward by rotating continuously along a surface or path.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The marble rolls across the smooth wooden floor.

pages.label: Page 7
pages.text: Benny puts his hands together. He makes a little cup. The ball rolls right in!
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Benny sitting with his small hands cupped together, eyes wide with surprise as the red ball nestles perfectly in his hands

vocabWords.definition_2_4: When things touch each other or are close.
vocabWords.definition_4_7: When two or more things are joined, touching, or very close to each other.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: In union or contact with each other, forming a combined unit or working as one.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: We put all the puzzle pieces together to make a picture.

vocabWords.definition_2_4: To create or build something new.
vocabWords.definition_4_7: When you create, build, or cause something to happen or exist.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: To create, construct, or cause something to come into being through action or effort.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: Dad makes pancakes for breakfast every Sunday morning.

vocabWords.definition_2_4: A round thing that holds water or milk.
vocabWords.definition_4_7: A small, round container that you can drink from or hold things.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: A small, typically round container with an open top, used for drinking or holding small objects.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: She drinks hot chocolate from her favorite cup.

pages.label: Page 8
pages.text: "I caught it! I caught it!" Benny jumps up and down. The ball bounces with him.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Benny jumping joyfully in the air holding the red ball high above his head, with his whole family clapping in the background

vocabWords.definition_2_4: When you grab something with your hands.
vocabWords.definition_4_7: When you successfully grab or capture something that was moving toward you.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Past tense of catch; successfully grasped or captured something that was in motion or trying to escape.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The firefighter caught the cat that fell from the tree.

vocabWords.definition_2_4: When something jumps up and down over and over.
vocabWords.definition_4_7: When something hits the ground and springs back up into the air repeatedly.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: The repeated action of hitting a surface and rebounding back up with elastic energy.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The basketball bounces on the court during practice.

vocabWords.definition_2_4: When you push up with your feet to go high.
vocabWords.definition_4_7: Using your legs to push yourself up into the air quickly and energetically.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: To propel yourself upward or forward through the air by pushing off forcefully with your legs.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: My dog jumps over the fence to chase the squirrel.

pages.label: Page 9
pages.text: Now Benny catches big throws too. His hands are quick and strong. He never gave up!
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Benny confidently catching the red ball with both hands as his family cheers, showing him a little bigger and more skilled

vocabWords.definition_2_4: When someone grabs things that are flying toward them.
vocabWords.definition_4_7: When someone successfully grabs or stops things that are moving through the air.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: The action of successfully grasping or intercepting objects that are thrown or moving toward you.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The baseball player catches fly balls during the game.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Moving or happening very fast, faster than normal or expected.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Characterized by high speed or rapid movement, acting or responding without delay or hesitation.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The quick rabbit ran away from the farmer's dog.

vocabWords.definition_2_4: Having lots of power and muscles.
vocabWords.definition_4_7: Having great physical power and the ability to lift or move heavy things.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Possessing great physical power, muscular force, or the ability to resist pressure and strain.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The strong elephant can lift heavy logs with its trunk.

pages.label: Page 10
pages.text: Benny throws the ball to Daddy. "Good catch!" says Benny with a big smile. They play until sunset.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Benny throwing the red ball to his father in golden sunset light, both of them smiling widely as they play catch together

vocabWords.definition_2_4: Sends something through the air to someone else.
vocabWords.definition_4_7: When someone uses their arm to send something flying toward another person.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: The action of propelling an object through the air using force and aim toward a target.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The quarterback throws the football to the receiver downfield.

vocabWords.definition_2_4: When your mouth goes up because you're happy.
vocabWords.definition_4_7: The happy look on your face when the corners of your mouth turn up.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: A facial expression showing happiness, pleasure, or friendliness by turning up the corners of the mouth.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: Her smile brightened up the whole room during the party.

vocabWords.definition_2_4: When the sun goes down and the sky gets dark.
vocabWords.definition_4_7: The time when the sun goes down and day changes into night.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: The time of evening when the sun disappears below the horizon and daylight fades into darkness.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: We watched the beautiful sunset over the ocean waves.

sp6.title: The Dance Contest
sp6.obstacle: she freezes up on stage during the dance competition

pages.label: Page 1
pages.text: Luna twirled across her bedroom floor, her purple tutu spinning like a flower in the wind. The golden sunlight streamed through her window as she practiced her favorite dance moves. Tomorrow was the big dance contest at school, and Luna had been preparing for weeks.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: A young girl with flowing hair dancing in her sunny bedroom, wearing a purple tutu, with dance posters on the walls and sunlight streaming through the window

vocabWords.definition_4_7: To spin around and around very fast in circles.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: To spin gracefully in circles, like a leaf dancing on the wind or a top spinning smoothly.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The ice skater twirled beautifully on the frozen pond.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: A short fluffy skirt that ballet dancers wear when performing.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: A special short, puffy skirt made of many layers of soft fabric that ballet dancers wear during performances.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The ballerina's pink tutu sparkled under the theater lights.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Light that flows or pours in like a river of brightness.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Light that flows continuously and smoothly through an opening, like water flowing gently down a stream.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: Morning light streamed through the kitchen window during breakfast.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Getting ready for something by practicing or planning ahead of time.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Working hard to get ready for something important by practicing and planning everything carefully in advance.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: Mom was preparing dinner by chopping vegetables and setting the table.

pages.label: Page 2
pages.text: "You're going to be wonderful, sweetheart," said Mama, clapping her hands to the rhythm. Luna's little brother Miguel cheered from the doorway, making Luna giggle. She felt confident and ready, her heart beating with excitement rather than worry.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Luna dancing while her mother sits nearby clapping, and her little brother peeks through the doorway with a big smile

vocabWords.definition_4_7: The beat in music that makes you want to clap along.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: A steady pattern of beats in music that repeats over and over, like your heartbeat or footsteps when walking.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The drummer kept a steady rhythm during the whole song.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Feeling sure that you can do something really well.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Feeling strong and sure about yourself, believing that you have the skills to succeed at something challenging.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: Jake felt confident about riding his bike without training wheels.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: A happy, bouncy feeling when something fun is about to happen.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: A wonderful, energetic feeling that fills your whole body when you're looking forward to something special or thrilling.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The children could barely contain their excitement on Christmas morning.

pages.label: Page 3
pages.text: The next morning, Luna arrived at school wearing her sparkly dance costume. The gymnasium buzzed with activity as dancers stretched and practiced. Colorful ribbons and balloons decorated the stage, and the smell of fresh popcorn filled the air.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: A school gymnasium decorated with colorful balloons and ribbons, with young dancers in various costumes warming up, and Luna in her sparkly outfit looking around

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Something that shines with lots of tiny bright flashes of light.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Covered with many small, glittering pieces that catch the light and create beautiful flashes that twinkle like stars.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: Her sparkly earrings caught everyone's attention at the party.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: A big indoor room where people play sports and exercise.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: A large indoor space with high ceilings where people gather to play sports, exercise, or hold special events and performances.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The basketball team practiced every day in the school gymnasium.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: A place that was full of busy, excited activity and noise.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Filled with energetic movement and excited sounds, like bees flying busily around their hive gathering nectar.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The playground buzzed with children's laughter during recess.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Made to look pretty by adding colorful things like ribbons or flowers.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Made beautiful and festive by carefully adding special ornaments, colors, and decorations to create a celebration atmosphere.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: We decorated the birthday cake with rainbow sprinkles and candles.

pages.label: Page 4
pages.text: Luna watched the other dancers perform their routines. There was Sam with his robot moves, and Emma with her graceful ballet. Each dancer brought something special to the stage, and the audience clapped enthusiastically for everyone.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Luna sitting in the audience area watching other children perform on stage - one doing robot dance moves, another doing ballet, with an engaged audience clapping

vocabWords.definition_4_7: A set of dance moves that are practiced and performed together.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: A carefully planned sequence of dance steps and movements that dancers practice until they can perform them perfectly together.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The cheerleaders learned new routines for the football game.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Moving in a smooth, beautiful way that looks easy and elegant.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Moving with smooth, flowing motions that look effortless and beautiful, like a swan gliding across a peaceful lake.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The cat made a graceful leap from the chair to the windowsill.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Clapping and cheering with lots of energy and excitement because you really enjoyed something.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Showing appreciation with great energy and genuine excitement, clapping loudly and cheering because something made you truly happy and impressed.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The crowd cheered enthusiastically when their team scored the winning goal.

pages.label: Page 5
pages.text: "Luna Martinez, you're next!" called Ms. Rodriguez, the dance teacher. Luna's stomach suddenly felt full of butterflies. She walked toward the bright stage lights, her sparkly costume catching the colorful beams. The gymnasium seemed much bigger than before.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Luna walking toward a brightly lit stage with colorful spotlights, her costume sparkling, while Ms. Rodriguez calls her name from the side

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Luna's last name, which tells people what family she belongs to.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: A family name passed down through generations that connects Luna to her relatives and cultural heritage from Spanish-speaking countries.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: Mrs. Martinez teaches second grade at the elementary school.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: The part inside your belly where food goes when you eat.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: The organ in your body where food is digested, and where you feel fluttery sensations when you're nervous or excited.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: My stomach growled loudly because I was hungry for lunch.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: A fluttery, nervous feeling you get in your belly before something important.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: The fluttering, tickling sensation in your stomach when you feel nervous or excited, like tiny wings beating gently inside you.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: I had butterflies before my piano recital, but they went away once I started playing.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Straight lines of bright light that shine from one place to another.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Rays of light that travel in straight lines from a source, creating bright paths that can illuminate and highlight different objects.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: Sunbeams shone through the forest trees, creating pretty patterns on the ground.

pages.label: Page 6
pages.text: As Luna stepped onto the stage, the bright lights made everything else disappear into darkness. She could barely see her family in the audience. Her heart pounded so loudly she was sure everyone could hear it. Her feet felt frozen to the floor.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Luna standing alone on a bright stage with spotlights, looking small and uncertain, with the audience area dimmed and shadowy around her

vocabWords.definition_4_7: To vanish or become impossible to see, like something invisible.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: To fade away completely from sight, becoming invisible as if by magic, so that no one can see it anymore.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The magician made the rabbit disappear from the hat with a wave of his wand.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Beat very hard and fast, like when your heart goes thump-thump-thump.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Beat with strong, heavy rhythms that you can feel and almost hear, like a drum being hit over and over again.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: Rain pounded on the roof during the thunderstorm last night.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Unable to move at all, like your body turned into ice.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Completely unable to move or act, as if your muscles have turned solid and won't respond to what your mind wants them to do.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The deer stood frozen when it saw the car's headlights approaching.

pages.label: Page 7
pages.text: The music started, but Luna couldn't move. Her mind went blank, and she forgot all her beautiful dance moves. The sparkly stage that had looked so magical now felt scary and enormous. She stood there like a statue, wishing she could disappear.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Luna standing very still on stage like a frozen statue, with her arms at her sides, while colorful stage lights shine down on her

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Empty, like when you can't remember anything at all.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Completely empty of thoughts or memories, like a clean sheet of paper with nothing written on it.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: When the teacher asked the question, my mind went completely blank.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: A person or animal made of stone that can't move.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: A carved figure of a person or animal, usually made from stone or metal, that stands perfectly still and never moves.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: There's a statue of a famous president in the town square.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Much, much bigger than normal, like a giant-sized thing.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Incredibly huge and much larger than usual, so big that it can feel overwhelming or scary because of its massive size.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The enormous whale swam gracefully through the deep blue ocean.

pages.label: Page 8
pages.text: Then Luna heard a familiar voice from the audience. "Go Luna! You can do it!" It was Miguel, her little brother, cheering just like he did at home. Mama's warm smile seemed to reach across the dark gymnasium like a gentle hug.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Luna on stage looking out toward the audience where she can see her little brother Miguel standing and cheering, and her mother's warm, encouraging smile

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Something you know well because you've heard or seen it before.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Something that feels comfortable and safe because you recognize it from past experiences, like a favorite song or voice.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The familiar smell of cookies reminded me of my grandmother's kitchen.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Shouting happy words to help someone feel brave and encouraged.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Calling out supportive words with enthusiasm and love to help someone feel confident and know that others believe in them.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The fans were cheering loudly for their favorite soccer team.

pages.label: Page 9
pages.text: Luna took a deep breath and closed her eyes. She remembered dancing in her sunny bedroom, feeling free and joyful. "I can do this," she whispered to herself. "I love to dance, and that's what matters most."
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Luna on stage with her eyes closed, taking a deep breath, with a peaceful expression on her face as she remembers her confidence

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Talked very, very quietly using just a soft breath of air.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Spoke in the softest possible voice, so quietly that only you or someone very close could hear the words.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: She whispered the secret so no one else would hear it.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Feeling very happy and full of good, warm feelings inside.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Filled with pure happiness that bubbles up from deep inside, making everything feel bright and wonderful like sunshine in your heart.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The children felt joyful as they played together in the warm spring sunshine.

pages.label: Page 10
pages.text: Luna opened her eyes and began to move. Her body remembered every step, every spin, every graceful gesture. She danced like she was back in her bedroom, filled with pure joy and the music in her heart. The audience clapped along, celebrating her courage.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Luna dancing beautifully on stage with her arms outstretched and tutu spinning, looking confident and joyful, with the audience clapping enthusiastically in the background

vocabWords.definition_4_7: A movement you make with your hands or body to show something.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: A purposeful movement of your hands, arms, or body that expresses a feeling or idea without using any words.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: He made a friendly gesture by waving hello to his new neighbor.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Being brave enough to do something even when you feel scared.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: The inner strength that helps you face your fears and do what's right, even when something feels difficult or frightening.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: It took courage for the little boy to pet the big, friendly dog.

pages.label: Page 11
pages.text: When the music ended, Luna took a bow with a huge smile on her face. She had remembered the most important thing of all: believing in herself. The warm applause filled the gymnasium, but the proudest feeling was glowing right inside her heart.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Luna taking a graceful bow on stage with a radiant smile, sparkly costume glittering under the lights, while the audience gives her a standing ovation

vocabWords.definition_4_7: The sound of many people clapping their hands together at once.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: The wonderful sound created when a group of people clap their hands together to show they enjoyed a performance or achievement.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The singer smiled as loud applause filled the concert hall after her beautiful song.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Thinking that something is true or that you can really do it.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Having faith and confidence in yourself or something else, trusting that it's real and possible even when it feels challenging.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: Believing in yourself is the first step to achieving your dreams.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Shining with a soft, warm light from the inside out.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Radiating warmth and light like a gentle flame, creating a beautiful brightness that comes from within and spreads outward.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: Her face was glowing with happiness after receiving the surprise gift.

comprehensionQuestions.question: What happened to Luna when she first stepped onto the stage?

options.text: She forgot her dance moves and couldn't move
She started dancing right away
She ran off the stage

comprehensionQuestions.question: Why do you think Luna was able to start dancing after hearing Miguel's voice?

options.text: His cheering reminded her that people who love her believe in her
She remembered she was wearing her sparkly costume
The music got louder and easier to hear

comprehensionQuestions.question: Can you think of a time when you felt nervous about doing something in front of other people?

options.text: Yes, I felt butterflies in my stomach too
I remember when someone cheered me on and it helped
I've felt proud of myself for being brave

predictionPause.question: Oh no! Luna is frozen on stage and can't remember her dance moves. What do you think might help her start dancing?

options.text: Someone she loves will encourage her from the audience
She'll remember how much fun dancing is and start moving
The music will change to her favorite song

sp7.title: Mountain Bike Trail
sp7.obstacle: the trail is harder than expected and his friends want to turn back

pages.label: Page 1
pages.text: Tyler pumped air into his bright red mountain bike tires, feeling the morning sunshine warm his shoulders. His best friends Maya, Sam, and Zoe were getting their bikes ready too, their colorful helmets gleaming in the golden light. "Today's the day we tackle Eagle Ridge Trail!" Tyler announced, his heart beating fast with excitement. The mountain trail stretched up through tall pine trees, looking like an adventure waiting to happen.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Four kids with colorful mountain bikes and helmets preparing in a sunny parking area at the base of a forested mountain trail, with pine trees in the background

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Used a tool to push air into something.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Forced air into something by pushing and pulling a handle or lever repeatedly.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: Dad pumped gas into the car at the station.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Shining very brightly in the light.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Reflecting light in a way that makes something look clean, bright, and almost sparkling.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The knight's gleaming armor caught everyone's attention at the parade.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: To try to do something difficult or challenging.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: To take on a tough job or challenge with determination and effort.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: Maria decided to tackle her messy bedroom on Saturday morning.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Went on for a long distance or time.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Extended far into the distance, reaching much further than you could easily see.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The desert stretched for miles without a single tree.

pages.label: Page 2
pages.text: The friends started pedaling up the winding dirt path, their bike tires crunching over fallen leaves and small pebbles. Cool mountain air filled their lungs as they rode past wildflowers that painted the trail edges purple and yellow. Tyler led the way, pointing out a red squirrel that scampered up an oak tree. "This is going to be the best ride ever!" he called back to his friends, who were laughing and enjoying the gentle uphill climb.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Four kids on colorful mountain bikes riding single file up a scenic dirt trail through a forest, with wildflowers on the sides and a red squirrel visible in a tree

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Curving and turning like a snake moves.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Following a twisting, curving path that turns left and right many times.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The winding river curved through the forest like a ribbon.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Making loud crushing sounds when stepping or rolling.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Creating crisp, breaking sounds as something hard presses down on small objects.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The children were crunching through autumn leaves in the yard.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Ran quickly with short, light steps.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Moved rapidly with small, quick steps, like a playful or frightened small animal.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The puppy scampered across the kitchen floor chasing a ball.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Beautiful flowers that grow naturally without people planting them.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Colorful flowers that grow wild in nature without anyone taking care of them.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: We picked wildflowers in the meadow to make a bouquet for Grandma.

pages.label: Page 3
pages.text: As they climbed higher, the trail became much steeper and rockier than expected. Tyler's legs started to burn as he pedaled harder, and he could hear his friends breathing heavily behind him. Sharp gray rocks poked up from the path, and the dirt had turned to loose gravel that made their wheels slip. "Wow, this is getting pretty tough," Maya panted, wiping sweat from her forehead as she carefully steered around a large boulder.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: The kids struggling to pedal up a much steeper, rockier section of trail with loose gravel and large boulders, looking tired but determined

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Going up at a sharper, more difficult angle.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Slanting upward so sharply that it becomes much harder to climb or walk on.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The stairs to the attic are steeper than the ones to the basement.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Having more rocks and stones than before.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Covered with more rocks and rough stones that make traveling harder and bumpier.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The beach became rockier as we walked away from the sandy area.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Very small rocks and stones mixed together.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Tiny pieces of rock and stone that are loose and can roll around under your feet.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The driveway was covered with gravel that made a crunching sound.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: A very big rock that's hard to move.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: An enormous rock that's so heavy and large it would take many people to move it.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The hikers sat on the boulder to rest and eat their lunch.

pages.label: Page 4
pages.text: The trail twisted around a bend and became even more challenging, with fallen logs blocking their path and deep ruts carved by mountain rain. Sam had to get off his bike and walk it over a particularly tricky spot where roots tangled across the trail. "Maybe we should turn back," Zoe suggested, her voice shaky as she looked at the steep climb ahead. Tyler felt his stomach sink – he really wanted to reach the top, but his friends looked worried and tired.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: The kids facing a very challenging section of trail with fallen logs, exposed roots, and deep ruts, with Sam walking his bike over obstacles

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Turned and curved in a winding way.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Curved and turned sharply in different directions, making it hard to see ahead.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The old tree's branches twisted up toward the sky in strange shapes.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Very difficult and requiring lots of effort.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: So hard that it tests your skills and makes you work really hard to succeed.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The math test was challenging, but Emma studied hard and did well.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Deep grooves or tracks worn into the ground.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Long, deep tracks carved into the dirt by wheels or water flowing over time.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The farmer's tractor left deep ruts in the muddy field.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Twisted together in a messy, knotted way.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Twisted and wound together so messily that it's hard to separate or get through.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: Her hair got tangled after playing outside in the windy weather.

pages.label: Page 5
pages.text: Tyler stopped his bike and looked back at his three friends, who had all gathered together behind him with concerned expressions. "I know this is harder than we thought it would be," he said, feeling disappointed but understanding their worry. Sam nodded and pointed up the mountain, where the trail disappeared around jagged rocks that looked almost impossible to climb. "What if we can't make it back down if we go any further?" he asked, his voice filled with genuine concern.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Tyler turned around on his bike looking back at his three friends who are clustered together looking worried, with the steep rocky trail continuing up behind Tyler

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Came together in one place as a group.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Collected together in the same spot, usually to talk about something important.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: The family gathered around the dinner table to share stories about their day.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Worried about something that might happen or go wrong.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Feeling worried or troubled about a situation that might become dangerous or difficult.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: Mom looked concerned when she saw the dark storm clouds approaching.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Having sharp, uneven edges that stick out.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Rough and sharp with pointed edges that stick out in different directions dangerously.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: Be careful of the jagged glass pieces from the broken window.

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Real and honest, not fake or pretend.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Truly felt and completely honest, coming from real feelings rather than just pretending.
vocabWords.exampleSentence: Her genuine smile showed she was really happy to see her friend.

pages.label: Page 6
pages.text: Tyler thought for a moment, then had an idea that made his eyes light up. "What if we work together to get through the tough parts?" he suggested with growing excitement. "We could take turns leading, and help each other over the really tricky spots!" Maya's face brightened as she considered this plan. "We could carry each other's water bottles when someone needs both hands for climbing," she added, and suddenly the challenge ahead seemed much more manageable.
pages.scene: illustration prompt: Tyler with an excited expression talking to his friends, who are now looking more hopeful and interested, gathered in a circle with their bikes

vocabWords.definition_4_7: Gave an idea about what they could do.
vocabWords.definition_7_10: Offered a helpful idea or plan for others to think about and possibly try.
